chapters.zh: 不从恶人的计谋，不站罪人的道路，不坐亵慢人的座位，
chapters.en: Blessed the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.

chapters.zh: 惟喜爱耶和华的律法，昼夜思想，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: But his delight in the law of the LORD; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.

chapters.zh: 他要像一棵树栽在溪水旁，按时候结果子，叶子也不枯干。凡他所做的尽都顺利。
chapters.en: And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.

chapters.zh: 恶人并不是这样，乃像糠秕被风吹散。
chapters.en: The ungodly not so: but like the chaff which the wind driveth away.

chapters.zh: 因此，当审判的时候恶人必站立不住；罪人在义人的会中也是如此。
chapters.en: Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华知道义人的道路；恶人的道路却必灭亡。
chapters.en: For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.

chapters.zh: 外邦为甚么争闹？万民为甚么谋算虚妄的事？
chapters.en: Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?

chapters.zh: 世上的君王一齐起来，臣宰一同商议，要敌挡耶和华并他的受膏者，
chapters.en: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed, ,

chapters.zh: 说：我们要挣开他们的捆绑，脱去他们的绳索。
chapters.en: Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.

chapters.zh: 那坐在天上的必发笑；主必嗤笑他们。
chapters.en: He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.

chapters.zh: 那时，他要在怒中责备他们，在烈怒中惊吓他们，
chapters.en: Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.

chapters.zh: 说：我已经立我的君在锡安 ─ 我的圣山上了。
chapters.en: Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.

chapters.zh: 受膏者说：我要传圣旨。耶和华曾对我说：你是我的儿子，我今日生你。
chapters.en: I will declare the decree: the LORD hath said unto me, Thou my Son; this day have I begotten thee.

chapters.zh: 你求我，我就将列国赐你为基业，将地极赐你为田产。
chapters.en: Ask of me, and I shall give the heathen thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth thy possession.

chapters.zh: 你必用铁杖打破他们；你必将他们如同窑匠的瓦器摔碎。
chapters.en: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel.

chapters.zh: 现在，你们君王应当省悟！你们世上的审判官该受管教！
chapters.en: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.

chapters.zh: 当存畏惧事奉耶和华，又当存战兢而快乐。
chapters.en: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

chapters.zh: 当以嘴亲子，恐怕他发怒，你们便在道中灭亡，因为他的怒气快要发作。凡投靠他的，都是有福的。
chapters.en: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed all they that put their trust in him.

chapters.zh: （大卫逃避他儿子押沙龙的时候作的诗。）耶和华啊，我的敌人何其加增；有许多人起来攻击我。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David, when he fled from Absalom his son.] LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many they that rise up against me.

chapters.zh: 有许多人议论我说：他得不著神的帮助。（细拉）
chapters.en: Many which say of my soul, no help for him in God. Selah.

chapters.zh: 但你 ─ 耶和华是我四围的盾牌，是我的荣耀，又是叫我抬起头来的。
chapters.en: But thou, O LORD, a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.

chapters.zh: 我用我的声音求告耶和华，他就从他的圣山上应允我。（细拉）
chapters.en: I cried unto the LORD with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我躺下睡觉，我醒著，耶和华都保佑我。
chapters.en: I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.

chapters.zh: 虽有成万的百姓来周围攻击我，我也不怕。
chapters.en: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set against me round about.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你起来！我的神啊，求你救我！因为你打了我一切仇敌的腮骨，敲碎了恶人的牙齿。
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.

chapters.zh: 救恩属乎耶和华；愿你赐福给你的百姓。（细拉）
chapters.en: Salvation unto the LORD: thy blessing upon thy people. Selah.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）显我为义的神啊，我呼吁的时候，求你应允我！我在困苦中，你曾使我宽广；现在求你怜恤我，听我的祷告！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm of David.] Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.

chapters.zh: 你们这上流人哪，你们将我的尊荣变为羞辱要到几时呢？你们喜爱虚妄，寻找虚假，要到几时呢？（细拉）
chapters.en: O ye sons of men, how long my glory into shame? will ye love vanity, seek after leasing? Selah.

chapters.zh: 你们要知道，耶和华已经分别虔诚人归他自己；我求告耶和华，他必听我。
chapters.en: But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.

chapters.zh: 你们应当畏惧，不可犯罪；在床上的时候，要心里思想，并要肃静。（细拉）
chapters.en: Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.

chapters.zh: 当献上公义的祭，又当倚靠耶和华。
chapters.en: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.

chapters.zh: 有许多人说：谁能指示我们甚么好处？耶和华啊，求你仰起脸来，光照我们。
chapters.en: many that say, Who will shew us good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.

chapters.zh: 你使我心里快乐，胜过那丰收五谷新酒的人。
chapters.en: Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time their corn and their wine increased.

chapters.zh: 我必安然躺下睡觉，因为独有你 ─ 耶和华使我安然居住。
chapters.en: I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。用吹的乐器。）耶和华啊，求你留心听我的言语，顾念我的心思！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Nehiloth, A Psalm of David.] Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.

chapters.zh: 我的王我的神啊，求你垂听我呼求的声音！因为我向你祈祷。
chapters.en: Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，早晨你必听我的声音；早晨我必向你陈明我的心意，并要警醒！
chapters.en: My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct unto thee, and will look up.

chapters.zh: 因为你不是喜悦恶事的神，恶人不能与你同居。
chapters.en: For thou not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.

chapters.zh: 狂傲人不能站在你眼前；凡作孽的，都是你所恨恶的。
chapters.en: The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.

chapters.zh: 说谎言的，你必灭绝；好流人血弄诡诈的，都为耶和华所憎恶。
chapters.en: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我必凭你丰盛的慈爱进入你的居所；我必存敬畏你的心向你的圣殿下拜。
chapters.en: But as for me, I will come thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你因我的仇敌，凭你的公义引领我，使你的道路在我面前正直。
chapters.en: Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.

chapters.zh: 因为，他们的口中没有诚实；他们的心里满有邪恶；他们的喉咙是敞开的坟墓；他们用舌头谄媚人。
chapters.en: For no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part very wickedness; their throat an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你定他们的罪！愿他们因自己的计谋跌倒；愿你在他们许多的过犯中把他们逐出，因为他们背叛了你。
chapters.en: Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.

chapters.zh: 凡投靠你的，愿他们喜乐，时常欢呼，因为你护庇他们；又愿那爱你名的人都靠你欢欣。
chapters.en: But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.

chapters.zh: 因为你必赐福与义人；耶和华啊，你必用恩惠如同盾牌四面护卫他。
chapters.en: For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as a shield.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器，调用第八。）耶和华啊，求你不要在怒中责备我，也不要在烈怒中惩罚我！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth upon Sheminith, A Psalm of David.] O LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你可怜我，因为我软弱。耶和华啊，求你医治我，因为我的骨头发战。
chapters.en: Have mercy upon me, O LORD; for I weak: O LORD, heal me; for my bones are vexed.

chapters.zh: 我心也大大地惊惶。耶和华啊，你要到几时才救我呢？
chapters.en: My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O LORD, how long?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你转回搭救我！因你的慈爱拯救我。
chapters.en: Return, O LORD, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies' sake.

chapters.zh: 因为，在死地无人记念你，在阴间有谁称谢你？
chapters.en: For in death no remembrance of thee: in the grave who shall give thee thanks?

chapters.zh: 我因唉哼而困乏；我每夜流泪，把床榻漂起，把褥子湿透。
chapters.en: I am weary with my groaning; all the night make I my bed to swim; I water my couch with my tears.

chapters.zh: 我因忧愁眼睛干瘪，又因我一切的敌人眼睛昏花。
chapters.en: Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 你们一切作孽的人，离开我罢！因为耶和华听了我哀哭的声音。
chapters.en: Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping.

chapters.zh: 耶和华听了我的恳求；耶和华必收纳我的祷告。
chapters.en: The LORD hath heard my supplication; the LORD will receive my prayer.

chapters.zh: 我的一切仇敌都必羞愧，大大惊惶；他们必要退后，忽然羞愧。
chapters.en: Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return be ashamed suddenly.

chapters.zh: （大卫指著便雅悯人古实的话，向耶和华唱的流离歌。）耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，我投靠你！求你救我脱离一切追赶我的人，将我救拔出来！
chapters.en: [Shiggaion of David, which he sang unto the LORD, concerning the words of Cush the Benjamite.] O LORD my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:

chapters.zh: 恐怕他们像狮子撕裂我，甚至撕碎，无人搭救。
chapters.en: Lest he tear my soul like a lion, rending in pieces, while none to deliver.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，我若行了这事，若有罪孽在我手里，
chapters.en: O LORD my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;

chapters.zh: 我若以恶报那与我交好的人 ─ 连那无故与我为敌的，我也救了他，
chapters.en: If I have rewarded evil unto him that was at peace with me; (yea, I have delivered him that without cause is mine enemy:)

chapters.zh: 就任凭仇敌追赶我，直到追上，将我的性命踏在地下，使我的荣耀归于灰尘。（细拉）
chapters.en: Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take ; yea, let him tread down my life upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你在怒中起来，挺身而立，抵挡我敌人的暴怒！求你为我兴起！你已经命定施行审判！
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me the judgment thou hast commanded.

chapters.zh: 愿众民的会环绕你！愿你从其上归于高位！
chapters.en: So shall the congregation of the people compass thee about: for their sakes therefore return thou on high.

chapters.zh: 耶和华向众民施行审判；耶和华啊，求你按我的公义和我心中的纯正判断我。
chapters.en: The LORD shall judge the people: judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity in me.

chapters.zh: 愿恶人的恶断绝！愿你坚立义人！因为公义的神察验人的心肠肺腑。
chapters.en: Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.

chapters.zh: 神是我的盾牌；他拯救心里正直的人。
chapters.en: My defence of God, which saveth the upright in heart.

chapters.zh: 神是公义的审判者，又是天天向恶人发怒的神。
chapters.en: God judgeth the righteous, and God is angry every day.

chapters.zh: 若有人不回头，他的刀必磨快，弓必上弦，预备妥当了。
chapters.en: If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.

chapters.zh: 他也预备了杀人的器械；他所射的是火箭。
chapters.en: He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.

chapters.zh: 试看恶人因奸恶而劬劳，所怀的是毒害，所生的是虚假。
chapters.en: Behold, he travaileth with iniquity, and hath conceived mischief, and brought forth falsehood.

chapters.zh: 他掘了坑，又挖深了，竟掉在自己所挖的阱里。
chapters.en: He made a pit, and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch he made.

chapters.zh: 他的毒害必临到他自己的头上；他的强暴必落到他自己的脑袋上。
chapters.en: His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violent dealing shall come down upon his own pate.

chapters.zh: 我要照著耶和华的公义称谢他，歌颂耶和华至高者的名。
chapters.en: I will praise the LORD according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the LORD most high.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。用迦特乐器。）耶和华 ─ 我们的主啊，你的名在全地何其美！你将你的荣耀彰显于天。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm of David.] O LORD our Lord, how excellent thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.

chapters.zh: 你因敌人的缘故，从婴孩和吃奶的口中，建立了能力，使仇敌和报仇的闭口无言。
chapters.en: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.

chapters.zh: 我观看你指头所造的天，并你所陈设的月亮星宿，
chapters.en: When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained;

chapters.zh: 便说：人算甚么，你竟顾念他？世人算甚么，你竟眷顾他？
chapters.en: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?

chapters.zh: 你叫他比天使（或译：神）微小一点，并赐他荣耀尊贵为冠冕。
chapters.en: For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour.

chapters.zh: 你派他管理你手所造的，使万物，就是一切的牛羊、田野的兽、空中的鸟、海里的鱼，凡经行海道的，都服在他的脚下。
chapters.en: Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all under his feet:

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: The fowl of the air, and the fish of the sea, passeth through the paths of the seas.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我们的主啊，你的名在全地何其美！
chapters.en: O LORD our Lord, how excellent thy name in all the earth!

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。调用慕拉便。）我要一心称谢耶和华；我要传扬你一切奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Muthlabben, A Psalm of David.] I will praise , O LORD, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.

chapters.zh: 我要因你欢喜快乐；至高者啊，我要歌颂你的名！
chapters.en: I will be glad and rejoice in thee: I will sing praise to thy name, O thou most High.

chapters.zh: 我的仇敌转身退去的时候，他们一见你的面就跌倒灭亡。
chapters.en: When mine enemies are turned back, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.

chapters.zh: 因你已经为我伸冤，为我辨屈；你坐在宝座上，按公义审判。
chapters.en: For thou hast maintained my right and my cause; thou satest in the throne judging right.

chapters.zh: 你曾斥责外邦，你曾灭绝恶人；你曾涂抹他们的名，直到永永远远。
chapters.en: Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked, thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 仇敌到了尽头；他们被毁坏，直到永远。你拆毁他们的城邑，连他们的名号都归于无有。
chapters.en: O thou enemy, destructions are come to a perpetual end: and thou hast destroyed cities; their memorial is perished with them.

chapters.zh: 惟耶和华坐著为王，直到永远；他已经为审判设摆他的宝座。
chapters.en: But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.

chapters.zh: 他要按公义审判世界，按正直判断万民。
chapters.en: And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又要给受欺压的人作高台，在患难的时候作高台。
chapters.en: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，认识你名的人要倚靠你，因你没有离弃寻求你的人。
chapters.en: And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, LORD, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.

chapters.zh: 应当歌颂居锡安的耶和华，将他所行的传扬在众民中。
chapters.en: Sing praises to the LORD, which dwelleth in Zion: declare among the people his doings.

chapters.zh: 因为那追讨流人血之罪的 ─ 他记念受屈的人，不忘记困苦人的哀求。
chapters.en: When he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them: he forgetteth not the cry of the humble.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你是从死门把我提拔起来的；求你怜恤我，看那恨我的人所加给我的苦难，
chapters.en: Have mercy upon me, O LORD; consider my trouble of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:

chapters.zh: 好叫我述说你一切的美德；我必在锡安城（原文是女子）的门因你的救恩欢乐。
chapters.en: That I may shew forth all thy praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion: I will rejoice in thy salvation.

chapters.zh: 外邦人陷在自己所掘的坑中；他们的脚在自己暗设的网罗里缠住了。
chapters.en: The heathen are sunk down in the pit they made: in the net which they hid is their own foot taken.

chapters.zh: 耶和华已将自己显明了，他已施行审判；恶人被自己手所做的缠住了（或译：他叫恶人被自己手所做的累住了）。（细拉）
chapters.en: The LORD is known the judgment he executeth: the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.

chapters.zh: 恶人，就是忘记神的外邦人，都必归到阴间。
chapters.en: The wicked shall be turned into hell, all the nations that forget God.

chapters.zh: 穷乏人必不永久被忘；困苦人的指望必不永远落空。
chapters.en: For the needy shall not alway be forgotten: the expectation of the poor shall perish for ever.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你起来，不容人得胜！愿外邦人在你面前受审判！
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD; let not man prevail: let the heathen be judged in thy sight.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你使外邦人恐惧；愿他们知道自己不过是人。（细拉）
chapters.en: Put them in fear, O LORD: the nations may know themselves men. Selah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你为甚么站在远处？在患难的时候为甚么隐藏？
chapters.en: Why standest thou afar off, O LORD? hidest thou in times of trouble?

chapters.zh: 恶人在骄横中把困苦人追得火急；愿他们陷在自己所设的计谋里。
chapters.en: The wicked in pride doth persecute the poor: let them be taken in the devices that they have imagined.

chapters.zh: 因为恶人以心愿自夸；贪财的背弃耶和华，并且轻慢他（或译：他祝福贪财的，却轻慢耶和华）。
chapters.en: For the wicked boasteth of his heart's desire, and blesseth the covetous, the LORD abhorreth.

chapters.zh: 恶人面带骄傲，说：耶和华必不追究；他一切所想的都以为没有神。
chapters.en: The wicked, through the pride of his countenance, will not seek : God not in all his thoughts.

chapters.zh: 凡他所做的，时常稳固；你的审判超过他的眼界。至于他一切的敌人，他都向他们喷气。
chapters.en: His ways are always grievous; thy judgments far above out of his sight: all his enemies, he puffeth at them.

chapters.zh: 他心里说：我必不动摇，世世代代不遭灾难。
chapters.en: He hath said in his heart, I shall not be moved: for never in adversity.

chapters.zh: 他满口是咒骂、诡诈、欺压，舌底是毒害、奸恶。
chapters.en: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and fraud: under his tongue mischief and vanity.

chapters.zh: 他在村庄埋伏等候；他在隐密处杀害无辜的人。他的眼睛窥探无倚无靠的人；
chapters.en: He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doth he murder the innocent: his eyes are privily set against the poor.

chapters.zh: 他埋伏在暗地，如狮子蹲在洞中。他埋伏，要掳去困苦人；他拉网，就把困苦人掳去。
chapters.en: He lieth in wait secretly as a lion in his den: he lieth in wait to catch the poor: he doth catch the poor, when he draweth him into his net.

chapters.zh: 他屈身蹲伏，无倚无靠的人就倒在他爪牙（爪牙：或译强暴人）之下。
chapters.en: He croucheth, humbleth himself, that the poor may fall by his strong ones.

chapters.zh: 他心里说：神竟忘记了；他掩面永不观看。
chapters.en: He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see .

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你起来！神啊，求你举手，不要忘记困苦人！
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up thine hand: forget not the humble.

chapters.zh: 恶人为何轻慢神，心里说：你必不追究？
chapters.en: Wherefore doth the wicked contemn God? he hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require .

chapters.zh: 其实你已经观看；因为奸恶毒害，你都看见了，为要以手施行报应。无倚无靠的人把自己交托你；你向来是帮助孤儿的。
chapters.en: Thou hast seen ; for thou beholdest mischief and spite, to requite with thy hand: the poor committeth himself unto thee; thou art the helper of the fatherless.

chapters.zh: 愿你打断恶人的膀臂；至于坏人，愿你追究他的恶，直到净尽。
chapters.en: Break thou the arm of the wicked and the evil : seek out his wickedness thou find none.

chapters.zh: 耶和华永永远远为王；外邦人从他的地已经灭绝了。
chapters.en: The LORD King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，谦卑人的心愿，你早已知道（原文是听见）。你必预备他们的心，也必侧耳听他们的祈求，
chapters.en: LORD, thou hast heard the desire of the humble: thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine ear to hear:

chapters.zh: 为要给孤儿和受欺压的人伸冤，使强横的人不再威吓他们。
chapters.en: To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may no more oppress.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）我是投靠耶和华；你们怎么对我说：你当像鸟飞往你的山去。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, of David.] In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee a bird to your mountain?

chapters.zh: 看哪，恶人弯弓，把箭搭在弦上，要在暗中射那心里正直的人。
chapters.en: For, lo, the wicked bend bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart.

chapters.zh: 根基若毁坏，义人还能做甚么呢？
chapters.en: If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?

chapters.zh: 耶和华在他的圣殿里；耶和华的宝座在天上；他的慧眼察看世人。
chapters.en: The LORD in his holy temple, the LORD'S throne in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.

chapters.zh: 耶和华试验义人；惟有恶人和喜爱强暴的人，他心里恨恶。
chapters.en: The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.

chapters.zh: 他要向恶人密布网罗；有烈火、硫磺、热风，作他们杯中的分。
chapters.en: Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: the portion of their cup.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华是公义的，他喜爱公义；正直人必得见他的面。
chapters.en: For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。调用第八。）耶和华啊，求你帮助，因虔诚人断绝了；世人中间的忠信人没有了。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Sheminith, A Psalm of David.] Help, LORD; for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithful fail from among the children of men.

chapters.zh: 人人向邻舍说谎；他们说话，是嘴唇油滑，心口不一。
chapters.en: They speak vanity every one with his neighbour: flattering lips with a double heart do they speak.

chapters.zh: 凡油滑的嘴唇和夸大的舌头，耶和华必要剪除。
chapters.en: The LORD shall cut off all flattering lips, the tongue that speaketh proud things:

chapters.zh: 他们曾说：我们必能以舌头得胜；我们的嘴唇是我们自己的，谁能作我们的主呢？
chapters.en: Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail; our lips our own: who lord over us?

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：因为困苦人的冤屈和贫穷人的叹息，我现在要起来，把他安置在他所切慕的稳妥之地。
chapters.en: For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith the LORD; I will set in safety puffeth at him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的言语是纯净的言语，如同银子在泥炉中炼过七次。
chapters.en: The words of the LORD pure words: silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你必保护他们；你必保佑他们永远脱离这世代的人。
chapters.en: Thou shalt keep them, O LORD, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.

chapters.zh: 下流人在世人中升高，就有恶人到处游行。
chapters.en: The wicked walk on every side, when the vilest men are exalted.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）耶和华啊，你忘记我要到几时呢？要到永远么？你掩面不顾我要到几时呢？
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?

chapters.zh: 我心里筹算，终日愁苦，要到几时呢？我的仇敌升高压制我，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，求你看顾我，应允我！使我眼目光明，免得我沉睡至死；
chapters.en: Consider hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the death;

chapters.zh: 免得我的仇敌说：我胜了他；免得我的敌人在我摇动的时候喜乐。
chapters.en: Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him; those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.

chapters.zh: 但我倚靠你的慈爱；我的心因你的救恩快乐。
chapters.en: But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.

chapters.zh: 我要向耶和华歌唱，因他用厚恩待我。
chapters.en: I will sing unto the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）愚顽人心里说：没有神。他们都是邪恶，行了可憎恶的事；没有一个人行善。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, of David.] The fool hath said in his heart, no God. They are corrupt, they have done abominable works, none that doeth good.

chapters.zh: 耶和华从天上垂看世人，要看有明白的没有，有寻求神的没有。
chapters.en: The LORD looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, seek God.

chapters.zh: 他们都偏离正路，一同变为污秽；并没有行善的，连一个也没有。
chapters.en: They are all gone aside, they are together become filthy: none that doeth good, no, not one.

chapters.zh: 作孽的都没有知识么？他们吞吃我的百姓，如同吃饭一样，并不求告耶和华。
chapters.en: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people they eat bread, and call not upon the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他们在那里大大地害怕，因为神在义人的族类中。
chapters.en: There were they in great fear: for God in the generation of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 你们叫困苦人的谋算变为羞辱；然而耶和华是他的避难所。
chapters.en: Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, because the LORD his refuge.

chapters.zh: 但愿以色列的救恩从锡安而出。耶和华救回他被掳的子民那时，雅各要快乐，以色列要欢喜。
chapters.en: Oh that the salvation of Israel out of Zion! when the LORD bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, Israel shall be glad.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华啊，谁能寄居你的帐幕？谁能住在你的圣山？
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] LORD, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?

chapters.zh: 就是行为正直、做事公义、心里说实话的人。
chapters.en: He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.

chapters.zh: 他不以舌头谗谤人，不恶待朋友，也不随伙毁谤邻里。
chapters.en: backbiteth not with his tongue, nor doeth evil to his neighbour, nor taketh up a reproach against his neighbour.

chapters.zh: 他眼中藐视匪类，却尊重那敬畏耶和华的人。他发了誓，虽然自己吃亏也不更改。
chapters.en: In whose eyes a vile person is contemned; but he honoureth them that fear the LORD. sweareth to hurt, and changeth not.

chapters.zh: 他不放债取利，不受贿赂以害无辜。行这些事的人必永不动摇。
chapters.en: putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent. He that doeth these shall never be moved.

chapters.zh: （大卫的金诗。）神啊，求你保佑我，因为我投靠你。
chapters.en: [Michtam of David.] Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你曾对耶和华说：你是我的主；我的好处不在你以外。
chapters.en: , thou hast said unto the LORD, Thou my Lord: my goodness not to thee;

chapters.zh: 论到世上的圣民，他们又美又善，是我最喜悦的。
chapters.en: to the saints that in the earth, and the excellent, in whom all my delight.

chapters.zh: 以别神代替耶和华的（或译：送礼物给别神的），他们的愁苦必加增；他们所浇奠的血我不献上；我嘴唇也不提别神的名号。
chapters.en: Their sorrows shall be multiplied hasten another : their drink offerings of blood will I not offer, nor take up their names into my lips.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的产业，是我杯中的分；我所得的，你为我持守。
chapters.en: The LORD the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.

chapters.zh: 用绳量给我的地界，坐落在佳美之处；我的产业实在美好。
chapters.en: The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant ; yea, I have a goodly heritage.

chapters.zh: 我必称颂那指教我的耶和华；我的心肠在夜间也警戒我。
chapters.en: I will bless the LORD, who hath given me counsel: my reins also instruct me in the night seasons.

chapters.zh: 我将耶和华常摆在我面前，因他在我右边，我便不致摇动。
chapters.en: I have set the LORD always before me: because at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

chapters.zh: 因此，我的心欢喜，我的灵（原文是荣耀）快乐；我的肉身也要安然居住。
chapters.en: Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoiceth: my flesh also shall rest in hope.

chapters.zh: 因为你必不将我的灵魂撇在阴间，也不叫你的圣者见朽坏。
chapters.en: For thou wilt not leave my soul in hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.

chapters.zh: 你必将生命的道路指示我。在你面前有满足的喜乐；在你右手中有永远的福乐。
chapters.en: Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence fulness of joy; at thy right hand pleasures for evermore.

chapters.zh: （大卫的祈祷。）耶和华啊，求你听闻公义，侧耳听我的呼吁！求你留心听我这不出于诡诈嘴唇的祈祷！
chapters.en: [A Prayer of David.] Hear the right, O LORD, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, not out of feigned lips.

chapters.zh: 愿我的判语从你面前发出；愿你的眼睛观看公正。
chapters.en: Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.

chapters.zh: 你已经试验我的心；你在夜间鉴察我；你熬炼我，却找不著甚么；我立志叫我口中没有过失。
chapters.en: Thou hast proved mine heart; thou hast visited in the night; thou hast tried me, shalt find nothing; I am purposed my mouth shall not transgress.

chapters.zh: 论到人的行为，我藉著你嘴唇的言语自己谨守，不行强暴人的道路。
chapters.en: Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept the paths of the destroyer.

chapters.zh: 我的脚踏定了你的路径；我的两脚未曾滑跌。
chapters.en: Hold up my goings in thy paths, my footsteps slip not.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我曾求告你，因为你必应允我；求你向我侧耳，听我的言语。
chapters.en: I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, my speech.

chapters.zh: 求你显出你奇妙的慈爱来；你是那用右手拯救投靠你的脱离起来攻击他们的人。
chapters.en: Shew thy marvellous lovingkindness, O thou that savest by thy right hand them which put their trust from those that rise up .

chapters.zh: 求你保护我，如同保护眼中的瞳人；将我隐藏在你翅膀的荫下，
chapters.en: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,

chapters.zh: 使我脱离那欺压我的恶人，就是围困我要害我命的仇敌。
chapters.en: From the wicked that oppress me, my deadly enemies, compass me about.

chapters.zh: 他们的心被脂油包裹；他们用口说骄傲的话。
chapters.en: They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.

chapters.zh: 他们围困了我们的脚步；他们瞪著眼，要把我们推倒在地。
chapters.en: They have now compassed us in our steps: they have set their eyes bowing down to the earth;

chapters.zh: 他像狮子急要抓食，又像少壮狮子蹲伏在暗处。
chapters.en: Like as a lion is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你起来，前去迎敌，将他打倒！用你的刀救护我命脱离恶人。
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, thy sword:

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你用手救我脱离世人，脱离那只在今生有福分的世人！你把你的财宝充满他们的肚腹；他们因有儿女就心满意足，将其余的财物留给他们的婴孩。
chapters.en: From men thy hand, O LORD, from men of the world, their portion in life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid : they are full of children, and leave the rest of their to their babes.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我必在义中见你的面；我醒了的时候，得见（或译：著）你的形像就心满意足了。
chapters.en: As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness: I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.

chapters.zh: （耶和华的仆人大卫的诗，交与伶长。当耶和华救他脱离一切仇敌和扫罗之手的日子，他向耶和华念这诗的话。说：）耶和华，我的力量啊，我爱你！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, of David, the servant of the LORD, who spake unto the LORD the words of this song in the day the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul: And he said,] I will love thee, O LORD, my strength.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的岩石，我的山寨，我的救主，我的神，我的磐石，我所投靠的。他是我的盾牌，是拯救我的角，是我的高台。
chapters.en: The LORD my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower.

chapters.zh: 我要求告当赞美的耶和华；这样我必从仇敌手中被救出来。
chapters.en: I will call upon the LORD, to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 曾有死亡的绳索缠绕我，匪类的急流使我惊惧，
chapters.en: The sorrows of death compassed me, and the floods of ungodly men made me afraid.

chapters.zh: 阴间的绳索缠绕我，死亡的网罗临到我。
chapters.en: The sorrows of hell compassed me about: the snares of death prevented me.

chapters.zh: 我在急难中求告耶和华，向我的神呼求。他从殿中听了我的声音；我在他面前的呼求入了他的耳中。
chapters.en: In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, into his ears.

chapters.zh: 那时，因他发怒，地就摇撼战抖；山的根基也震动摇撼。
chapters.en: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken, because he was wroth.

chapters.zh: 从他鼻孔冒烟上腾；从他口中发火焚烧，连炭也著了。
chapters.en: There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.

chapters.zh: 他又使天下垂，亲自降临，有黑云在他脚下。
chapters.en: He bowed the heavens also, and came down: and darkness under his feet.

chapters.zh: 他坐著基路伯飞行；他藉著风的翅膀快飞。
chapters.en: And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: yea, he did fly upon the wings of the wind.

chapters.zh: 他以黑暗为藏身之处，以水的黑暗、天空的厚云为他四围的行宫。
chapters.en: He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him dark waters thick clouds of the skies.

chapters.zh: 因他面前的光辉，他的厚云行过便有冰雹火炭。
chapters.en: At the brightness before him his thick clouds passed, hail and coals of fire.

chapters.zh: 耶和华也在天上打雷；至高者发出声音便有冰雹火炭。
chapters.en: The LORD also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail and coals of fire.

chapters.zh: 他射出箭来，使仇敌四散；多多发出闪电，使他们扰乱。
chapters.en: Yea, he sent out his arrows, and scattered them; and he shot out lightnings, and discomfited them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的斥责一发，你鼻孔的气一出，海底就出现，大地的根基也显露。
chapters.en: Then the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were discovered at thy rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.

chapters.zh: 他从高天伸手抓住我，把我从大水中拉上来。
chapters.en: He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.

chapters.zh: 他救我脱离我的劲敌和那些恨我的人，因为他们比我强盛。
chapters.en: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them which hated me: for they were too strong for me.

chapters.zh: 我遭遇灾难的日子，他们来攻击我；但耶和华是我的倚靠。
chapters.en: They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.

chapters.zh: 他又领我到宽阔之处；他救拔我，因他喜悦我。
chapters.en: He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华按著我的公义报答我，按著我手中的清洁赏赐我。
chapters.en: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.

chapters.zh: 因为我遵守了耶和华的道，未曾作恶离开我的神。
chapters.en: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

chapters.zh: 他的一切典章常在我面前；他的律例我也未曾丢弃。
chapters.en: For all his judgments before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me.

chapters.zh: 我在他面前作了完全人；我也保守自己远离我的罪孽。
chapters.en: I was also upright before him, and I kept myself from mine iniquity.

chapters.zh: 所以，耶和华按我的公义，按我在他眼前手中的清洁偿还我。
chapters.en: Therefore hath the LORD recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.

chapters.zh: 慈爱的人，你以慈爱待他；完全的人，你以完全待他。
chapters.en: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;

chapters.zh: 清洁的人，你以清洁待他；乖僻的人，你以弯曲待他。
chapters.en: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.

chapters.zh: 困苦的百姓，你必拯救；高傲的眼目，你必使他降卑。
chapters.en: For thou wilt save the afflicted people; but wilt bring down high looks.

chapters.zh: 你必点著我的灯；耶和华 ─ 我的神必照明我的黑暗。
chapters.en: For thou wilt light my candle: the LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.

chapters.zh: 我藉著你冲入敌军，藉著我的神跳过墙垣。
chapters.en: For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.

chapters.zh: 至于神，他的道是完全的；耶和华的话是炼净的。凡投靠他的，他便作他们的盾牌。
chapters.en: God, his way perfect: the word of the LORD is tried: he a buckler to all those that trust in him.

chapters.zh: 除了耶和华，谁是神呢？除了我们的神，谁是磐石呢？
chapters.en: For who God save the LORD? or who a rock save our God?

chapters.zh: 惟有那以力量束我的腰、使我行为完全的，他是神。
chapters.en: God that girdeth me with strength, and maketh my way perfect.

chapters.zh: 他使我的脚快如母鹿的蹄，又使我在高处安稳。
chapters.en: He maketh my feet like hinds' , and setteth me upon my high places.

chapters.zh: 他教导我的手能以争战，甚至我的膀臂能开铜弓。
chapters.en: He teacheth my hands to war, so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.

chapters.zh: 你把你的救恩给我作盾牌；你的右手扶持我；你的温和使我为大。
chapters.en: Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.

chapters.zh: 你使我脚下的地步宽阔；我的脚未曾滑跌。
chapters.en: Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.

chapters.zh: 我要追赶我的仇敌，并要追上他们；不将他们灭绝，我总不归回。
chapters.en: I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.

chapters.zh: 我要打伤他们，使他们不能起来；他们必倒在我的脚下。
chapters.en: I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.

chapters.zh: 因为你曾以力量束我的腰，使我能争战；你也使那起来攻击我的都服在我以下。
chapters.en: For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.

chapters.zh: 你又使我的仇敌在我面前转背逃跑，叫我能以剪除那恨我的人。
chapters.en: Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.

chapters.zh: 他们呼求，却无人拯救；就是呼求耶和华，他也不应允。
chapters.en: They cried, but none to save unto the LORD, but he answered them not.

chapters.zh: 我捣碎他们，如同风前的灰尘，倒出他们，如同街上的泥土。
chapters.en: Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets.

chapters.zh: 你救我脱离百姓的争竞，立我作列国的元首；我素不认识的民必事奉我。
chapters.en: Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people I have not known shall serve me.

chapters.zh: 他们一听见我的名声就必顺从我；外邦人要投降我。
chapters.en: As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.

chapters.zh: 外邦人要衰残，战战兢兢地出他们的营寨。
chapters.en: The strangers shall fade away, and be afraid out of their close places.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是活神。愿我的磐石被人称颂；愿救我的神被人尊崇。
chapters.en: The LORD liveth; and blessed my rock; and let the God of my salvation be exalted.

chapters.zh: 这位神，就是那为我伸冤、使众民服在我以下的。
chapters.en: God that avengeth me, and subdueth the people under me.

chapters.zh: 你救我脱离仇敌，又把我举起，高过那些起来攻击我的；你救我脱离强暴的人。
chapters.en: He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea, thou liftest me up above those that rise up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，因此我要在外邦中称谢你，歌颂你的名。
chapters.en: Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.

chapters.zh: 耶和华赐极大的救恩给他所立的王，施慈爱给他的受膏者，就是给大卫和他的后裔，直到永远。
chapters.en: Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）诸天述说神的荣耀；穹苍传扬他的手段。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament sheweth his handywork.

chapters.zh: 这日到那日发出言语；这夜到那夜传出知识。
chapters.en: Day unto day uttereth speech, and night unto night sheweth knowledge.

chapters.zh: 无言无语，也无声音可听。
chapters.en: no speech nor language, their voice is not heard.

chapters.zh: 他的量带通遍天下，他的言语传到地极。神在其间为太阳安设帐幕；
chapters.en: Their line is gone out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. In them hath he set a tabernacle for the sun,

chapters.zh: 太阳如同新郎出洞房，又如勇士欢然奔路。
chapters.en: Which as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, rejoiceth as a strong man to run a race.

chapters.zh: 他从天这边出来，绕到天那边，没有一物被隐藏不得他的热气。
chapters.en: His going forth from the end of the heaven, and his circuit unto the ends of it: and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的律法全备，能苏醒人心；耶和华的法度确定，能使愚人有智慧。
chapters.en: The law of the LORD perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the LORD sure, making wise the simple.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的训词正直，能快活人的心；耶和华的命令清洁，能明亮人的眼目。
chapters.en: The statutes of the LORD right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the LORD pure, enlightening the eyes.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的道理洁净，存到永远；耶和华的典章真实，全然公义 ─
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD clean, enduring for ever: the judgments of the LORD true righteous altogether.

chapters.zh: 都比金子可羡慕，且比极多的精金可羡慕；比蜜甘甜，且比蜂房下滴的蜜甘甜。
chapters.en: More to be desired than gold, yea, than much fine gold: sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.

chapters.zh: 况且你的仆人因此受警戒，守著这些便有大赏。
chapters.en: Moreover by them is thy servant warned: in keeping of them great reward.

chapters.zh: 谁能知道自己的错失呢？愿你赦免我隐而未现的过错。
chapters.en: Who can understand errors? cleanse thou me from secret .

chapters.zh: 求你拦阻仆人不犯任意妄为的罪，不容这罪辖制我，我便完全，免犯大罪。
chapters.en: Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous ; let them not have dominion over me: then shall I be upright, and I shall be innocent from the great transgression.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的磐石，我的救赎主啊，愿我口中的言语、心里的意念在你面前蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: Let the words of my mouth, and the meditation of my heart, be acceptable in thy sight, O LORD, my strength, and my redeemer.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）愿耶和华在你遭难的日子应允你；愿名为雅各神的高举你。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] The LORD hear thee in the day of trouble; the name of the God of Jacob defend thee;

chapters.zh: 愿他从圣所救助你，从锡安坚固你，
chapters.en: Send thee help from the sanctuary, and strengthen thee out of Zion;

chapters.zh: 记念你的一切供献，悦纳你的燔祭，（细拉）
chapters.en: Remember all thy offerings, and accept thy burnt sacrifice; Selah.

chapters.zh: 将你心所愿的赐给你，成就你的一切筹算。
chapters.en: Grant thee according to thine own heart, and fulfil all thy counsel.

chapters.zh: 我们要因你的救恩夸胜，要奉我们神的名竖立旌旗。愿耶和华成就你一切所求的！
chapters.en: We will rejoice in thy salvation, and in the name of our God we will set up banners: the LORD fulfil all thy petitions.

chapters.zh: 现在我知道耶和华救护他的受膏者，必从他的圣天上应允他，用右手的能力救护他。
chapters.en: Now know I that the LORD saveth his anointed; he will hear him from his holy heaven with the saving strength of his right hand.

chapters.zh: 有人靠车，有人靠马，但我们要提到耶和华 ─ 我们神的名。
chapters.en: Some in chariots, and some in horses: but we will remember the name of the LORD our God.

chapters.zh: 他们都屈身仆倒，我们却起来，立得正直。
chapters.en: They are brought down and fallen: but we are risen, and stand upright.

chapters.zh: 求耶和华施行拯救；我们呼求的时候，愿王应允我们！
chapters.en: Save, LORD: let the king hear us when we call.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）耶和华啊，王必因你的能力欢喜；因你的救恩，他的快乐何其大！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] The king shall joy in thy strength, O LORD; and in thy salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!

chapters.zh: 他心里所愿的，你已经赐给他；他嘴唇所求的，你未尝不应允。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou hast given him his heart's desire, and hast not withholden the request of his lips. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你以美福迎接他，把精金的冠冕戴在他头上。
chapters.en: For thou preventest him with the blessings of goodness: thou settest a crown of pure gold on his head.

chapters.zh: 他向你求寿，你便赐给他，就是日子长久，直到永远。
chapters.en: He asked life of thee, thou gavest him, length of days for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 他因你的救恩大有荣耀；你又将尊荣威严加在他身上。
chapters.en: His glory great in thy salvation: honour and majesty hast thou laid upon him.

chapters.zh: 你使他有洪福，直到永远，又使他在你面前欢喜快乐。
chapters.en: For thou hast made him most blessed for ever: thou hast made him exceeding glad with thy countenance.

chapters.zh: 王倚靠耶和华，因至高者的慈爱必不摇动。
chapters.en: For the king trusteth in the LORD, and through the mercy of the most High he shall not be moved.

chapters.zh: 你的手要搜出你的一切仇敌；你的右手要搜出那些恨你的人。
chapters.en: Thine hand shall find out all thine enemies: thy right hand shall find out those that hate thee.

chapters.zh: 你发怒的时候，要使他们如在炎热的火炉中。耶和华要在他的震怒中吞灭他们；那火要把他们烧尽了。
chapters.en: Thou shalt make them as a fiery oven in the time of thine anger: the LORD shall swallow them up in his wrath, and the fire shall devour them.

chapters.zh: 你必从世上灭绝他们的子孙（原文是果子），从人间灭绝他们的后裔。
chapters.en: Their fruit shalt thou destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.

chapters.zh: 因为他们有意加害于你；他们想出计谋，却不能做成。
chapters.en: For they intended evil against thee: they imagined a mischievous device, they are not able .

chapters.zh: 你必使他们转背逃跑，向他们的脸搭箭在弦。
chapters.en: Therefore shalt thou make them turn their back, thou shalt make ready upon thy strings against the face of them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，愿你因自己的能力显为至高！这样，我们就唱诗，歌颂你的大能。
chapters.en: Be thou exalted, LORD, in thine own strength: will we sing and praise thy power.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。调用朝鹿。）我的神，我的神！为甚么离弃我？为甚么远离不救我？不听我唉哼的言语？
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Aijeleth Shahar, A Psalm of David.] My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? far from helping me, the words of my roaring?

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，我白日呼求，你不应允，夜间呼求，并不住声。
chapters.en: O my God, I cry in the daytime, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.

chapters.zh: 但你是圣洁的，是用以色列的赞美为宝座（或译：居所）的。
chapters.en: But thou holy, that inhabitest the praises of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我们的祖宗倚靠你；他们倚靠你，你便解救他们。
chapters.en: Our fathers trusted in thee: they trusted, and thou didst deliver them.

chapters.zh: 他们哀求你，便蒙解救；他们倚靠你，就不羞愧。
chapters.en: They cried unto thee, and were delivered: they trusted in thee, and were not confounded.

chapters.zh: 但我是虫，不是人，被众人羞辱，被百姓藐视。
chapters.en: But I a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised of the people.

chapters.zh: 凡看见我的都嗤笑我；他们撇嘴摇头，说：
chapters.en: All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, ,

chapters.zh: 他把自己交托耶和华，耶和华可以救他罢！耶和华既喜悦他，可以搭救他罢！
chapters.en: He trusted on the LORD he would deliver him: let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.

chapters.zh: 但你是叫我出母腹的；我在母怀里，你就使我有倚靠的心。
chapters.en: But thou he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope upon my mother's breasts.

chapters.zh: 我自出母胎就被交在你手里；从我母亲生我，你就是我的神。
chapters.en: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou my God from my mother's belly.

chapters.zh: 求你不要远离我！因为急难临近了，没有人帮助我。
chapters.en: Be not far from me; for trouble near; for none to help.

chapters.zh: 有许多公牛围绕我，巴珊大力的公牛四面困住我。
chapters.en: Many bulls have compassed me: strong of Bashan have beset me round.

chapters.zh: 他们向我张口，好像抓撕吼叫的狮子。
chapters.en: They gaped upon me their mouths, a ravening and a roaring lion.

chapters.zh: 我如水被倒出来；我的骨头都脱了节；我心在我里面如蜡镕化。
chapters.en: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.

chapters.zh: 我的精力枯干，如同瓦片；我的舌头贴在我牙床上。你将我安置在死地的尘土中。
chapters.en: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.

chapters.zh: 犬类围著我，恶党环绕我；他们扎了我的手，我的脚。
chapters.en: For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.

chapters.zh: 我的骨头，我都能数过；他们瞪著眼看我。
chapters.en: I may tell all my bones: they look stare upon me.

chapters.zh: 他们分我的外衣，为我的里衣拈阄。
chapters.en: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你不要远离我！我的救主啊，求你快来帮助我！
chapters.en: But be not thou far from me, O LORD: O my strength, haste thee to help me.

chapters.zh: 求你救我的灵魂脱离刀剑，救我的生命（生命：原文是独一者）脱离犬类，
chapters.en: Deliver my soul from the sword; my darling from the power of the dog.

chapters.zh: 救我脱离狮子的口；你已经应允我，使我脱离野牛的角。
chapters.en: Save me from the lion's mouth: for thou hast heard me from the horns of the unicorns.

chapters.zh: 我要将你的名传与我的弟兄，在会中我要赞美你。
chapters.en: I will declare thy name unto my brethren: in the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.

chapters.zh: 你们敬畏耶和华的人要赞美他！雅各的后裔都要荣耀他！以色列的后裔都要惧怕他！
chapters.en: Ye that fear the LORD, praise him; all ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; and fear him, all ye the seed of Israel.

chapters.zh: 因为他没有藐视憎恶受苦的人，也没有向他掩面；那受苦之人呼吁的时候，他就垂听。
chapters.en: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him; but when he cried unto him, he heard.

chapters.zh: 我在大会中赞美你的话是从你而来的；我要在敬畏耶和华的人面前还我的愿。
chapters.en: My praise of thee in the great congregation: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.

chapters.zh: 谦卑的人必吃得饱足；寻求耶和华的人必赞美他。愿你们的心永远活著！
chapters.en: The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the LORD that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.

chapters.zh: 地的四极都要想念耶和华，并且归顺他；列国的万族都要在你面前敬拜。
chapters.en: All the ends of the world shall remember and turn unto the LORD: and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.

chapters.zh: 因为国权是耶和华的；他是管理万国的。
chapters.en: For the kingdom the LORD'S: and he the governor among the nations.

chapters.zh: 地上一切丰肥的人必吃喝而敬拜；凡下到尘土中 ─ 不能存活自己性命的人 ─ 都要在他面前下拜。
chapters.en: All fat upon earth shall eat and worship: all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him: and none can keep alive his own soul.

chapters.zh: 他必有后裔事奉他；主所行的事必传与后代。
chapters.en: A seed shall serve him; it shall be accounted to the Lord for a generation.

chapters.zh: 他们必来把他的公义传给将要生的民，言明这事是他所行的。
chapters.en: They shall come, and shall declare his righteousness unto a people that shall be born, that he hath done .

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华是我的牧者，我必不致缺乏。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] The LORD my shepherd; I shall not want.

chapters.zh: 他使我躺卧在青草地上，领我在可安歇的水边。
chapters.en: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still waters.

chapters.zh: 他使我的灵魂苏醒，为自己的名引导我走义路。
chapters.en: He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

chapters.zh: 我虽然行过死荫的幽谷，也不怕遭害，因为你与我同在；你的杖，你的竿，都安慰我。
chapters.en: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.

chapters.zh: 在我敌人面前，你为我摆设筵席；你用油膏了我的头，使我的福杯满溢。
chapters.en: Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies: thou anointest my head with oil; my cup runneth over.

chapters.zh: 我一生一世必有恩惠慈爱随著我；我且要住在耶和华的殿中，直到永远。
chapters.en: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）地和其中所充满的，世界和住在其间的，都属耶和华。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] The earth the LORD'S, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 他把地建立在海上，安定在大水之上。
chapters.en: For he hath founded it upon the seas, and established it upon the floods.

chapters.zh: 谁能登耶和华的山？谁能站在他的圣所？
chapters.en: Who shall ascend into the hill of the LORD? or who shall stand in his holy place?

chapters.zh: 就是手洁心清、不向虚妄、起誓不怀诡诈的人。
chapters.en: He that hath clean hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lifted up his soul unto vanity, nor sworn deceitfully.

chapters.zh: 他必蒙耶和华赐福，又蒙救他的神使他成义。
chapters.en: He shall receive the blessing from the LORD, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.

chapters.zh: 这是寻求耶和华的族类，是寻求你面的雅各。（细拉）
chapters.en: This the generation of them that seek him, that seek thy face, O Jacob. Selah.

chapters.zh: 众城门哪，你们要抬起头来！永久的门户，你们要被举起！那荣耀的王将要进来！
chapters.en: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.

chapters.zh: 荣耀的王是谁呢？就是有力有能的耶和华，在战场上有能的耶和华！
chapters.en: Who this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.

chapters.zh: 众城门哪，你们要抬起头来！永久的门户，你们要把头抬起！那荣耀的王将要进来！
chapters.en: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; even lift up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.

chapters.zh: 荣耀的王是谁呢？万军之耶和华，他是荣耀的王！（细拉）
chapters.en: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, he the King of glory. Selah.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华啊，我的心仰望你。
chapters.en: [ of David.] Unto thee, O LORD, do I lift up my soul.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，我素来倚靠你；求你不要叫我羞愧，不要叫我的仇敌向我夸胜。
chapters.en: O my God, I trust in thee: let me not be ashamed, let not mine enemies triumph over me.

chapters.zh: 凡等候你的必不羞愧；惟有那无故行奸诈的必要羞愧。
chapters.en: Yea, let none that wait on thee be ashamed: let them be ashamed which transgress without cause.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你将你的道指示我，将你的路教训我！
chapters.en: Shew me thy ways, O LORD; teach me thy paths.

chapters.zh: 求你以你的真理引导我，教训我，因为你是救我的神。我终日等候你。
chapters.en: Lead me in thy truth, and teach me: for thou the God of my salvation; on thee do I wait all the day.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你记念你的怜悯和慈爱，因为这是亘古以来所常有的。
chapters.en: Remember, O LORD, thy tender mercies and thy lovingkindnesses; for they ever of old.

chapters.zh: 求你不要记念我幼年的罪愆和我的过犯；耶和华啊，求你因你的恩惠，按你的慈爱记念我。
chapters.en: Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions: according to thy mercy remember thou me for thy goodness' sake, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是良善正直的，所以他必指示罪人走正路。
chapters.en: Good and upright the LORD: therefore will he teach sinners in the way.

chapters.zh: 他必按公平引领谦卑人，将他的道教训他们。
chapters.en: The meek will he guide in judgment: and the meek will he teach his way.

chapters.zh: 凡遵守他的约和他法度的人，耶和华都以慈爱诚实待他。
chapters.en: All the paths of the LORD mercy and truth unto such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你因你的名赦免我的罪，因为我的罪重大。
chapters.en: For thy name's sake, O LORD, pardon mine iniquity; for it great.

chapters.zh: 谁敬畏耶和华，耶和华必指示他当选择的道路。
chapters.en: What man he that feareth the LORD? him shall he teach in the way he shall choose.

chapters.zh: 他必安然居住；他的后裔必承受地土。
chapters.en: His soul shall dwell at ease; and his seed shall inherit the earth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华与敬畏他的人亲密；他必将自己的约指示他们。
chapters.en: The secret of the LORD with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.

chapters.zh: 我的眼目时常仰望耶和华，因为他必将我的脚从网里拉出来。
chapters.en: Mine eyes ever toward the LORD; for he shall pluck my feet out of the net.

chapters.zh: 求你转向我，怜恤我，因为我是孤独困苦。
chapters.en: Turn thee unto me, and have mercy upon me; for I desolate and afflicted.

chapters.zh: 我心里的愁苦甚多，求你救我脱离我的祸患。
chapters.en: The troubles of my heart are enlarged: bring thou me out of my distresses.

chapters.zh: 求你看顾我的困苦，我的艰难，赦免我一切的罪。
chapters.en: Look upon mine affliction and my pain; and forgive all my sins.

chapters.zh: 求你察看我的仇敌，因为他们人多，并且痛痛地恨我。
chapters.en: Consider mine enemies; for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.

chapters.zh: 求你保护我的性命，搭救我，使我不致羞愧，因为我投靠你。
chapters.en: O keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be ashamed; for I put my trust in thee.

chapters.zh: 愿纯全、正直保守我，因为我等候你。
chapters.en: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me; for I wait on thee.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你救赎以色列脱离他一切的愁苦。
chapters.en: Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his troubles.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华啊，求你为我伸冤，因我向来行事纯全；我又倚靠耶和华，并不摇动。
chapters.en: [ of David.] Judge me, O LORD; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the LORD; I shall not slide.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你察看我，试验我，熬炼我的肺腑心肠。
chapters.en: Examine me, O LORD, and prove me; try my reins and my heart.

chapters.zh: 因为你的慈爱常在我眼前，我也按你的真理而行。
chapters.en: For thy lovingkindness before mine eyes: and I have walked in thy truth.

chapters.zh: 我没有和虚谎人同坐，也不与瞒哄人的同群。
chapters.en: I have not sat with vain persons, neither will I go in with dissemblers.

chapters.zh: 我恨恶恶人的会，必不与恶人同坐。
chapters.en: I have hated the congregation of evil doers; and will not sit with the wicked.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我要洗手表明无辜，才环绕你的祭坛；
chapters.en: I will wash mine hands in innocency: so will I compass thine altar, O LORD:

chapters.zh: 我好发称谢的声音，也要述说你一切奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: That I may publish with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我喜爱你所住的殿和你显荣耀的居所。
chapters.en: LORD, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.

chapters.zh: 不要把我的灵魂和罪人一同除掉；不要把我的性命和流人血的一同除掉。
chapters.en: Gather not my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloody men:

chapters.zh: 他们的手中有奸恶，右手满有贿赂。
chapters.en: In whose hands mischief, and their right hand is full of bribes.

chapters.zh: 至于我，却要行事纯全；求你救赎我，怜恤我！
chapters.en: But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity: redeem me, and be merciful unto me.

chapters.zh: 我的脚站在平坦地方；在众会中我要称颂耶和华！
chapters.en: My foot standeth in an even place: in the congregations will I bless the LORD.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华是我的亮光，是我的拯救，我还怕谁呢？耶和华是我性命的保障（或译：力量），我还惧谁呢？
chapters.en: [ of David.] The LORD my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?

chapters.zh: 那作恶的就是我的仇敌，前来吃我肉的时候就绊跌仆倒。
chapters.en: When the wicked, mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell.

chapters.zh: 虽有军兵安营攻击我，我的心也不害怕；虽然兴起刀兵攻击我，我必仍旧安稳。
chapters.en: Though an host should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear: though war should rise against me, in this I confident.

chapters.zh: 有一件事，我曾求耶和华，我仍要寻求：就是一生一世住在耶和华的殿中，瞻仰他的荣美，在他的殿里求问。
chapters.en: One have I desired of the LORD, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the LORD, and to enquire in his temple.

chapters.zh: 因为我遭遇患难，他必暗暗地保守我；在他亭子里，把我藏在他帐幕的隐密处，将我高举在磐石上。
chapters.en: For in the time of trouble he shall hide me in his pavilion: in the secret of his tabernacle shall he hide me; he shall set me up upon a rock.

chapters.zh: 现在我得以昂首，高过四面的仇敌。我要在他的帐幕里欢然献祭；我要唱诗歌颂耶和华。
chapters.en: And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我用声音呼吁的时候，求你垂听；并求你怜恤我，应允我。
chapters.en: Hear, O LORD, I cry with my voice: have mercy also upon me, and answer me.

chapters.zh: 你说：你们当寻求我的面。那时我心向你说：耶和华啊，你的面我正要寻求。
chapters.en: , Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, LORD, will I seek.

chapters.zh: 不要向我掩面。不要发怒赶逐仆人，你向来是帮助我的。救我的神啊，不要丢掉我，也不要离弃我。
chapters.en: Hide not thy face from me; put not thy servant away in anger: thou hast been my help; leave me not, neither forsake me, O God of my salvation.

chapters.zh: 我父母离弃我，耶和华必收留我。
chapters.en: When my father and my mother forsake me, then the LORD will take me up.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你将你的道指教我，因我仇敌的缘故引导我走平坦的路。
chapters.en: Teach me thy way, O LORD, and lead me in a plain path, because of mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 求你不要把我交给敌人，遂其所愿；因为妄作见证的和口吐凶言的，起来攻击我。
chapters.en: Deliver me not over unto the will of mine enemies: for false witnesses are risen up against me, and such as breathe out cruelty.

chapters.zh: 我若不信在活人之地得见耶和华的恩惠，就早已丧胆了。
chapters.en: , unless I had believed to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 要等候耶和华！当壮胆，坚固你的心！我再说，要等候耶和华！
chapters.en: Wait on the LORD: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the LORD.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华啊，我要求告你！我的磐石啊，不要向我缄默！倘若你向我闭口，我就如将死的人一样。
chapters.en: [ of David.] Unto thee will I cry, O LORD my rock; be not silent to me: lest, thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.

chapters.zh: 我呼求你，向你至圣所举手的时候，求你垂听我恳求的声音！
chapters.en: Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.

chapters.zh: 不要把我和恶人并作孽的一同除掉；他们与邻舍说和平话，心里却是奸恶。
chapters.en: Draw me not away with the wicked, and with the workers of iniquity, which speak peace to their neighbours, but mischief in their hearts.

chapters.zh: 愿你按著他们所做的，并他们所行的恶事待他们。愿你照著他们手所做的待他们，将他们所应得的报应加给他们。
chapters.en: Give them according to their deeds, and according to the wickedness of their endeavours: give them after the work of their hands; render to them their desert.

chapters.zh: 他们既然不留心耶和华所行的和他手所做的，他就必毁坏他们，不建立他们。
chapters.en: Because they regard not the works of the LORD, nor the operation of his hands, he shall destroy them, and not build them up.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是应当称颂的，因为他听了我恳求的声音。
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的力量，是我的盾牌；我心里倚靠他就得帮助。所以我心中欢乐，我必用诗歌颂赞他。
chapters.en: The LORD my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是他百姓的力量，又是他受膏者得救的保障。
chapters.en: The LORD their strength, and he the saving strength of his anointed.

chapters.zh: 求你拯救你的百姓，赐福给你的产业，牧养他们，扶持他们，直到永远。
chapters.en: Save thy people, and bless thine inheritance: feed them also, and lift them up for ever.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）神的众子啊，你们要将荣耀、能力归给耶和华，归给耶和华！
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] Give unto the LORD, O ye mighty, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

chapters.zh: 要将耶和华的名所当得的荣耀归给他，以圣洁的（的：或译为）妆饰敬拜耶和华。
chapters.en: Give unto the LORD the glory due unto his name; worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音发在水上；荣耀的神打雷，耶和华打雷在大水之上。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD upon the waters: the God of glory thundereth: the LORD upon many waters.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音大有能力；耶和华的声音满有威严。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD powerful; the voice of the LORD full of majesty.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音震破香柏树；耶和华震碎利巴嫩的香柏树。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD breaketh the cedars; yea, the LORD breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.

chapters.zh: 他也使之跳跃如牛犊，使利巴嫩和西连跳跃如野牛犊。
chapters.en: He maketh them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young unicorn.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音使火燄分岔。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD divideth the flames of fire.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音震动旷野；耶和华震动加低斯的旷野。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD shaketh the wilderness; the LORD shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的声音惊动母鹿落胎，树木也脱落净光。凡在他殿中的，都称说他的荣耀。
chapters.en: The voice of the LORD maketh the hinds to calve, and discovereth the forests: and in his temple doth every one speak of glory.

chapters.zh: 洪水泛滥之时，耶和华坐著为王；耶和华坐著为王，直到永远。
chapters.en: The LORD sitteth upon the flood; yea, the LORD sitteth King for ever.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必赐力量给他的百姓；耶和华必赐平安的福给他的百姓。
chapters.en: The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.

chapters.zh: （大卫在献殿的时候，作这诗歌。）耶和华啊，我要尊崇你，因为你曾提拔我，不叫仇敌向我夸耀。
chapters.en: [A Psalm Song the dedication of the house of David.] I will extol thee, O LORD; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，我曾呼求你，你医治了我。
chapters.en: O LORD my God, I cried unto thee, and thou hast healed me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你曾把我的灵魂从阴间救上来，使我存活，不至于下坑。
chapters.en: O LORD, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的圣民哪，你们要歌颂他，称赞他可记念的圣名。
chapters.en: Sing unto the LORD, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

chapters.zh: 因为，他的怒气不过是转眼之间；他的恩典乃是一生之久。一宿虽然有哭泣，早晨便必欢呼。
chapters.en: For his anger a moment; in his favour life: weeping may endure for a night, but joy in the morning.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我凡事平顺便说：我永不动摇。
chapters.en: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你曾施恩，叫我的江山稳固；你掩了面，我就惊惶。
chapters.en: LORD, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, I was troubled.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我曾求告你；我向耶和华恳求，说：
chapters.en: I cried to thee, O LORD; and unto the LORD I made supplication.

chapters.zh: 我被害流血，下到坑中，有甚么益处呢？尘土岂能称赞你，传说你的诚实么？
chapters.en: What profit in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你应允我，怜恤我！耶和华啊，求你帮助我！
chapters.en: Hear, O LORD, and have mercy upon me: LORD, be thou my helper.

chapters.zh: 你已将我的哀哭变为跳舞，将我的麻衣脱去，给我披上喜乐，
chapters.en: Thou hast turned for me my mourning into dancing: thou hast put off my sackcloth, and girded me with gladness;

chapters.zh: 好叫我的灵（原文是荣耀）歌颂你，并不住声。耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，我要称谢你，直到永远！
chapters.en: To the end that glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）耶和华啊，我投靠你；求你使我永不羞愧；凭你的公义搭救我！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.

chapters.zh: 求你侧耳而听，快快救我！作我坚固的磐石，拯救我的保障！
chapters.en: Bow down thine ear to me; deliver me speedily: be thou my strong rock, for an house of defence to save me.

chapters.zh: 因为你是我的岩石，我的山寨；所以，求你为你名的缘故引导我，指点我。
chapters.en: For thou my rock and my fortress; therefore for thy name's sake lead me, and guide me.

chapters.zh: 求你救我脱离人为我暗设的网罗，因为你是我的保障。
chapters.en: Pull me out of the net that they have laid privily for me: for thou my strength.

chapters.zh: 我将我的灵魂交在你手里；耶和华诚实的神啊，你救赎了我。
chapters.en: Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O LORD God of truth.

chapters.zh: 我恨恶那信奉虚无之神的人；我却倚靠耶和华。
chapters.en: I have hated them that regard lying vanities: but I trust in the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我要为你的慈爱高兴欢喜；因为你见过我的困苦，知道我心中的艰难。
chapters.en: I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;

chapters.zh: 你未曾把我交在仇敌手里；你使我的脚站在宽阔之处。
chapters.en: And hast not shut me up into the hand of the enemy: thou hast set my feet in a large room.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你怜恤我，因为我在急难之中；我的眼睛因忧愁而干瘪，连我的身心也不安舒。
chapters.en: Have mercy upon me, O LORD, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, , my soul and my belly.

chapters.zh: 我的生命为愁苦所消耗；我的年岁为叹息所旷废。我的力量因我的罪孽衰败；我的骨头也枯干。
chapters.en: For my life is spent with grief, and my years with sighing: my strength faileth because of mine iniquity, and my bones are consumed.

chapters.zh: 我因一切敌人成了羞辱，在我的邻舍跟前更甚；那认识我的都惧怕我，在外头看见我的都躲避我。
chapters.en: I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.

chapters.zh: 我被人忘记，如同死人，无人记念；我好像破碎的器皿。
chapters.en: I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind: I am like a broken vessel.

chapters.zh: 我听见了许多人的谗谤，四围都是惊吓；他们一同商议攻击我的时候，就图谋要害我的性命。
chapters.en: For I have heard the slander of many: fear on every side: while they took counsel together against me, they devised to take away my life.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我仍旧倚靠你；我说：你是我的神。
chapters.en: But I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou my God.

chapters.zh: 我终身的事在你手中；求你救我脱离仇敌的手和那些逼迫我的人。
chapters.en: My times in thy hand: deliver me from the hand of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.

chapters.zh: 求你使你的脸光照仆人，凭你的慈爱拯救我。
chapters.en: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant: save me for thy mercies' sake.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你叫我不致羞愧，因为我曾呼吁你；求你使恶人羞愧，使他们在阴间缄默无声。
chapters.en: Let me not be ashamed, O LORD; for I have called upon thee: let the wicked be ashamed, let them be silent in the grave.

chapters.zh: 那撒谎的人逞骄傲轻慢，出狂妄的话攻击义人；愿他的嘴哑而无言。
chapters.en: Let the lying lips be put to silence; which speak grievous things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.

chapters.zh: 敬畏你、投靠你的人，你为他们所积存的，在世人面前所施行的恩惠是何等大呢！
chapters.en: how great thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee; thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee before the sons of men!

chapters.zh: 你必把他们藏在你面前的隐密处，免得遇见人的计谋；你必暗暗地保守他们在亭子里，免受口舌的争闹。
chapters.en: Thou shalt hide them in the secret of thy presence from the pride of man: thou shalt keep them secretly in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是应当称颂的，因为他在坚固城里向我施展奇妙的慈爱。
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD: for he hath shewed me his marvellous kindness in a strong city.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我曾急促地说：我从你眼前被隔绝。然而，我呼求你的时候，你仍听我恳求的声音。
chapters.en: For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的圣民哪，你们都要爱他！耶和华保护诚实人，足足报应行事骄傲的人。
chapters.en: O love the LORD, all ye his saints: the LORD preserveth the faithful, and plentifully rewardeth the proud doer.

chapters.zh: 凡仰望耶和华的人，你们都要壮胆，坚固你们的心！
chapters.en: Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the LORD.

chapters.zh: （大卫的训诲诗。）得赦免其过、遮盖其罪的，这人是有福的！
chapters.en: [ of David, Maschil.] Blessed transgression forgiven, sin covered.

chapters.zh: 凡心里没有诡诈、耶和华不算为有罪的，这人是有福的！
chapters.en: Blessed the man unto whom the LORD imputeth not iniquity, and in whose spirit no guile.

chapters.zh: 我闭口不认罪的时候，因终日唉哼而骨头枯干。
chapters.en: When I kept silence, my bones waxed old through my roaring all the day long.

chapters.zh: 黑夜白日，你的手在我身上沉重；我的精液耗尽，如同夏天的干旱。（细拉）
chapters.en: For day and night thy hand was heavy upon me: my moisture is turned into the drought of summer. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我向你陈明我的罪，不隐瞒我的恶。我说：我要向耶和华承认我的过犯，你就赦免我的罪恶。（细拉）
chapters.en: I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the LORD; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

chapters.zh: 为此，凡虔诚人都当趁你可寻找的时候祷告你；大水泛溢的时候，必不能到他那里。
chapters.en: For this shall every one that is godly pray unto thee in a time when thou mayest be found: surely in the floods of great waters they shall not come nigh unto him.

chapters.zh: 你是我藏身之处；你必保佑我脱离苦难，以得救的乐歌四面环绕我。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou my hiding place; thou shalt preserve me from trouble; thou shalt compass me about with songs of deliverance. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我要教导你，指示你当行的路；我要定睛在你身上劝戒你。
chapters.en: I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.

chapters.zh: 你不可像那无知的骡马，必用嚼环辔头勒住他；不然，就不能驯服。
chapters.en: Be ye not as the horse, as the mule, have no understanding: whose mouth must be held in with bit and bridle, lest they come near unto thee.

chapters.zh: 恶人必多受苦楚；惟独倚靠耶和华的必有慈爱四面环绕他。
chapters.en: Many sorrows to the wicked: but he that trusteth in the LORD, mercy shall compass him about.

chapters.zh: 你们义人应当靠耶和华欢喜快乐；你们心里正直的人都当欢呼。
chapters.en: Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, ye righteous: and shout for joy, all upright in heart.

chapters.zh: 义人哪，你们应当靠耶和华欢乐；正直人的赞美是合宜的。
chapters.en: Rejoice in the LORD, O ye righteous: praise is comely for the upright.

chapters.zh: 你们应当弹琴称谢耶和华，用十弦瑟歌颂他。
chapters.en: Praise the LORD with harp: sing unto him with the psaltery an instrument of ten strings.

chapters.zh: 应当向他唱新歌，弹得巧妙，声音洪亮。
chapters.en: Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud noise.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华的言语正直；凡他所做的尽都诚实。
chapters.en: For the word of the LORD right; and all his works in truth.

chapters.zh: 他喜爱仁义公平；遍地满了耶和华的慈爱。
chapters.en: He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 诸天藉耶和华的命而造；万象藉他口中的气而成。
chapters.en: By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.

chapters.zh: 他聚集海水如垒，收藏深洋在库房。
chapters.en: He gathereth the waters of the sea together as an heap: he layeth up the depth in storehouses.

chapters.zh: 愿全地都敬畏耶和华！愿世上的居民都惧怕他！
chapters.en: Let all the earth fear the LORD: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.

chapters.zh: 因为他说有，就有，命立，就立。
chapters.en: For he spake, and it was ; he commanded, and it stood fast.

chapters.zh: 耶和华使列国的筹算归于无有，使众民的思念无有功效。
chapters.en: The LORD bringeth the counsel of the heathen to nought: he maketh the devices of the people of none effect.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的筹算永远立定；他心中的思念万代常存。
chapters.en: The counsel of the LORD standeth for ever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.

chapters.zh: 以耶和华为神的，那国是有福的！他所拣选为自己产业的，那民是有福的！
chapters.en: Blessed the nation whose God the LORD; the people he hath chosen for his own inheritance.

chapters.zh: 耶和华从天上观看；他看见一切的世人。
chapters.en: The LORD looketh from heaven; he beholdeth all the sons of men.

chapters.zh: 从他的居所往外察看地上一切的居民。
chapters.en: From the place of his habitation he looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth.

chapters.zh: 他是那造成他们众人心的，留意他们一切作为的。
chapters.en: He fashioneth their hearts alike; he considereth all their works.

chapters.zh: 君王不能因兵多得胜；勇士不能因力大得救。
chapters.en: There is no king saved by the multitude of an host: a mighty man is not delivered by much strength.

chapters.zh: 靠马得救是枉然的；马也不能因力大救人。
chapters.en: An horse a vain thing for safety: neither shall he deliver by his great strength.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的眼目看顾敬畏他的人和仰望他慈爱的人，
chapters.en: Behold, the eye of the LORD upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;

chapters.zh: 要救他们的命脱离死亡，并使他们在饥荒中存活。
chapters.en: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.

chapters.zh: 我们的心向来等候耶和华；他是我们的帮助，我们的盾牌。
chapters.en: Our soul waiteth for the LORD: he our help and our shield.

chapters.zh: 我们的心必靠他欢喜，因为我们向来倚靠他的圣名。
chapters.en: For our heart shall rejoice in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你照著我们所仰望你的，向我们施行慈爱！
chapters.en: Let thy mercy, O LORD, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.

chapters.zh: （大卫在亚比米勒面前装疯，被他赶出去，就作这诗。）我要时时称颂耶和华；赞美他的话必常在我口中。
chapters.en: [ of David, when he changed his behaviour before Abimelech; who drove him away, and he departed.] I will bless the LORD at all times: his praise continually in my mouth.

chapters.zh: 我的心必因耶和华夸耀；谦卑人听见就要喜乐。
chapters.en: My soul shall make her boast in the LORD: the humble shall hear , and be glad.

chapters.zh: 你们和我当称耶和华为大，一同高举他的名。
chapters.en: O magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.

chapters.zh: 我曾寻求耶和华，他就应允我，救我脱离了一切的恐惧。
chapters.en: I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.

chapters.zh: 凡仰望他的，便有光荣；他们的脸必不蒙羞。
chapters.en: They looked unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.

chapters.zh: 我这困苦人呼求，耶和华便垂听，救我脱离一切患难。
chapters.en: This poor man cried, and the LORD heard , and saved him out of all his troubles.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者在敬畏他的人四围安营，搭救他们。
chapters.en: The angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.

chapters.zh: 你们要尝尝主恩的滋味，便知道他是美善；投靠他的人有福了！
chapters.en: O taste and see that the LORD good: blessed the man trusteth in him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的圣民哪，你们当敬畏他，因敬畏他的一无所缺。
chapters.en: O fear the LORD, ye his saints: for no want to them that fear him.

chapters.zh: 少壮狮子还缺食忍饿，但寻求耶和华的甚么好处都不缺。
chapters.en: The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the LORD shall not want any good .

chapters.zh: 众弟子啊，你们当来听我的话！我要将敬畏耶和华的道教训你们。
chapters.en: Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 有何人喜好存活，爱慕长寿，得享美福，
chapters.en: What man desireth life, loveth days, that he may see good?

chapters.zh: 就要禁止舌头不出恶言，嘴唇不说诡诈的话。
chapters.en: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.

chapters.zh: 要离恶行善，寻求和睦，一心追赶。
chapters.en: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的眼目看顾义人；他的耳朵听他们的呼求。
chapters.en: The eyes of the LORD upon the righteous, and his ears unto their cry.

chapters.zh: 耶和华向行恶的人变脸，要从世上除灭他们的名号。
chapters.en: The face of the LORD against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.

chapters.zh: 义人呼求，耶和华听见了，便救他们脱离一切患难。
chapters.en: cry, and the LORD heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.

chapters.zh: 耶和华靠近伤心的人，拯救灵性痛悔的人。
chapters.en: The LORD nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.

chapters.zh: 义人多有苦难，但耶和华救他脱离这一切，
chapters.en: Many the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all.

chapters.zh: 又保全他一身的骨头，连一根也不折断。
chapters.en: He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.

chapters.zh: 恶必害死恶人；恨恶义人的，必被定罪。
chapters.en: Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.

chapters.zh: 耶和华救赎他仆人的灵魂；凡投靠他的，必不致定罪。
chapters.en: The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华啊，与我相争的，求你与他们相争！与我相战的，求你与他们相战！
chapters.en: [ of David.] Plead , O LORD, with them that strive with me: fight against them that fight against me.

chapters.zh: 拿著大小的盾牌，起来帮助我。
chapters.en: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for mine help.

chapters.zh: 抽出枪来，挡住那追赶我的；求你对我的灵魂说：我是拯救你的。
chapters.en: Draw out also the spear, and stop against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I thy salvation.

chapters.zh: 愿那寻索我命的，蒙羞受辱！愿那谋害我的，退后羞愧！
chapters.en: Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion that devise my hurt.

chapters.zh: 愿他们像风前的糠，有耶和华的使者赶逐他们。
chapters.en: Let them be as chaff before the wind: and let the angel of the LORD chase .

chapters.zh: 愿他们的道路又暗又滑，有耶和华的使者追赶他们。
chapters.en: Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the LORD persecute them.

chapters.zh: 因他们无故地为我暗设网罗，无故地挖坑，要害我的性命。
chapters.en: For without cause have they hid for me their net a pit, without cause they have digged for my soul.

chapters.zh: 愿灾祸忽然临到他身上！愿他暗设的网缠住自己！愿他落在其中遭灾祸！
chapters.en: Let destruction come upon him at unawares; and let his net that he hath hid catch himself: into that very destruction let him fall.

chapters.zh: 我的心必靠耶和华快乐，靠他的救恩高兴。
chapters.en: And my soul shall be joyful in the LORD: it shall rejoice in his salvation.

chapters.zh: 我的骨头都要说：耶和华啊，谁能像你救护困苦人脱离那比他强壮的，救护困苦穷乏人脱离那抢夺他的？
chapters.en: All my bones shall say, LORD, who like unto thee, which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from him that spoileth him?

chapters.zh: 凶恶的见证人起来，盘问我所不知道的事。
chapters.en: False witnesses did rise up; they laid to my charge that I knew not.

chapters.zh: 他们向我以恶报善，使我的灵魂孤苦。
chapters.en: They rewarded me evil for good the spoiling of my soul.

chapters.zh: 至于我，当他们有病的时候，我便穿麻衣，禁食，刻苦己心；我所求的都归到自己的怀中。
chapters.en: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting; and my prayer returned into mine own bosom.

chapters.zh: 我这样行，好像他是我的朋友，我的弟兄；我屈身悲哀，如同人为母亲哀痛。
chapters.en: I behaved myself as though my friend brother: I bowed down heavily, as one that mourneth mother.

chapters.zh: 我在患难中，他们却欢喜，大家聚集。我所不认识的那些下流人聚集攻击我；他们不住地把我撕裂。
chapters.en: But in mine adversity they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together: , the abjects gathered themselves together against me, and I knew not; they did tear , and ceased not:

chapters.zh: 他们如同席上好嬉笑的狂妄人向我咬牙。
chapters.en: With hypocritical mockers in feasts, they gnashed upon me with their teeth.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你看著不理要到几时呢？求你救我的灵魂脱离他们的残害！救我的生命（生命：原文是独一者）脱离少壮狮子！
chapters.en: Lord, how long wilt thou look on? rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions.

chapters.zh: 我在大会中要称谢你，在众民中要赞美你。
chapters.en: I will give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.

chapters.zh: 求你不容那无理与我为仇的向我夸耀！不容那无故恨我的向我挤眼！
chapters.en: Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause.

chapters.zh: 因为他们不说和平话，倒想出诡诈的言语害地上的安静人。
chapters.en: For they speak not peace: but they devise deceitful matters against quiet in the land.

chapters.zh: 他们大大张口攻击我，说：阿哈，阿哈，我们的眼已经看见了！
chapters.en: Yea, they opened their mouth wide against me, said, Aha, aha, our eye hath seen .

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你已经看见了，求你不要闭口！主啊，求你不要远离我！
chapters.en: thou hast seen, O LORD: keep not silence: O Lord, be not far from me.

chapters.zh: 我的神我的主啊，求你奋兴醒起，判清我的事，伸明我的冤！
chapters.en: Stir up thyself, and awake to my judgment, unto my cause, my God and my Lord.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，求你按你的公义判断我，不容他们向我夸耀！
chapters.en: Judge me, O LORD my God, according to thy righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.

chapters.zh: 不容他们心里说：阿哈，遂我们的心愿了！不容他们说：我们已经把他吞了！
chapters.en: Let them not say in their hearts, Ah, so would we have it: let them not say, We have swallowed him up.

chapters.zh: 愿那喜欢我遭难的一同抱愧蒙羞！愿那向我妄自尊大的披惭愧，蒙羞辱！
chapters.en: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify against me.

chapters.zh: 愿那喜悦我冤屈得伸的（冤屈得伸：原文是公义）欢呼快乐；愿他们常说：当尊耶和华为大！耶和华喜悦他的仆人平安。
chapters.en: Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.

chapters.zh: 我的舌头要终日论说你的公义，时常赞美你。
chapters.en: And my tongue shall speak of thy righteousness of thy praise all the day long.

chapters.zh: （耶和华的仆人大卫的诗，交与伶长。）恶人的罪过在他心里说：我眼中不怕神！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, of David the servant of the LORD.] The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, no fear of God before his eyes.

chapters.zh: 他自夸自媚，以为他的罪孽终不显露，不被恨恶。
chapters.en: For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.

chapters.zh: 他口中的言语尽是罪孽诡诈；他与智慧善行已经断绝。
chapters.en: The words of his mouth iniquity and deceit: he hath left off to be wise, to do good.

chapters.zh: 他在床上图谋罪孽，定意行不善的道，不憎恶恶事。
chapters.en: He deviseth mischief upon his bed; he setteth himself in a way not good; he abhorreth not evil.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的慈爱上及诸天；你的信实达到穹苍。
chapters.en: Thy mercy, O LORD, in the heavens; thy faithfulness unto the clouds.

chapters.zh: 你的公义好像高山；你的判断如同深渊。耶和华啊，人民、牲畜，你都救护。
chapters.en: Thy righteousness like the great mountains; thy judgments a great deep: O LORD, thou preservest man and beast.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你的慈爱何其宝贵！世人投靠在你翅膀的荫下。
chapters.en: How excellent thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.

chapters.zh: 他们必因你殿里的肥甘得以饱足；你也必叫他们喝你乐河的水。
chapters.en: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou shalt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.

chapters.zh: 因为，在你那里有生命的源头；在你的光中，我们必得见光。
chapters.en: For with thee the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.

chapters.zh: 愿你常施慈爱给认识你的人，常以公义待心里正直的人。
chapters.en: O continue thy lovingkindness unto them that know thee; and thy righteousness to the upright in heart.

chapters.zh: 不容骄傲人的脚践踏我；不容凶恶人的手赶逐我。
chapters.en: Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remove me.

chapters.zh: 在那里，作孽的人已经仆倒；他们被推倒，不能再起来。
chapters.en: There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down, and shall not be able to rise.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）不要为作恶的心怀不平，也不要向那行不义的生出嫉妒。
chapters.en: [ of David.] Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.

chapters.zh: 因为他们如草快被割下，又如青菜快要枯干。
chapters.en: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.

chapters.zh: 你当倚靠耶和华而行善，住在地上，以他的信实为粮；
chapters.en: Trust in the LORD, and do good; shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.

chapters.zh: 又要以耶和华为乐，他就将你心里所求的赐给你。
chapters.en: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.

chapters.zh: 当将你的事交托耶和华，并倚靠他，他就必成全。
chapters.en: Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring to pass.

chapters.zh: 他要使你的公义如光发出，使你的公平明如正午。
chapters.en: And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.

chapters.zh: 你当默然倚靠耶和华，耐性等候他；不要因那道路通达的和那恶谋成就的心怀不平。
chapters.en: Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.

chapters.zh: 当止住怒气，离弃忿怒；不要心怀不平，以致作恶。
chapters.en: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.

chapters.zh: 因为作恶的必被剪除；惟有等候耶和华的必承受地土。
chapters.en: For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the LORD, they shall inherit the earth.

chapters.zh: 还有片时，恶人要归于无有；你就是细察他的住处也要归于无有。
chapters.en: For yet a little while, and the wicked not : yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it not .

chapters.zh: 但谦卑人必承受地土，以丰盛的平安为乐。
chapters.en: But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

chapters.zh: 恶人设谋害义人，又向他咬牙。
chapters.en: The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.

chapters.zh: 主要笑他，因见他受罚的日子将要来到。
chapters.en: The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.

chapters.zh: 恶人已经弓上弦，刀出鞘，要打倒困苦穷乏的人，要杀害行动正直的人。
chapters.en: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to slay such as be of upright conversation.

chapters.zh: 他们的刀必刺入自己的心；他们的弓必被折断。
