chapters.en: Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.

chapters.zh: 一个义人所有的虽少，强过许多恶人的富余。
chapters.en: A little that a righteous man hath better than the riches of many wicked.

chapters.zh: 因为恶人的膀臂必被折断；但耶和华是扶持义人。
chapters.en: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the LORD upholdeth the righteous.

chapters.zh: 耶和华知道完全人的日子；他们的产业要存到永远。
chapters.en: The LORD knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.

chapters.zh: 他们在急难的时候不致羞愧，在饥荒的日子必得饱足。
chapters.en: They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.

chapters.zh: 恶人却要灭亡。耶和华的仇敌要像羊羔的脂油（或译：像草地的华美）；他们要消灭，要如烟消灭。
chapters.en: But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.

chapters.zh: 恶人借贷而不偿还；义人却恩待人，并且施舍。
chapters.en: The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous sheweth mercy, and giveth.

chapters.zh: 蒙耶和华赐福的必承受地土；被他咒诅的必被剪除。
chapters.en: For blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and cursed of him shall be cut off.

chapters.zh: 义人的脚步被耶和华立定；他的道路，耶和华也喜爱。
chapters.en: The steps of a man are ordered by the LORD: and he delighteth in his way.

chapters.zh: 他虽失脚也不至全身仆倒，因为耶和华用手搀扶他（或译：搀扶他的手）。
chapters.en: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the LORD upholdeth his hand.

chapters.zh: 我从前年幼，现在年老，却未见过义人被弃，也未见过他的后裔讨饭。
chapters.en: I have been young, and am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.

chapters.zh: 他终日恩待人，借给人；他的后裔也蒙福！
chapters.en: ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed blessed.

chapters.zh: 你当离恶行善，就可永远安居。
chapters.en: Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.

chapters.zh: 因为，耶和华喜爱公平，不撇弃他的圣民；他们永蒙保佑，但恶人的后裔必被剪除。
chapters.en: For the LORD loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.

chapters.zh: 义人必承受地土，永居其上。
chapters.en: The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.

chapters.zh: 义人的口谈论智慧；他的舌头讲说公平。
chapters.en: The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.

chapters.zh: 神的律法在他心里；他的脚总不滑跌。
chapters.en: The law of his God in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.

chapters.zh: 恶人窥探义人，想要杀他。
chapters.en: The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必不撇他在恶人手中；当审判的时候，也不定他的罪。
chapters.en: The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

chapters.zh: 你当等候耶和华，遵守他的道，他就抬举你，使你承受地土；恶人被剪除的时候，你必看见。
chapters.en: Wait on the LORD, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see .

chapters.zh: 我见过恶人大有势力，好像一根青翠树在本土生发。
chapters.en: I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading himself like a green bay tree.

chapters.zh: 有人从那里经过，不料，他没有了；我也寻找他，却寻不著。
chapters.en: Yet he passed away, and, lo, he not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.

chapters.zh: 你要细察那完全人，观看那正直人，因为和平人有好结局。
chapters.en: Mark the perfect , and behold the upright: for the end of man peace.

chapters.zh: 至于犯法的人，必一同灭绝；恶人终必剪除。
chapters.en: But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.

chapters.zh: 但义人得救是由于耶和华；他在患难时作他们的营寨。
chapters.en: But the salvation of the righteous of the LORD: their strength in the time of trouble.

chapters.zh: 耶和华帮助他们，解救他们；他解救他们脱离恶人，把他们救出来，因为他们投靠他。
chapters.en: And the LORD shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.

chapters.zh: （大卫的记念诗。）耶和华啊，求你不要在怒中责备我，不要在烈怒中惩罚我！
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David, to bring to remembrance.] O LORD, rebuke me not in thy wrath: neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

chapters.zh: 因为，你的箭射入我身；你的手压住我。
chapters.en: For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore.

chapters.zh: 因你的恼怒，我的肉无一完全；因我的罪过，我的骨头也不安宁。
chapters.en: no soundness in my flesh because of thine anger; neither rest in my bones because of my sin.

chapters.zh: 我的罪孽高过我的头，如同重担叫我担当不起。
chapters.en: For mine iniquities are gone over mine head: as an heavy burden they are too heavy for me.

chapters.zh: 因我的愚昧，我的伤发臭流脓。
chapters.en: My wounds stink are corrupt because of my foolishness.

chapters.zh: 我疼痛，大大拳曲，终日哀痛。
chapters.en: I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.

chapters.zh: 我满腰是火；我的肉无一完全。
chapters.en: For my loins are filled with a loathsome : and no soundness in my flesh.

chapters.zh: 我被压伤，身体疲倦；因心里不安，我就唉哼。
chapters.en: I am feeble and sore broken: I have roared by reason of the disquietness of my heart.

chapters.zh: 主啊，我的心愿都在你面前；我的叹息不向你隐瞒。
chapters.en: Lord, all my desire before thee; and my groaning is not hid from thee.

chapters.zh: 我心跳动，我力衰微，连我眼中的光也没有了。
chapters.en: My heart panteth, my strength faileth me: as for the light of mine eyes, it also is gone from me.

chapters.zh: 我的良朋密友因我的灾病都躲在旁边站著；我的亲戚本家也远远地站立。
chapters.en: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my sore; and my kinsmen stand afar off.

chapters.zh: 那寻索我命的设下网罗；那想要害我的口出恶言，终日思想诡计。
chapters.en: They also that seek after my life lay snares : and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.

chapters.zh: 但我如聋子不听，像哑巴不开口。
chapters.en: But I, as a deaf , heard not; and as a dumb man openeth not his mouth.

chapters.zh: 我如不听见的人，口中没有回话。
chapters.en: Thus I was as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth no reproofs.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我仰望你！主 ─ 我的神啊，你必应允我！
chapters.en: For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.

chapters.zh: 我曾说：恐怕他们向我夸耀；我失脚的时候，他们向我夸大。
chapters.en: For I said, , lest they should rejoice over me: when my foot slippeth, they magnify against me.

chapters.zh: 我几乎跌倒；我的痛苦常在我面前。
chapters.en: For I ready to halt, and my sorrow continually before me.

chapters.zh: 我要承认我的罪孽；我要因我的罪忧愁。
chapters.en: For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.

chapters.zh: 但我的仇敌又活泼又强壮，无理恨我的增多了。
chapters.en: But mine enemies lively, they are strong: and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.

chapters.zh: 以恶报善的与我作对，因我是追求良善。
chapters.en: They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow good .

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你不要撇弃我！我的神啊，求你不要远离我！
chapters.en: Forsake me not, O LORD: O my God, be not far from me.

chapters.zh: 拯救我的主啊，求你快快帮助我！
chapters.en: Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长耶杜顿。）我曾说：我要谨慎我的言行，免得我舌头犯罪；恶人在我面前的时候，我要用嚼环勒住我的口。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David.] I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.

chapters.zh: 我默然无声，连好话也不出口；我的愁苦就发动了，
chapters.en: I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, from good; and my sorrow was stirred.

chapters.zh: 我的心在我里面发热。我默想的时候，火就烧起，我便用舌头说话。
chapters.en: My heart was hot within me, while I was musing the fire burned: spake I with my tongue,

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你叫我晓得我身之终！我的寿数几何？叫我知道我的生命不长！
chapters.en: LORD, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it I may know how frail I .

chapters.zh: 你使我的年日窄如手掌；我一生的年数，在你面前如同无有。各人最稳妥的时候，真是全然虚幻。（细拉）
chapters.en: Behold, thou hast made my days an handbreadth; and mine age as nothing before thee: verily every man at his best state altogether vanity. Selah.

chapters.zh: 世人行动实系幻影。他们忙乱，真是枉然；积蓄财宝，不知将来有谁收取。
chapters.en: Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up , and knoweth not who shall gather them.

chapters.zh: 主啊，如今我等甚么呢？我的指望在乎你！
chapters.en: And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope in thee.

chapters.zh: 求你救我脱离一切的过犯，不要使我受愚顽人的羞辱。
chapters.en: Deliver me from all my transgressions: make me not the reproach of the foolish.

chapters.zh: 因我所遭遇的是出于你，我就默然不语。
chapters.en: I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst .

chapters.zh: 求你把你的责罚从我身上免去；因你手的责打，我便消灭。
chapters.en: Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.

chapters.zh: 你因人的罪恶惩罚他的时候，叫他的笑容（的笑容：或译所喜爱的）消灭，如衣被虫所咬。世人真是虚幻！（细拉）
chapters.en: When thou with rebukes dost correct man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely every man vanity. Selah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你听我的祷告，留心听我的呼求！我流泪，求你不要静默无声！因为我在你面前是客旅，是寄居的，像我列祖一般。
chapters.en: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I a stranger with thee, a sojourner, as all my fathers .

chapters.zh: 求你宽容我，使我在去而不返之先可以力量复原。
chapters.en: O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）我曾耐性等候耶和华；他垂听我的呼求。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] I waited patiently for the LORD; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.

chapters.zh: 他从祸坑里，从淤泥中，把我拉上来，使我的脚立在磐石上，使我脚步稳当。
chapters.en: He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, established my goings.

chapters.zh: 他使我口唱新歌，就是赞美我们神的话。许多人必看见而惧怕，并要倚靠耶和华。
chapters.en: And he hath put a new song in my mouth, praise unto our God: many shall see , and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.

chapters.zh: 那倚靠耶和华、不理会狂傲和偏向虚假之辈的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: Blessed that man that maketh the LORD his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，你所行的奇事，并你向我们所怀的意念甚多，不能向你陈明。若要陈明，其事不可胜数。
chapters.en: Many, O LORD my God, thy wonderful works thou hast done, and thy thoughts to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: I would declare and speak , they are more than can be numbered.

chapters.zh: 祭物和礼物，你不喜悦；你已经开通我的耳朵。燔祭和赎罪祭非你所要。
chapters.en: Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.

chapters.zh: 那时我说：看哪，我来了！我的事在经卷上已经记载了。
chapters.en: Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book written of me,

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，我乐意照你的旨意行；你的律法在我心里。
chapters.en: I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law within my heart.

chapters.zh: 我在大会中宣传公义的佳音；我必不止住我的嘴唇。耶和华啊，这是你所知道的。
chapters.en: I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O LORD, thou knowest.

chapters.zh: 我未曾把你的公义藏在心里；我已陈明你的信实和你的救恩；我在大会中未曾隐瞒你的慈爱和诚实。
chapters.en: I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy lovingkindness and thy truth from the great congregation.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你不要向我止住你的慈悲！愿你的慈爱和诚实常常保佑我！
chapters.en: Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O LORD: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.

chapters.zh: 因有无数的祸患围困我，我的罪孽追上了我，使我不能昂首；这罪孽比我的头发还多，我就心寒胆战。
chapters.en: For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你开恩搭救我！耶和华啊，求你速速帮助我！
chapters.en: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me: O LORD, make haste to help me.

chapters.zh: 愿那些寻找我、要灭我命的，一同抱愧蒙羞！愿那些喜悦我受害的，退后受辱！
chapters.en: Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.

chapters.zh: 愿那些对我说阿哈、阿哈的，因羞愧而败亡！
chapters.en: Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.

chapters.zh: 愿一切寻求你的，因你高兴欢喜！愿那些喜爱你救恩的，常说：当尊耶和华为大！
chapters.en: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The LORD be magnified.

chapters.zh: 但我是困苦穷乏的，主仍顾念我；你是帮助我的，搭救我的。神啊，求你不要耽延！
chapters.en: But I poor and needy; the Lord thinketh upon me: thou my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）眷顾贫穷的有福了！他遭难的日子，耶和华必搭救他。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] Blessed he that considereth the poor: the LORD will deliver him in time of trouble.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必保全他，使他存活；他必在地上享福。求你不要把他交给仇敌，遂其所愿。
chapters.en: The LORD will preserve him, and keep him alive; he shall be blessed upon the earth: and thou wilt not deliver him unto the will of his enemies.

chapters.zh: 他病重在榻，耶和华必扶持他；他在病中，你必给他铺床。
chapters.en: The LORD will strengthen him upon the bed of languishing: thou wilt make all his bed in his sickness.

chapters.zh: 我曾说：耶和华啊，求你怜恤我，医治我！因为我得罪了你。
chapters.en: I said, LORD, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.

chapters.zh: 我的仇敌用恶言议论我说：他几时死，他的名才灭亡呢？
chapters.en: Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?

chapters.zh: 他来看我就说假话；他心存奸恶，走到外边才说出来。
chapters.en: And if he come to see , he speaketh vanity: his heart gathereth iniquity to itself; he goeth abroad, he telleth .

chapters.zh: 一切恨我的，都交头接耳地议论我；他们设计要害我。
chapters.en: All that hate me whisper together against me: against me do they devise my hurt.

chapters.zh: 他们说：有怪病贴在他身上；他已躺卧，必不能再起来。
chapters.en: An evil disease, , cleaveth fast unto him: and that he lieth he shall rise up no more.

chapters.zh: 连我知己的朋友，我所倚靠、吃过我饭的也用脚踢我。
chapters.en: Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up heel against me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你怜恤我，使我起来，好报复他们！
chapters.en: But thou, O LORD, be merciful unto me, and raise me up, that I may requite them.

chapters.zh: 因我的仇敌不得向我夸胜，我从此便知道你喜爱我。
chapters.en: By this I know that thou favourest me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.

chapters.zh: 你因我纯正就扶持我，使我永远站在你的面前。
chapters.en: And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 以色列的神是应当称颂的，从亘古直到永远。阿们！阿们！
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的训诲诗，交与伶长。）神啊，我的心切慕你，如鹿切慕溪水。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Maschil, for the sons of Korah.] As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.

chapters.zh: 我的心渴想神，就是永生神；我几时得朝见神呢？
chapters.en: My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?

chapters.zh: 我昼夜以眼泪当饮食；人不住地对我说：你的神在哪里呢？
chapters.en: My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where thy God?

chapters.zh: 我从前与众人同往，用欢呼称赞的声音领他们到神的殿里，大家守节。我追想这些事，我的心极其悲伤。
chapters.en: When I remember these , I pour out my soul in me: for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, with a multitude that kept holyday.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你为何忧闷？为何在我里面烦躁？应当仰望神，因他笑脸帮助我；我还要称赞他。
chapters.en: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him the help of his countenance.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，我的心在我里面忧闷，所以我从约但地，从黑门岭，从米萨山记念你。
chapters.en: O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.

chapters.zh: 你的瀑布发声，深渊就与深渊响应；你的波浪洪涛漫过我身。
chapters.en: Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy waterspouts: all thy waves and thy billows are gone over me.

chapters.zh: 白昼，耶和华必向我施慈爱；黑夜，我要歌颂祷告赐我生命的神。
chapters.en: the LORD will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song with me, my prayer unto the God of my life.

chapters.zh: 我要对神 ─ 我的磐石说：你为何忘记我呢？我为何因仇敌的欺压时常哀痛呢？
chapters.en: I will say unto God my rock, Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

chapters.zh: 我的敌人辱骂我，好像打碎我的骨头，不住地对我说：你的神在哪里呢？
chapters.en: with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where thy God?

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你为何忧闷？为何在我里面烦躁？应当仰望神，因我还要称赞他。他是我脸上的光荣（原文是帮助），是我的神。
chapters.en: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, the health of my countenance, and my God.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你伸我的冤，向不虔诚的国为我辨屈；求你救我脱离诡诈不义的人。
chapters.en: Judge me, O God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation: O deliver me from the deceitful and unjust man.

chapters.zh: 因为你是赐我力量的神，为何丢弃我呢？我为何因仇敌的欺压时常哀痛呢？
chapters.en: For thou the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

chapters.zh: 求你发出你的亮光和真实，好引导我，带我到你的圣山，到你的居所！
chapters.en: O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me; let them bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy tabernacles.

chapters.zh: 我就走到神的祭坛，到我最喜乐的神那里。神啊，我的神，我要弹琴称赞你！
chapters.en: Then will I go unto the altar of God, unto God my exceeding joy: yea, upon the harp will I praise thee, O God my God.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你为何忧闷？为何在我里面烦躁？应当仰望神，因我还要称赞他。他是我脸上的光荣（原文是帮助），是我的神。
chapters.en: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope in God: for I shall yet praise him, the health of my countenance, and my God.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的训诲诗，交与伶长。）神啊，你在古时，我们列祖的日子所行的事，我们亲耳听见了；我们的列祖也给我们述说过。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician for the sons of Korah, Maschil.] We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.

chapters.zh: 你曾用手赶出外邦人，却栽培了我们列祖；你苦待列邦，却叫我们列祖发达。
chapters.en: thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.

chapters.zh: 因为他们不是靠自己的刀剑得地土，也不是靠自己的膀臂得胜，乃是靠你的右手、你的膀臂，和你脸上的亮光，因为你喜悦他们。
chapters.en: For they got not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them: but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto them.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你是我的王；求你出令使雅各得胜。
chapters.en: Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.

chapters.zh: 我们靠你要推倒我们的敌人，靠你的名要践踏那起来攻击我们的人。
chapters.en: Through thee will we push down our enemies: through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.

chapters.zh: 因为，我必不靠我的弓；我的刀也不能使我得胜。
chapters.en: For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.

chapters.zh: 惟你救了我们脱离敌人，使恨我们的人羞愧。
chapters.en: But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.

chapters.zh: 我们终日因神夸耀，还要永远称谢你的名。（细拉）
chapters.en: In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.

chapters.zh: 但如今你丢弃了我们，使我们受辱，不和我们的军兵同去。
chapters.en: But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.

chapters.zh: 你使我们向敌人转身退后；那恨我们的人任意抢夺。
chapters.en: Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.

chapters.zh: 你使我们当作快要被吃的羊，把我们分散在列邦中。
chapters.en: Thou hast given us like sheep for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.

chapters.zh: 你卖了你的子民也不赚利，所得的价值并不加添你的资财。
chapters.en: Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase by their price.

chapters.zh: 你使我们受邻国的羞辱，被四围的人嗤笑讥刺。
chapters.en: Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.

chapters.zh: 你使我们在列邦中作了笑谈，使众民向我们摇头。
chapters.en: Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.

chapters.zh: 我的凌辱终日在我面前，我脸上的羞愧将我遮蔽，
chapters.en: My confusion continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,

chapters.zh: 都因那辱骂毁谤人的声音，又因仇敌和报仇人的缘故。
chapters.en: For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.

chapters.zh: 这都临到我们身上，我们却没有忘记你，也没有违背你的约。
chapters.en: All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.

chapters.zh: 我们的心没有退后；我们的脚也没有偏离你的路。
chapters.en: Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;

chapters.zh: 你在野狗之处压伤我们，用死荫遮蔽我们。
chapters.en: Though thou hast sore broken us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.

chapters.zh: 倘若我们忘了神的名，或向别神举手，
chapters.en: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;

chapters.zh: 神岂不鉴察这事么？因为他晓得人心里的隐秘。
chapters.en: Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.

chapters.zh: 我们为你的缘故终日被杀；人看我们如将宰的羊。
chapters.en: Yea, for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你睡醒，为何尽睡呢？求你兴起，不要永远丢弃我们！
chapters.en: Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast not off for ever.

chapters.zh: 你为何掩面，不顾我们所遭的苦难和所受的欺压？
chapters.en: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, forgettest our affliction and our oppression?

chapters.zh: 我们的性命伏于尘土；我们的肚腹紧贴地面。
chapters.en: For our soul is bowed down to the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the earth.

chapters.zh: 求你起来帮助我们！凭你的慈爱救赎我们！
chapters.en: Arise for our help, and redeem us for thy mercies' sake.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的训诲诗，又是爱慕歌，交与伶长。调用百合花。）我心里涌出美辞；我论到我为王做的事，我的舌头是快手笔。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Shoshannim, for the sons of Korah, Maschil, A Song of loves.] My heart is inditing a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made touching the king: my tongue the pen of a ready writer.

chapters.zh: 你比世人更美；在你嘴里满有恩惠；所以神赐福给你，直到永远。
chapters.en: Thou art fairer than the children of men: grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.

chapters.zh: 大能者啊，愿你腰间佩刀，大有荣耀和威严！
chapters.en: Gird thy sword upon thigh, O mighty, with thy glory and thy majesty.

chapters.zh: 为真理、谦卑、公义赫然坐车前往，无不得胜；你的右手必显明可畏的事。
chapters.en: And in thy majesty ride prosperously because of truth and meekness righteousness; and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.

chapters.zh: 你的箭锋快，射中王敌之心；万民仆倒在你以下。
chapters.en: Thine arrows sharp in the heart of the king's enemies; the people fall under thee.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你的宝座是永永远远的；你的国权是正直的。
chapters.en: Thy throne, O God, for ever and ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom a right sceptre.

chapters.zh: 你喜爱公义，恨恶罪恶；所以神 ─ 就是你的神 ─ 用喜乐油膏你，胜过膏你的同伴。
chapters.en: Thou lovest righteousness, and hatest wickedness: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

chapters.zh: 你的衣服都有没药、沉香、肉桂的香气；象牙宫中有丝弦乐器的声音使你欢喜。
chapters.en: All thy garments of myrrh, and aloes, cassia, out of the ivory palaces, whereby they have made thee glad.

chapters.zh: 有君王的女儿在你尊贵妇女之中；王后佩戴俄斐金饰站在你右边。
chapters.en: Kings' daughters among thy honourable women: upon thy right hand did stand the queen in gold of Ophir.

chapters.zh: 女子啊，你要听，要想，要侧耳而听！不要记念你的民和你的父家，
chapters.en: Hearken, O daughter, and consider, and incline thine ear; forget also thine own people, and thy father's house;

chapters.zh: 王就羡慕你的美貌；因为他是你的主，你当敬拜他。
chapters.en: So shall the king greatly desire thy beauty: for he thy Lord; and worship thou him.

chapters.zh: 推罗的民（原文是女子）必来送礼；民中的富足人也必向你求恩。
chapters.en: And the daughter of Tyre with a gift; the rich among the people shall intreat thy favour.

chapters.zh: 王女在宫里极其荣华；他的衣服是用金线绣的。
chapters.en: The king's daughter all glorious within: her clothing of wrought gold.

chapters.zh: 他要穿锦绣的衣服，被引到王前；随从他的陪伴童女也要被带到你面前。
chapters.en: She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of needlework: the virgins her companions that follow her shall be brought unto thee.

chapters.zh: 他们要欢喜快乐被引导；他们要进入王宫。
chapters.en: With gladness and rejoicing shall they be brought: they shall enter into the king's palace.

chapters.zh: 你的子孙要接续你的列祖；你要立他们在全地作王。
chapters.en: Instead of thy fathers shall be thy children, whom thou mayest make princes in all the earth.

chapters.zh: 我必叫你的名被万代记念，所以万民要永永远远称谢你。
chapters.en: I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗歌，交与伶长。调用女音。）神是我们的避难所，是我们的力量，是我们在患难中随时的帮助。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician for the sons of Korah, A Song upon Alamoth.] God our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

chapters.zh: 所以，地虽改变，山虽摇动到海心，
chapters.en: Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;

chapters.zh: 其中的水虽匉訇翻腾，山虽因海涨而战抖，我们也不害怕。（细拉）
chapters.en: the waters thereof roar be troubled, the mountains shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.

chapters.zh: 有一道河，这河的分使神的城欢喜；这城就是至高者居住的圣所。
chapters.en: a river, the streams whereof shall make glad the city of God, the holy of the tabernacles of the most High.

chapters.zh: 神在其中，城必不动摇；到天一亮，神必帮助这城。
chapters.en: God in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, right early.

chapters.zh: 外邦喧嚷，列国动摇；神发声，地便镕化。
chapters.en: The heathen raged, the kingdoms were moved: he uttered his voice, the earth melted.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华与我们同在；雅各的神是我们的避难所！（细拉）
chapters.en: The LORD of hosts with us; the God of Jacob our refuge. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你们来看耶和华的作为，看他使地怎样荒凉。
chapters.en: Come, behold the works of the LORD, what desolations he hath made in the earth.

chapters.zh: 他止息刀兵，直到地极；他折弓、断枪，把战车焚烧在火中。
chapters.en: He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he breaketh the bow, and cutteth the spear in sunder; he burneth the chariot in the fire.

chapters.zh: 你们要休息，要知道我是神！我必在外邦中被尊崇，在遍地上也被尊崇。
chapters.en: Be still, and know that I God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华与我们同在；雅各的神是我们的避难所！
chapters.en: The LORD of hosts with us; the God of Jacob our refuge. Selah.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗，交与伶长。）万民哪，你们都要拍掌！要用夸胜的声音向神呼喊！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah.] O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华至高者是可畏的；他是治理全地的大君王。
chapters.en: For the LORD most high terrible; a great King over all the earth.

chapters.zh: 他叫万民服在我们以下，又叫列邦服在我们脚下。
chapters.en: He shall subdue the people under us, and the nations under our feet.

chapters.zh: 他为我们选择产业，就是他所爱之雅各的荣耀。（细拉）
chapters.en: He shall choose our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神上升，有喊声相送；耶和华上升，有角声相送。
chapters.en: God is gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.

chapters.zh: 你们要向神歌颂，歌颂！向我们王歌颂，歌颂！
chapters.en: Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.

chapters.zh: 因为神是全地的王；你们要用悟性歌颂。
chapters.en: For God the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.

chapters.zh: 神作王治理万国；神坐在他的圣宝座上。
chapters.en: God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.

chapters.zh: 列邦的君王聚集要作亚伯拉罕之神的民。因为世界的盾牌是属神的；他为至高！
chapters.en: The princes of the people are gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth unto God: he is greatly exalted.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗歌。）耶和华本为大！在我们神的城中，在他的圣山上，该受大赞美。
chapters.en: [A Song Psalm for the sons of Korah.] Great the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, the mountain of his holiness.

chapters.zh: 锡安山 ─ 大君王的城，在北面居高华美，为全地所喜悦。
chapters.en: Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, mount Zion, the sides of the north, the city of the great King.

chapters.zh: 神在其宫中，自显为避难所。
chapters.en: God is known in her palaces for a refuge.

chapters.zh: 看哪，众王会合，一同经过。
chapters.en: For, lo, the kings were assembled, they passed by together.

chapters.zh: 他们见了这城就惊奇丧胆，急忙逃跑。
chapters.en: They saw so they marvelled; they were troubled, hasted away.

chapters.zh: 他们在那里被战兢疼痛抓住，好像产难的妇人一样。
chapters.en: Fear took hold upon them there, pain, as of a woman in travail.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你用东风打破他施的船只。
chapters.en: Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish with an east wind.

chapters.zh: 我们在万军之耶和华的城中 ─ 就是我们神的城中 ─ 所看见的，正如我们所听见的。神必坚立这城，直到永远。（细拉）
chapters.en: As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我们在你的殿中想念你的慈爱。
chapters.en: We have thought of thy lovingkindness, O God, in the midst of thy temple.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你受的赞美正与你的名相称，直到地极！你的右手满了公义。
chapters.en: According to thy name, O God, so thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.

chapters.zh: 因你的判断，锡安山应当欢喜，犹大的城邑（原文是女子）应当快乐。
chapters.en: Let mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 你们当周游锡安，四围旋绕，数点城楼，
chapters.en: Walk about Zion, and go round about her: tell the towers thereof.

chapters.zh: 细看他的外郭，察看他的宫殿，为要传说到后代。
chapters.en: Mark ye well her bulwarks, consider her palaces; that ye may tell to the generation following.

chapters.zh: 因为这神永永远远为我们的神；他必作我们引路的，直到死时。
chapters.en: For this God our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide unto death.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗，交与伶长。）万民哪，你们都当听这话！世上一切的居民，
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah.] Hear this, all people; give ear, all inhabitants of the world:

chapters.zh: 无论上流下流，富足贫穷，都当留心听！
chapters.en: Both low and high, rich and poor, together.

chapters.zh: 我口要说智慧的言语；我心要想通达的道理。
chapters.en: My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart of understanding.

chapters.zh: 我要侧耳听比喻，用琴解谜语。
chapters.en: I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.

chapters.zh: 在患难的日子，奸恶随我脚跟，四面环绕我，我何必惧怕？
chapters.en: Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?

chapters.zh: 那些倚仗财货自夸钱财多的人，
chapters.en: They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;

chapters.zh: 一个也无法赎自己的弟兄，也不能替他将赎价给神，
chapters.en: None can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:

chapters.zh: 叫他长远活著，不见朽坏；因为赎他生命的价值极贵，只可永远罢休。
chapters.en: (For the redemption of their soul precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: That he should still live for ever, not see corruption.

chapters.zh: 他必见智慧人死，又见愚顽人和畜类人一同灭亡，将他们的财货留给别人。
chapters.en: For he seeth wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.

chapters.zh: 他们心里思想：他们的家室必永存，住宅必留到万代；他们以自己的名称自己的地。
chapters.en: Their inward thought their houses for ever, their dwelling places to all generations; they call lands after their own names.

chapters.zh: 但人居尊贵中不能长久，如同死亡的畜类一样。
chapters.en: Nevertheless man in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts perish.

chapters.zh: 他们行的这道本为自己的愚昧；但他们以后的人还佩服他们的话语。（细拉）
chapters.en: This their way their folly: yet their posterity approve their sayings. Selah.

chapters.zh: 他们如同羊群派定下阴间；死亡必作他们的牧者。到了早晨，正直人必管辖他们；他们的美容必被阴间所灭，以致无处可存。
chapters.en: Like sheep they are laid in the grave; death shall feed on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their beauty shall consume in the grave from their dwelling.

chapters.zh: 只是神必救赎我的灵魂脱离阴间的权柄，因他必收纳我。（细拉）
chapters.en: But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me. Selah.

chapters.zh: 见人发财、家室增荣的时候，你不要惧怕；
chapters.en: Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;

chapters.zh: 因为，他死的时候甚么也不能带去；他的荣耀不能随他下去。
chapters.en: For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.

chapters.zh: 他活著的时候，虽然自夸为有福（你若利己，人必夸奖你）；
chapters.en: Though while he lived he blessed his soul: and will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself.

chapters.zh: 他仍必归到他历代的祖宗那里，永不见光。
chapters.en: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.

chapters.zh: 人在尊贵中而不醒悟，就如死亡的畜类一样。
chapters.en: Man in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts perish.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗。）大能者神 ─ 耶和华已经发言招呼天下，从日出之地到日落之处。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of Asaph.] The mighty God, the LORD, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.

chapters.zh: 从全美的锡安中，神已经发光了。
chapters.en: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God hath shined.

chapters.zh: 我们的神要来，决不闭口。有烈火在他面前吞灭；有暴风在他四围大刮。
chapters.en: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.

chapters.zh: 他招呼上天下地，为要审判他的民，
chapters.en: He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people.

chapters.zh: 说：招聚我的圣民到我这里来，就是那些用祭物与我立约的人。
chapters.en: Gather my saints together unto me; those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.

chapters.zh: 诸天必表明他的公义，因为神是施行审判的。（细拉）
chapters.en: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God judge himself. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我的民哪，你们当听我的话！以色列啊，我要劝戒你；我是神，是你的神！
chapters.en: Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against thee: I God, thy God.

chapters.zh: 我并不因你的祭物责备你；你的燔祭常在我面前。
chapters.en: I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices or thy burnt offerings, continually before me.

chapters.zh: 我不从你家中取公牛，也不从你圈内取山羊；
chapters.en: I will take no bullock out of thy house, he goats out of thy folds.

chapters.zh: 因为，树林中的百兽是我的，千山上的牲畜也是我的。
chapters.en: For every beast of the forest mine, the cattle upon a thousand hills.

chapters.zh: 山中的飞鸟，我都知道；野地的走兽也都属我。
chapters.en: I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field mine.

chapters.zh: 我若是饥饿，我不用告诉你，因为世界和其中所充满的都是我的。
chapters.en: If I were hungry, I would not tell thee: for the world mine, and the fulness thereof.

chapters.zh: 我岂吃公牛的肉呢？我岂喝山羊的血呢？
chapters.en: Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?

chapters.zh: 你们要以感谢为祭献与神，又要向至高者还你的愿，
chapters.en: Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:

chapters.zh: 并要在患难之日求告我；我必搭救你，你也要荣耀我。
chapters.en: And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.

chapters.zh: 但神对恶人说：你怎敢传说我的律例，口中提到我的约呢？
chapters.en: But unto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or thou shouldest take my covenant in thy mouth?

chapters.zh: 其实你恨恶管教，将我的言语丢在背后。
chapters.en: Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.

chapters.zh: 你见了盗贼就乐意与他同伙，又与行奸淫的人一同有分。
chapters.en: When thou sawest a thief, then thou consentedst with him, and hast been partaker with adulterers.

chapters.zh: 你口任说恶言；你舌编造诡诈。
chapters.en: Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.

chapters.zh: 你坐著毁谤你的兄弟，谗毁你亲母的儿子。
chapters.en: Thou sittest speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine own mother's son.

chapters.zh: 你行了这些事，我还闭口不言，你想我恰和你一样；其实我要责备你，将这些事摆在你眼前。
chapters.en: These hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether as thyself: I will reprove thee, and set in order before thine eyes.

chapters.zh: 你们忘记神的，要思想这事，免得我把你们撕碎，无人搭救。
chapters.en: Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I tear in pieces, and none to deliver.

chapters.zh: 凡以感谢献上为祭的便是荣耀我；那按正路而行的，我必使他得著我的救恩。
chapters.en: Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth conversation will I shew the salvation of God.

chapters.zh: （大卫与拔示巴同室以后，先知拿单来见他；他作这诗，交与伶长。）神啊，求你按你的慈爱怜恤我！按你丰盛的慈悲涂抹我的过犯！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came unto him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba.] Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.

chapters.zh: 求你将我的罪孽洗除净尽，并洁除我的罪！
chapters.en: Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.

chapters.zh: 因为，我知道我的过犯；我的罪常在我面前。
chapters.en: For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin ever before me.

chapters.zh: 我向你犯罪，惟独得罪了你；在你眼前行了这恶，以致你责备我的时候显为公义，判断我的时候显为清正。
chapters.en: Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, be clear when thou judgest.

chapters.zh: 我是在罪孽里生的，在我母亲怀胎的时候就有了罪。
chapters.en: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.

chapters.zh: 你所喜爱的是内里诚实；你在我隐密处，必使我得智慧。
chapters.en: Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden thou shalt make me to know wisdom.

chapters.zh: 求你用牛膝草洁净我，我就干净；求你洗涤我，我就比雪更白。
chapters.en: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.

chapters.zh: 求你使我得听欢喜快乐的声音，使你所压伤的骨头可以踊跃。
chapters.en: Make me to hear joy and gladness; the bones thou hast broken may rejoice.

chapters.zh: 求你掩面不看我的罪，涂抹我一切的罪孽。
chapters.en: Hide thy face from my sins, and blot out all mine iniquities.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你为我造清洁的心，使我里面重新有正直（或译：坚定）的灵。
chapters.en: Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.

chapters.zh: 不要丢弃我，使我离开你的面；不要从我收回你的圣灵。
chapters.en: Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy spirit from me.

chapters.zh: 求你使我仍得救恩之乐，赐我乐意的灵扶持我，
chapters.en: Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation; and uphold me free spirit.

chapters.zh: 我就把你的道指教有过犯的人，罪人必归顺你。
chapters.en: will I teach transgressors thy ways; and sinners shall be converted unto thee.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你是拯救我的神；求你救我脱离流人血的罪！我的舌头就高声歌唱你的公义。
chapters.en: Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你使我嘴唇张开，我的口便传扬赞美你的话！
chapters.en: O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.

chapters.zh: 你本不喜爱祭物，若喜爱，我就献上；燔祭，你也不喜悦。
chapters.en: For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give : thou delightest not in burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 神所要的祭就是忧伤的灵；神啊，忧伤痛悔的心，你必不轻看。
chapters.en: The sacrifices of God a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.

chapters.zh: 求你随你的美意善待锡安，建造耶路撒冷的城墙。
chapters.en: Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion: build thou the walls of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 那时，你必喜爱公义的祭和燔祭并全牲的燔祭；那时，人必将公牛献在你坛上。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousness, with burnt offering and whole burnt offering: then shall they offer bullocks upon thine altar.

chapters.zh: （以东人多益来告诉扫罗说：大卫到了亚希米勒家。那时，大卫作这训诲诗，交与伶长。）勇士啊，你为何以作恶自夸？神的慈爱是常存的。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Maschil, of David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, and said unto him, David is come to the house of Ahimelech.] Why boastest thou thyself in mischief, O mighty man? the goodness of God continually.

chapters.zh: 你的舌头邪恶诡诈，好像剃头刀，快利伤人。
chapters.en: Thy tongue deviseth mischiefs; like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.

chapters.zh: 你爱恶胜似爱善，又爱说谎，不爱说公义。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou lovest evil more than good; lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.

chapters.zh: 诡诈的舌头啊，你爱说一切毁灭的话！
chapters.en: Thou lovest all devouring words, O deceitful tongue.

chapters.zh: 神也要毁灭你，直到永远；他要把你拿去，从你的帐棚中抽出，从活人之地将你拔出。（细拉）
chapters.en: God shall likewise destroy thee for ever, he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of dwelling place, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.

chapters.zh: 义人要看见而惧怕，并要笑他，
chapters.en: The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:

chapters.zh: 说：看哪，这就是那不以神为他力量的人，只倚仗他丰富的财物，在邪恶上坚立自己。
chapters.en: Lo, the man made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, strengthened himself in his wickedness.

chapters.zh: 至于我，就像神殿中的青橄榄树；我永永远远倚靠神的慈爱。
chapters.en: But I like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 我要称谢你，直到永远，因为你行了这事。我也要在你圣民面前仰望你的名；这名本为美好。
chapters.en: I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done : and I will wait on thy name; for good before thy saints.

chapters.zh: （大卫的训诲诗，交与伶长。调用麻哈拉。）愚顽人心里说：没有神。他们都是邪恶，行了可憎恶的罪孽；没有一个人行善。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Mahalath, Maschil, of David.] The fool hath said in his heart, no God. Corrupt are they, and have done abominable iniquity: none that doeth good.

chapters.zh: 神从天上垂看世人，要看有明白的没有？有寻求他的没有？
chapters.en: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were that did understand, that did seek God.

chapters.zh: 他们各人都退后，一同变为污秽；并没有行善的，连一个也没有。
chapters.en: Every one of them is gone back: they are altogether become filthy; none that doeth good, no, not one.

chapters.zh: 作孽的没有知识么？他们吞吃我的百姓如同吃饭一样，并不求告神。
chapters.en: Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people they eat bread: they have not called upon God.

chapters.zh: 他们在无可惧怕之处就大大害怕，因为神把那安营攻击你之人的骨头散开了。你使他们蒙羞，因为神弃绝了他们。
chapters.en: There were they in great fear, no fear was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth thee: thou hast put to shame, because God hath despised them.

chapters.zh: 但愿以色列的救恩从锡安而出。神救回他被掳的子民那时，雅各要快乐，以色列要欢喜。
chapters.en: Oh that the salvation of Israel out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, Israel shall be glad.

chapters.zh: （西弗人来对扫罗说：大卫岂不是在我们那里藏身么？那时，大卫作这训诲诗，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）神啊，求你以你的名救我，凭你的大能为我伸冤。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, of David, when the Ziphims came and said to Saul, Doth not David hide himself with us?] Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你听我的祷告，留心听我口中的言语。
chapters.en: Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.

chapters.zh: 因为，外人起来攻击我，强暴人寻索我的命；他们眼中没有神。（细拉）
chapters.en: For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神是帮助我的，是扶持我命的。
chapters.en: Behold, God mine helper: the Lord with them that uphold my soul.

chapters.zh: 他要报应我仇敌所行的恶；求你凭你的诚实灭绝他们。
chapters.en: He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.

chapters.zh: 我要把甘心祭献给你。耶和华啊，我要称赞你的名；这名本为美好。
chapters.en: I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O LORD; for good.

chapters.zh: 他从一切的急难中把我救出来；我的眼睛也看见了我仇敌遭报。
chapters.en: For he hath delivered me out of all trouble: and mine eye hath seen upon mine enemies.

chapters.zh: （大卫的训诲诗，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）神啊，求你留心听我的祷告，不要隐藏不听我的恳求！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth, Maschil, of David.] Give ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.

chapters.zh: 求你侧耳听我，应允我。我哀叹不安，发声唉哼，
chapters.en: Attend unto me, and hear me: I mourn in my complaint, and make a noise;

chapters.zh: 都因仇敌的声音，恶人的欺压；因为他们将罪孽加在我身上，发怒气逼迫我。
chapters.en: Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.

chapters.zh: 我心在我里面甚是疼痛；死的惊惶临到我身。
chapters.en: My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.

chapters.zh: 恐惧战兢归到我身；惊恐漫过了我。
chapters.en: Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.

chapters.zh: 我说：但愿我有翅膀像鸽子，我就飞去，得享安息。
chapters.en: And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! would I fly away, and be at rest.

chapters.zh: 我必远游，宿在旷野。（细拉）
chapters.en: Lo, would I wander far off, remain in the wilderness. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我必速速逃到避所，脱离狂风暴雨。
chapters.en: I would hasten my escape from the windy storm tempest.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你吞灭他们，变乱他们的舌头！因为我在城中见了强暴争竞的事。
chapters.en: Destroy, O Lord, divide their tongues: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

chapters.zh: 他们在城墙上昼夜绕行；在城内也有罪孽和奸恶。
chapters.en: Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof: mischief also and sorrow in the midst of it.

chapters.zh: 邪恶在其中；欺压和诡诈不离街市。
chapters.en: Wickedness in the midst thereof: deceit and guile depart not from her streets.

chapters.zh: 原来不是仇敌辱骂我，若是仇敌，还可忍耐；也不是恨我的人向我狂大，若是恨我的人就必躲避他。
chapters.en: For not an enemy reproached me; then I could have borne : neither he that hated me did magnify against me; then I would have hid myself from him:

chapters.zh: 不料是你；你原与我平等，是我的同伴，是我知己的朋友！
chapters.en: But thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.

chapters.zh: 我们素常彼此谈论，以为甘甜；我们与群众在神的殿中同行。
chapters.en: We took sweet counsel together, walked unto the house of God in company.

chapters.zh: 愿死亡忽然临到他们！愿他们活活地下入阴间！因为他们的住处，他们的心中，都是邪恶。
chapters.en: Let death seize upon them, let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness in their dwellings, among them.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我要求告神；耶和华必拯救我。
chapters.en: As for me, I will call upon God; and the LORD shall save me.

chapters.zh: 我要晚上、早晨、晌午哀声悲叹；他也必听我的声音。
chapters.en: Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray, and cry aloud: and he shall hear my voice.

chapters.zh: 他救赎我命脱离攻击我的人，使我得享平安，因为与我相争的人甚多。
chapters.en: He hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle against me: for there were many with me.

chapters.zh: 那没有更变、不敬畏神的人，从太古常存的神必听见而苦待他。
chapters.en: God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.

chapters.zh: 他背了约，伸手攻击与他和好的人。
chapters.en: He hath put forth his hands against such as be at peace with him: he hath broken his covenant.

chapters.zh: 他的口如奶油光滑，他的心却怀著争战；他的话比油柔和，其实是拔出来的刀。
chapters.en: of his mouth were smoother than butter, but war in his heart: his words were softer than oil, yet they drawn swords.

chapters.zh: 你要把你的重担卸给耶和华，他必抚养你；他永不叫义人动摇。
chapters.en: Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你必使恶人下入灭亡的坑；流人血、行诡诈的人必活不到半世，但我要倚靠你。
chapters.en: But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; but I will trust in thee.

chapters.zh: （非利士人在迦特拿住大卫。那时，他作这金诗，交与伶长。调用远方无声鸽。）神啊，求你怜悯我，因为人要把我吞了，终日攻击欺压我。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Jonathelemrechokim, Michtam of David, when the Philistines took him in Gath.] Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.

chapters.zh: 我的仇敌终日要把我吞了，因逞骄傲攻击我的人甚多。
chapters.en: Mine enemies would daily swallow up: for many that fight against me, O thou most High.

chapters.zh: 我惧怕的时候要倚靠你。
chapters.en: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.

chapters.zh: 我倚靠神，我要赞美他的话；我倚靠神，必不惧怕。血气之辈能把我怎么样呢？
chapters.en: In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.

chapters.zh: 他们终日颠倒我的话；他们一切的心思都是要害我。
chapters.en: Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts against me for evil.

chapters.zh: 他们聚集，埋伏窥探我的脚踪，等候要害我的命。
chapters.en: They gather themselves together, they hide themselves, they mark my steps, when they wait for my soul.

chapters.zh: 他们岂能因罪孽逃脱么？神啊，求你在怒中使众民堕落！
chapters.en: Shall they escape by iniquity? in anger cast down the people, O God.

chapters.zh: 我几次流离，你都记数；求你把我眼泪装在你的皮袋里。这不都记在你册子上么？
chapters.en: Thou tellest my wanderings: put thou my tears into thy bottle: not in thy book?

chapters.zh: 我呼求的日子，我的仇敌都要转身退后。神帮助我，这是我所知道的。
chapters.en: When I cry , then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God for me.

chapters.zh: 我倚靠神，我要赞美他的话我倚靠耶和华，我要赞美他的话。
chapters.en: In God will I praise word: in the LORD will I praise word.

chapters.zh: 我倚靠神，必不惧怕。人能把我怎么样呢？
chapters.en: In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我向你所许的愿在我身上；我要将感谢祭献给你。
chapters.en: Thy vows upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.

chapters.zh: 因为你救我的命脱离死亡。你岂不是救护我的脚不跌倒、使我在生命光中行在神面前么？
chapters.en: For thou hast delivered my soul from death: not my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?

chapters.zh: （大卫逃避扫罗，藏在洞里。那时，他作这金诗，交与伶长。调用休要毁坏。）神啊，求你怜悯我，怜悯我！因为我的心投靠你。我要投靠在你翅膀的荫下，等到灾害过去。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave.] Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until calamities be overpast.

chapters.zh: 我要求告至高的神，就是为我成全诸事的神。
chapters.en: I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth for me.

chapters.zh: 那要吞我的人辱骂我的时候，神从天上必施恩救我，也必向我发出慈爱和诚实。
chapters.en: He shall send from heaven, and save me the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.

chapters.zh: 我的性命在狮子中间；我躺卧在性如烈火的世人当中。他们的牙齿是枪、箭；他们的舌头是快刀。
chapters.en: My soul among lions: I lie them that are set on fire, the sons of men, whose teeth spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.

chapters.zh: 神啊，愿你崇高过于诸天！愿你的荣耀高过全地！
chapters.en: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; thy glory above all the earth.

chapters.zh: 他们为我的脚设下网罗，压制我的心；他们在我面前挖了坑，自己反掉在其中。（细拉）
chapters.en: They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bowed down: they have digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen . Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我心坚定，我心坚定；我要唱诗，我要歌颂！
chapters.en: My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.

chapters.zh: 我的灵（原文是荣耀）啊，你当醒起！琴瑟啊，你们当醒起！我自己要极早醒起！
chapters.en: Awake up, my glory; awake, psaltery and harp: I will awake early.

chapters.zh: 主啊，我要在万民中称谢你，在列邦中歌颂你！
chapters.en: I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing unto thee among the nations.

chapters.zh: 因为，你的慈爱高及诸天；你的诚实达到穹苍。
chapters.en: For thy mercy great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.

chapters.zh: 神啊，愿你崇高过于诸天！愿你的荣耀高过全地！
chapters.en: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: thy glory above all the earth.

chapters.zh: （大卫的金诗，交与伶长。调用休要毁坏。）世人哪，你们默然不语，真合公义么？施行审判，岂按正直么？
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David.] Do ye indeed speak righteousness, O congregation? do ye judge uprightly, O ye sons of men?

chapters.zh: 不然！你们是心中作恶；你们在地上秤出你们手所行的强暴。
chapters.en: Yea, in heart ye work wickedness; ye weigh the violence of your hands in the earth.

chapters.zh: 恶人一出母胎就与神疏远，一离母腹便走错路，说谎话。
chapters.en: The wicked are estranged from the womb: they go astray as soon as they be born, speaking lies.

chapters.zh: 他们的毒气好像蛇的毒气；他们好像塞耳的聋虺，
chapters.en: Their poison like the poison of a serpent: like the deaf adder stoppeth her ear;

chapters.zh: 不听行法术的声音，虽用极灵的咒语也是不听。
chapters.en: Which will not hearken to the voice of charmers, charming never so wisely.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你敲碎他们口中的牙；耶和华啊，求你敲掉少壮狮子的大牙！
chapters.en: Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 愿他们消灭，如急流的水一般；他们瞅准射箭的时候，愿箭头彷佛砍断。
chapters.en: Let them melt away as waters run continually: he bendeth his arrows, let them be as cut in pieces.

chapters.zh: 愿他们像蜗牛消化过去，又像妇人坠落未见天日的胎。
chapters.en: As a snail melteth, let pass away: the untimely birth of a woman, they may not see the sun.

chapters.zh: 你们用荆棘烧火，锅还未热，他要用旋风把青的和烧著的一齐刮去。
chapters.en: Before your pots can feel the thorns, he shall take them away as with a whirlwind, both living, and in wrath.

chapters.zh: 义人见仇敌遭报就欢喜，要在恶人的血中洗脚。
chapters.en: The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 因此，人必说：义人诚然有善报；在地上果有施行判断的神！
chapters.en: So that a man shall say, Verily a reward for the righteous: verily he is a God that judgeth in the earth.

chapters.zh: （扫罗打发人窥探大卫的房屋，要杀他。那时，大卫用这金诗，交与伶长。调用休要毁坏。）我的神啊，求你救我脱离仇敌，把我安置在高处，得脱那些起来攻击我的人。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Altaschith, Michtam of David; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him.] Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.

chapters.zh: 求你救我脱离作孽的人和喜爱流人血的人！
chapters.en: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.

chapters.zh: 因为，他们埋伏要害我的命；有能力的人聚集来攻击我。耶和华啊，这不是为我的过犯，也不是为我的罪愆。
chapters.en: For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not my transgression, nor my sin, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 我虽然无过，他们预备整齐，跑来攻击我。求你兴起鉴察，帮助我！
chapters.en: They run and prepare themselves without fault: awake to help me, and behold.

chapters.zh: 万军之神 ─ 耶和华以色列的神啊！求你兴起，惩治万邦！不要怜悯行诡诈的恶人！（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou therefore, O LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.

chapters.zh: 他们晚上转回，叫号如狗，围城绕行。
chapters.en: They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.

chapters.zh: 他们口中喷吐恶言，嘴里有刀；他们说：有谁听见？
chapters.en: Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords in their lips: for who, , doth hear?

chapters.zh: 但你 ─ 耶和华必笑话他们；你要嗤笑万邦。
chapters.en: But thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.

chapters.zh: 我的力量啊，我必仰望你，因为神是我的高台。
chapters.en: his strength will I wait upon thee: for God my defence.

chapters.zh: 我的神要以慈爱迎接我；神要叫我看见我仇敌遭报。
chapters.en: The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see upon mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 不要杀他们，恐怕我的民忘记。主啊，你是我们的盾牌；求你用你的能力使他们四散，且降为卑。
chapters.en: Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them down, O Lord our shield.

chapters.zh: 他们口中的罪和嘴里的言语，并咒骂虚谎的话，愿他们在骄傲之中被缠住了。
chapters.en: the sin of their mouth the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying they speak.

chapters.zh: 求你发怒，使他们消灭，以致归于无有，叫他们知道神在雅各中间掌权，直到地极。（细拉）
chapters.en: Consume in wrath, consume , that they not : and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.

chapters.zh: 到了晚上，任凭他们转回；任凭他们叫号如狗，围城绕行。
chapters.en: And at evening let them return; let them make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.

chapters.zh: 他们必走来走去，寻找食物，若不得饱就终夜在外。
chapters.en: Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.

chapters.zh: 但我要歌颂你的力量，早晨要高唱你的慈爱；因为你作过我的高台，在我急难的日子作过我的避难所。
chapters.en: But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.

chapters.zh: 我的力量啊，我要歌颂你；因为神是我的高台，是赐恩与我的神。
chapters.en: Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God my defence, the God of my mercy.

chapters.zh: （大卫与两河间的亚兰并琐巴的亚兰争战的时候，约押转回，在盐谷攻击以东，杀了一万二千人。那时，大卫作这金诗叫人学习，交与伶长。调用为证的百合花。）神啊，你丢弃了我们，使我们破败；你向我们发怒，求你使我们复兴！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Shushaneduth, Michtam of David, to teach; when he strove with Aramnaharaim and with Aramzobah, when Joab returned, and smote of Edom in the valley of salt twelve thousand.] O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.

chapters.zh: 你使地震动，而且崩裂；求你将裂口医好，因为地摇动。
chapters.en: Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh.

chapters.zh: 你叫你的民遇见艰难；你叫我们喝那使人东倒西歪的酒。
chapters.en: Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made us to drink the wine of astonishment.

chapters.zh: 你把旌旗赐给敬畏你的人，可以为真理扬起来。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.

chapters.zh: 求你应允我们，用右手拯救我们，好叫你所亲爱的人得救。
chapters.en: That thy beloved may be delivered; save thy right hand, and hear me.

chapters.zh: 神已经指著他的圣洁说（说：或译应许我）：我要欢乐；我要分开示剑，丈量疏割谷。
chapters.en: God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.

chapters.zh: 基列是我的，玛拿西也是我的。以法莲是护卫我头的；犹大是我的杖。
chapters.en: Gilead mine, and Manasseh mine; Ephraim also the strength of mine head; Judah my lawgiver;

chapters.zh: 摩押是我的沐浴盆；我要向以东抛鞋。非利士啊，你还能因我欢呼么？
chapters.en: Moab my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me.

chapters.zh: 谁能领我进坚固城？谁能引我到以东地？
chapters.en: Who will bring me the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?

chapters.zh: 神啊，你不是丢弃了我们么？神啊，你不和我们的军兵同去么？
chapters.en: not thou, O God, hadst cast us off? and , O God, didst not go out with our armies?

chapters.zh: 求你帮助我们攻击敌人，因为人的帮助是枉然的。
chapters.en: Give us help from trouble: for vain the help of man.

chapters.zh: 我们倚靠神才得施展大能，因为践踏我们敌人的就是他。
chapters.en: Through God we shall do valiantly: for he shall tread down our enemies.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）神啊，求你听我的呼求，侧耳听我的祷告！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Neginah, of David.] Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.

chapters.zh: 我心里发昏的时候，我要从地极求告你。求你领我到那比我更高的磐石！
chapters.en: From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: lead me to the rock is higher than I.

chapters.zh: 因为你作过我的避难所，作过我的坚固台，脱离仇敌。
chapters.en: For thou hast been a shelter for me, a strong tower from the enemy.

chapters.zh: 我要永远住在你的帐幕里！我要投靠在你翅膀下的隐密处！（细拉）
chapters.en: I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever: I will trust in the covert of thy wings. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你原是听了我所许的愿；你将产业赐给敬畏你名的人。
chapters.en: For thou, O God, hast heard my vows: thou hast given the heritage of those that fear thy name.

chapters.zh: 你要加添王的寿数；他的年岁必存到世世。
chapters.en: Thou wilt prolong the king's life: his years as many generations.

chapters.zh: 他必永远坐在神面前；愿你预备慈爱和诚实保佑他！
chapters.en: He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, may preserve him.

chapters.zh: 这样，我要歌颂你的名，直到永远，好天天还我所许的愿。
chapters.en: So will I sing praise unto thy name for ever, that I may daily perform my vows.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，照耶杜顿的作法，交与伶长。）我的心默默无声，专等候神；我的救恩是从他而来。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of David.] Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him my salvation.

chapters.zh: 惟独他是我的磐石，我的拯救；他是我的高台，我必不很动摇。
chapters.en: He only my rock and my salvation; my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.

chapters.zh: 你们大家攻击一人，把他毁坏，如同毁坏歪斜的墙、将倒的壁，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall a tottering fence.

chapters.zh: 他们彼此商议，专要从他的尊位上把他推下；他们喜爱谎话，口虽祝福，心却咒诅。（细拉）
chapters.en: They only consult to cast down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你当默默无声，专等候神，因为我的盼望是从他而来。
chapters.en: My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation from him.

chapters.zh: 惟独他是我的磐石，我的拯救；他是我的高台，我必不动摇。
chapters.en: He only my rock and my salvation: my defence; I shall not be moved.

chapters.zh: 我的拯救、我的荣耀都在乎神；我力量的磐石、我的避难所都在乎神。
chapters.en: In God my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, my refuge, in God.

chapters.zh: 你们众民当时时倚靠他，在他面前倾心吐意；神是我们的避难所。（细拉）
chapters.en: Trust in him at all times; people, pour out your heart before him: God a refuge for us. Selah.

chapters.zh: 下流人真是虚空；上流人也是虚假；放在天平里就必浮起；他们一共比空气还轻。
chapters.en: Surely men of low degree vanity, men of high degree a lie: to be laid in the balance, they altogether than vanity.

chapters.zh: 不要仗势欺人，也不要因抢夺而骄傲；若财宝加增，不要放在心上。
chapters.en: Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart .

chapters.zh: 神说了一次、两次，我都听见：就是能力都属乎神。
chapters.en: God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power unto God.

chapters.zh: 主啊，慈爱也是属乎你，因为你照著各人所行的报应他。
chapters.en: Also unto thee, O Lord, mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.

chapters.zh: （大卫在犹大旷野的时候，作了这诗。）神啊，你是我的神，我要切切地寻求你，在干旱疲乏无水之地，我渴想你；我的心切慕你。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah.] O God, thou my God; early will I seek thee: my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee in a dry and thirsty land, where no water is;

chapters.zh: 我在圣所中曾如此瞻仰你，为要见你的能力和你的荣耀。
chapters.en: To see thy power and thy glory, so I have seen thee in the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 因你的慈爱比生命更好，我的嘴唇要颂赞你。
chapters.en: Because thy lovingkindness better than life, my lips shall praise thee.

chapters.zh: 我还活的时候要这样称颂你；我要奉你的名举手。
chapters.en: Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.

chapters.zh: 我在床上记念你，在夜更的时候思想你；我的心就像饱足了骨髓肥油，我也要以欢乐的嘴唇赞美你。
chapters.en: My soul shall be satisfied as marrow and fatness; and my mouth shall praise with joyful lips:

chapters.zh: 合和译本并入上一节
chapters.en: When I remember thee upon my bed, meditate on thee in the watches.

chapters.zh: 因为你曾帮助我，我就在你翅膀的荫下欢呼。
chapters.en: Because thou hast been my help, therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.

chapters.zh: 我心紧紧地跟随你；你的右手扶持我。
chapters.en: My soul followeth hard after thee: thy right hand upholdeth me.

chapters.zh: 但那些寻索要灭我命的人必往地底下去；
chapters.en: But those seek my soul, to destroy , shall go into the lower parts of the earth.

chapters.zh: 他们必被刀剑所杀，被野狗所吃。
chapters.en: They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.

chapters.zh: 但是王必因神欢喜。凡指著他发誓的必要夸口，因为说谎之人的口必被塞住。
chapters.en: But the king shall rejoice in God; every one that sweareth by him shall glory: but the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）神啊，我哀叹的时候，求你听我的声音！求你保护我的性命，不受仇敌的惊恐！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer: preserve my life from fear of the enemy.

chapters.zh: 求你把我隐藏，使我脱离作恶之人的暗谋和作孽之人的扰乱。
chapters.en: Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:

chapters.zh: 他们磨舌如刀，发出苦毒的言语，好像比准了的箭，
chapters.en: Who whet their tongue like a sword, bend their arrows, bitter words:

chapters.zh: 要在暗地射完全人；他们忽然射他，并不惧怕。
chapters.en: That they may shoot in secret at the perfect: suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.

chapters.zh: 他们彼此勉励设下恶计；他们商量暗设网罗，说：谁能看见？
chapters.en: They encourage themselves an evil matter: they commune of laying snares privily; they say, Who shall see them?

chapters.zh: 他们图谋奸恶，说：我们是极力图谋的。他们各人的意念心思是深的。
chapters.en: They search out iniquities; they accomplish a diligent search: both the inward of every one , and the heart, deep.

chapters.zh: 但神要射他们；他们忽然被箭射伤。
chapters.en: But God shall shoot at them an arrow; suddenly shall they be wounded.

chapters.zh: 他们必然绊跌，被自己的舌头所害；凡看见他们的必都摇头。
chapters.en: So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.

chapters.zh: 众人都要害怕，要传扬神的工作，并且明白他的作为。
chapters.en: And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.

chapters.zh: 义人必因耶和华欢喜，并要投靠他；凡心里正直的人都要夸口。
chapters.en: The righteous shall be glad in the LORD, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗歌，交与伶长。）神啊，锡安的人都等候赞美你；所许的愿也要向你偿还。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm Song of David.] Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion: and unto thee shall the vow be performed.

chapters.zh: 听祷告的主啊，凡有血气的都要来就你。
chapters.en: O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.

chapters.zh: 罪孽胜了我；至于我们的过犯，你都要赦免。
chapters.en: Iniquities prevail against me: our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.

chapters.zh: 你所拣选、使他亲近你、住在你院中的，这人便为有福！我们必因你居所、你圣殿的美福知足了。
chapters.en: Blessed thou choosest, and causest to approach he may dwell in thy courts: we shall be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, of thy holy temple.

chapters.zh: 拯救我们的神啊，你必以威严秉公义应允我们；你本是一切地极和海上远处的人所倚靠的。
chapters.en: terrible things in righteousness wilt thou answer us, O God of our salvation; the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of them that are afar off the sea:

chapters.zh: 他既以大能束腰，就用力量安定诸山，
chapters.en: Which by his strength setteth fast the mountains; girded with power:

chapters.zh: 使诸海的响声和其中波浪的响声，并万民的喧哗，都平静了。
chapters.en: Which stilleth the noise of the seas, the noise of their waves, and the tumult of the people.

chapters.zh: 住在地极的人因你的神迹惧怕；你使日出日落之地都欢呼。
chapters.en: They also that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.

chapters.zh: 你眷顾地，降下透雨，使地大得肥美。神的河满了水；你这样浇灌了地，好为人预备五谷。
chapters.en: Thou visitest the earth, and waterest it: thou greatly enrichest it with the river of God, is full of water: thou preparest them corn, when thou hast so provided for it.

chapters.zh: 你浇透地的犁沟，润平犁脊，降甘霖，使地软和；其中发长的，蒙你赐福。
chapters.en: Thou waterest the ridges thereof abundantly: thou settlest the furrows thereof: thou makest it soft with showers: thou blessest the springing thereof.

chapters.zh: 你以恩典为年岁的冠冕；你的路径都滴下脂油，
chapters.en: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; and thy paths drop fatness.

chapters.zh: 滴在旷野的草场上。小山以欢乐束腰；
chapters.en: They drop the pastures of the wilderness: and the little hills rejoice on every side.

chapters.zh: 草场以羊群为衣；谷中也长满了五谷。这一切都欢呼歌唱。
chapters.en: The pastures are clothed with flocks; the valleys also are covered over with corn; they shout for joy, they also sing.

chapters.zh: （一篇诗歌，交与伶长。）全地都当向神欢呼！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Song Psalm.] Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:

chapters.zh: 歌颂他名的荣耀！用赞美的言语将他的荣耀发明！
chapters.en: Sing forth the honour of his name: make his praise glorious.

chapters.zh: 当对神说：你的作为何等可畏！因你的大能，仇敌要投降你。
chapters.en: Say unto God, How terrible thy works! through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.

chapters.zh: 全地要敬拜你，歌颂你，要歌颂你的名。（细拉）
chapters.en: All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing thy name. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你们来看神所行的，他向世人所做之事是可畏的。
chapters.en: Come and see the works of God: terrible doing toward the children of men.

chapters.zh: 他将海变成干地，众民步行过河；我们在那里因他欢喜。
chapters.en: He turned the sea into dry : they went through the flood on foot: there did we rejoice in him.

chapters.zh: 他用权能治理万民，直到永远。他的眼睛鉴察列邦；悖逆的人不可自高。（细拉）
chapters.en: He ruleth by his power for ever; his eyes behold the nations: let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.

chapters.zh: 万民哪，你们当称颂我们的神，使人得听赞美他的声音。
chapters.en: O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:

chapters.zh: 他使我们的性命存活，也不叫我们的脚摇动。
chapters.en: Which holdeth our soul in life, and suffereth not our feet to be moved.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你曾试验我们，熬炼我们，如熬炼银子一样。
chapters.en: For thou, O God, hast proved us: thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.

chapters.zh: 你使我们进入网罗，把重担放在我们的身上。
chapters.en: Thou broughtest us into the net; thou laidst affliction upon our loins.

chapters.zh: 你使人坐车轧我们的头；我们经过水火，你却使我们到丰富之地。
chapters.en: Thou hast caused men to ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water: but thou broughtest us out into a wealthy .

chapters.zh: 我要用燔祭进你的殿，向你还我的愿，
chapters.en: I will go into thy house with burnt offerings: I will pay thee my vows,

chapters.zh: 就是在急难时我嘴唇所发的、口中所许的。
chapters.en: Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.

chapters.zh: 我要把肥牛作燔祭，将公羊的香祭献给你，又把公牛和山羊献上。（细拉）
chapters.en: I will offer unto thee burnt sacrifices of fatlings, with the incense of rams; I will offer bullocks with goats. Selah.

chapters.zh: 凡敬畏神的人，你们都来听！我要述说他为我所行的事。
chapters.en: Come hear, all ye that fear God, and I will declare what he hath done for my soul.

chapters.zh: 我曾用口求告他；我的舌头也称他为高。
chapters.en: I cried unto him with my mouth, and he was extolled with my tongue.

chapters.zh: 我若心里注重罪孽，主必不听。
chapters.en: If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear :

chapters.zh: 但神实在听见了；他侧耳听了我祷告的声音。
chapters.en: verily God hath heard ; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.

chapters.zh: 神是应当称颂的！他并没有推却我的祷告，也没有叫他的慈爱离开我。
chapters.en: Blessed God, which hath not turned away my prayer, nor his mercy from me.

chapters.zh: （一篇诗歌，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）愿神怜悯我们，赐福与我们，用脸光照我们，（细拉）
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm Song.] God be merciful unto us, and bless us; cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.

chapters.zh: 好叫世界得知你的道路，万国得知你的救恩。
chapters.en: That thy way may be known upon earth, thy saving health among all nations.

chapters.zh: 神啊，愿列邦称赞你！愿万民都称赞你！
chapters.en: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.

chapters.zh: 愿万国都快乐欢呼；因为你必按公正审判万民，引导世上的万国。（细拉）
chapters.en: O let the nations be glad and sing for joy: for thou shalt judge the people righteously, and govern the nations upon earth. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，愿列邦称赞你！愿万民都称赞你！
chapters.en: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.

chapters.zh: 地已经出了土产；神 ─ 就是我们的神要赐福与我们。
chapters.en: shall the earth yield her increase; God, our own God, shall bless us.

chapters.zh: 神要赐福与我们；地的四极都要敬畏他！
chapters.en: God shall bless us; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗歌，交与伶长。）愿神兴起，使他的仇敌四散，叫那恨他的人从他面前逃跑。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm Song of David.] Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered: let them also that hate him flee before him.

chapters.zh: 他们被驱逐，如烟被风吹散；恶人见神之面而消灭，如蜡被火镕化。
chapters.en: As smoke is driven away, drive away: as wax melteth before the fire, let the wicked perish at the presence of God.

chapters.zh: 惟有义人必然欢喜，在神面前高兴快乐。
chapters.en: But let the righteous be glad; let them rejoice before God: yea, let them exceedingly rejoice.

chapters.zh: 你们当向神唱诗，歌颂他的名；为那坐车行过旷野的修平大路。他的名是耶和华，要在他面前欢乐！
chapters.en: Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name JAH, and rejoice before him.

chapters.zh: 神在他的圣所作孤儿的父，作寡妇的伸冤者。
chapters.en: A father of the fatherless, and a judge of the widows, God in his holy habitation.

chapters.zh: 神叫孤独的有家，使被囚的出来享福；惟有悖逆的住在干燥之地。
chapters.en: God setteth the solitary in families: he bringeth out those which are bound with chains: but the rebellious dwell in a dry .

chapters.zh: 神啊，你曾在你百姓前头出来，在旷野行走。（细拉）
chapters.en: O God, when thou wentest forth before thy people, when thou didst march through the wilderness; Selah:

chapters.zh: 那时，地见神的面而震动，天也落雨；西乃山见以色列神的面也震动。
chapters.en: The earth shook, the heavens also dropped at the presence of God: Sinai itself at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你降下大雨；你产业以色列疲乏的时候，你使他坚固。
chapters.en: Thou, O God, didst send a plentiful rain, whereby thou didst confirm thine inheritance, when it was weary.

chapters.zh: 你的会众住在其中；神啊，你的恩惠是为困苦人预备的。
chapters.en: Thy congregation hath dwelt therein: thou, O God, hast prepared of thy goodness for the poor.

chapters.zh: 主发命令，传好信息的妇女成了大群。
chapters.en: The Lord gave the word: great the company of those that published .

chapters.zh: 统兵的君王逃跑了，逃跑了；在家等候的妇女分受所夺的。
chapters.en: Kings of armies did flee apace: and she that tarried at home divided the spoil.

chapters.zh: 你们安卧在羊圈的时候，好像鸽子的翅膀镀白银，翎毛镀黄金一般。
chapters.en: Though ye have lien among the pots, the wings of a dove covered with silver, and her feathers with yellow gold.

chapters.zh: 全能者在境内赶散列王的时候，势如飘雪在撒们。
chapters.en: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, it was as snow in Salmon.

chapters.zh: 巴珊山是神的山；巴珊山是多峰多岭的山。
chapters.en: The hill of God the hill of Bashan; an high hill the hill of Bashan.

chapters.zh: 你们多峰多岭的山哪，为何斜看神所愿居住的山？耶和华必住这山，直到永远！
chapters.en: Why leap ye, ye high hills? the hill God desireth to dwell in; yea, the LORD will dwell for ever.

chapters.zh: 神的车葱讵万盈千；主在其中，好像在西乃圣山一样。
chapters.en: The chariots of God twenty thousand, thousands of angels: the Lord among them, Sinai, in the holy .

chapters.zh: 你已经升上高天，掳掠仇敌；你在人间，就是在悖逆的人间，受了供献，叫耶和华神可以与他们同住。
chapters.en: Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast led captivity captive: thou hast received gifts for men; yea, the rebellious also, that the LORD God might dwell .

chapters.zh: 天天背负我们重担的主，就是拯救我们的神，是应当称颂的！（细拉）
chapters.en: Blessed the Lord, daily loadeth us the God of our salvation. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神是为我们施行诸般救恩的神；人能脱离死亡是在乎主耶和华。
chapters.en: our God the God of salvation; and unto GOD the Lord the issues from death.

chapters.zh: 但神要打破他仇敌的头，就是那常犯罪之人的发顶。
chapters.en: But God shall wound the head of his enemies, the hairy scalp of such an one as goeth on still in his trespasses.

chapters.zh: 主说：我要使众民从巴珊而归，使他们从深海而回，
chapters.en: The Lord said, I will bring again from Bashan, I will bring again from the depths of the sea:

chapters.zh: 使你打碎仇敌，你的脚踹在血中，使你狗的舌头从其中得分。
chapters.en: That thy foot may be dipped in the blood of enemies, the tongue of thy dogs in the same.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你是我的神，我的王；人已经看见你行走，进入圣所。
chapters.en: They have seen thy goings, O God; the goings of my God, my King, in the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 歌唱的行在前，作乐的随在后，都在击鼓的童女中间。
chapters.en: The singers went before, the players on instruments after; among the damsels playing with timbrels.

chapters.zh: 从以色列源头而来的，当在各会中称颂主神！
chapters.en: Bless ye God in the congregations, the Lord, from the fountain of Israel.

chapters.zh: 在那里，有统管他们的小便雅悯，有犹大的首领和他们的群众，有西布伦的首领，有拿弗他利的首领。
chapters.en: There little Benjamin their ruler, the princes of Judah their council, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.

chapters.zh: 以色列的能力是神所赐的；神啊，求你坚固你为我们所成全的事！
chapters.en: Thy God hath commanded thy strength: strengthen, O God, that which thou hast wrought for us.

chapters.zh: 因你耶路撒冷的殿，列王必带贡物献给你。
chapters.en: Because of thy temple at Jerusalem shall kings bring presents unto thee.

chapters.zh: 求你叱喝芦苇中的野兽和群公牛，并列邦中的牛犊。把银块踹在脚下；神已经赶散好争战的列邦。
chapters.en: Rebuke the company of spearmen, the multitude of the bulls, with the calves of the people, submit himself with pieces of silver: scatter thou the people delight in war.

chapters.zh: 埃及的公侯要出来朝见神；古实人要急忙举手祷告。
chapters.en: Princes shall come out of Egypt; Ethiopia shall soon stretch out her hands unto God.

chapters.zh: 世上的列国啊，你们要向神歌唱；愿你们歌颂主！
chapters.en: Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:

chapters.zh: 歌颂那自古驾行在诸天以上的主！他发出声音，是极大的声音。
chapters.en: To him that rideth upon the heavens of heavens, of old; lo, he doth send out his voice, a mighty voice.

chapters.zh: 你们要将能力归给神。他的威荣在以色列之上；他的能力是在穹苍。
chapters.en: Ascribe ye strength unto God: his excellency over Israel, and his strength in the clouds.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你从圣所显为可畏；以色列的神是那将力量权能赐给他百姓的。神是应当称颂的！
chapters.en: O God, terrible out of thy holy places: the God of Israel he that giveth strength and power unto people. Blessed God.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。调用百合花。）神啊，求你救我！因为众水要淹没我。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Shoshannim, of David.] Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto soul.

chapters.zh: 我陷在深淤泥中，没有立脚之地；我到了深水中，大水漫过我身。
chapters.en: I sink in deep mire, where no standing: I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.

chapters.zh: 我因呼求困乏，喉咙发干；我因等候神，眼睛失明。
chapters.en: I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.

chapters.zh: 无故恨我的，比我头发还多；无理与我为仇、要把我剪除的，甚为强盛。我没有抢夺的，要叫我偿还。
chapters.en: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored which I took not away.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我的愚昧，你原知道；我的罪愆不能隐瞒。
chapters.en: O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.

chapters.zh: 万军的主 ─ 耶和华啊，求你叫那等候你的，不要因我蒙羞！以色列的神啊，求你叫那寻求你的，不要因我受辱！
chapters.en: Let not them that wait on thee, O Lord GOD of hosts, be ashamed for my sake: let not those that seek thee be confounded for my sake, O God of Israel.

chapters.zh: 因我为你的缘故受了辱骂，满面羞愧。
chapters.en: Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame hath covered my face.

chapters.zh: 我的弟兄看我为外路人；我的同胞看我为外邦人。
chapters.en: I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother's children.

chapters.zh: 因我为你的殿心里焦急，如同火烧，并且辱骂你人的辱骂都落在我身上。
chapters.en: For the zeal of thine house hath eaten me up; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.

chapters.zh: 我哭泣，以禁食刻苦我心；这倒算为我的羞辱。
chapters.en: When I wept, my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.

chapters.zh: 我拿麻布当衣裳，就成了他们的笑谈。
chapters.en: I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.

chapters.zh: 坐在城门口的谈论我；酒徒也以我为歌曲。
chapters.en: They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I the song of the drunkards.

chapters.zh: 但我在悦纳的时候向你 ─ 耶和华祈祷。神啊，求你按你丰盛的慈爱，凭你拯救的诚实应允我！
chapters.en: But as for me, my prayer unto thee, O LORD, an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.

chapters.zh: 求你搭救我出离淤泥，不叫我陷在其中；求你使我脱离那些恨我的人，使我出离深水。
chapters.en: Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink: let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.

chapters.zh: 求你不容大水漫过我，不容深渊吞灭我，不容坑坎在我以上合口。
chapters.en: Let not the waterflood overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit shut her mouth upon me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你应允我！因为你的慈爱本为美好；求你按你丰盛的慈悲回转眷顾我！
chapters.en: Hear me, O LORD; for thy lovingkindness good: turn unto me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.

chapters.zh: 不要掩面不顾你的仆人；我是在急难之中，求你速速地应允我！
chapters.en: And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble: hear me speedily.

chapters.zh: 求你亲近我，救赎我！求你因我的仇敌把我赎回！
chapters.en: Draw nigh unto my soul, redeem it: deliver me because of mine enemies.

chapters.zh: 你知道我受的辱骂、欺凌、羞辱；我的敌人都在你面前。
chapters.en: Thou hast known my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries all before thee.

chapters.zh: 辱骂伤破了我的心，我又满了忧愁。我指望有人体恤，却没有一个；我指望有人安慰，却找不著一个。
chapters.en: Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: and I looked to take pity, but none; and for comforters, but I found none.

chapters.zh: 他们拿苦胆给我当食物；我渴了，他们拿醋给我喝。
chapters.en: They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.

chapters.zh: 愿他们的筵席在他们面前变为网罗，在他们平安的时候变为机槛。
chapters.en: Let their table become a snare before them: and for welfare, a trap.

chapters.zh: 愿他们的眼睛昏蒙，不得看见；愿你使他们的腰常常战抖。
chapters.en: Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.

chapters.zh: 求你将你的恼恨倒在他们身上，叫你的烈怒追上他们。
chapters.en: Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful anger take hold of them.

chapters.zh: 愿他们的住处变为荒场；愿他们的帐棚无人居住。
chapters.en: Let their habitation be desolate; let none dwell in their tents.

chapters.zh: 因为，你所击打的，他们就逼迫；你所击伤的，他们戏说他的愁苦。
chapters.en: For they persecute whom thou hast smitten; and they talk to the grief of those whom thou hast wounded.

chapters.zh: 愿你在他们的罪上加罪，不容他们在你面前称义。
chapters.en: Add iniquity unto their iniquity: and let them not come into thy righteousness.

chapters.zh: 愿他们从生命册上被涂抹，不得记录在义人之中。
chapters.en: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.

chapters.zh: 但我是困苦忧伤的；神啊，愿你的救恩将我安置在高处。
chapters.en: But I poor and sorrowful: let thy salvation, O God, set me up on high.

chapters.zh: 我要以诗歌赞美神的名，以感谢称他为大！
chapters.en: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.

chapters.zh: 这便叫耶和华喜悦，胜似献牛，或是献有角有蹄的公牛。
chapters.en: also shall please the LORD better than an ox bullock that hath horns and hoofs.

chapters.zh: 谦卑的人看见了就喜乐；寻求神的人，愿你们的心苏醒。
chapters.en: The humble shall see be glad: and your heart shall live that seek God.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华听了穷乏人，不藐视被囚的人。
chapters.en: For the LORD heareth the poor, and despiseth not his prisoners.

chapters.zh: 愿天和地、洋海和其中一切的动物都赞美他！
chapters.en: Let the heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moveth therein.

chapters.zh: 因为神要拯救锡安，建造犹大的城邑；他的民要在那里居住，得以为业。
chapters.en: For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah: that they may dwell there, and have it in possession.

chapters.zh: 他仆人的后裔要承受为业；爱他名的人也要住在其中。
chapters.en: The seed also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his name shall dwell therein.

chapters.zh: （大卫的记念诗，交与伶长。）神啊，求你快快搭救我！耶和华啊，求你速速帮助我！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, of David, to bring to remembrance.] , O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 愿那些寻索我命的，抱愧蒙羞；愿那些喜悦我遭害的，退后受辱。
chapters.en: Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward, and put to confusion, that desire my hurt.

chapters.zh: 愿那些对我说阿哈、阿哈的，因羞愧退后。
chapters.en: Let them be turned back for a reward of their shame that say, Aha, aha.

chapters.zh: 愿一切寻求你的，因你高兴欢喜；愿那些喜爱你救恩的，常说：当尊神为大！
chapters.en: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.

chapters.zh: 但我是困苦穷乏的；神啊，求你速速到我这里来！你是帮助我的，搭救我的。耶和华啊，求你不要耽延！
chapters.en: But I poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou my help and my deliverer; O LORD, make no tarrying.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我投靠你；求你叫我永不羞愧！
chapters.en: In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust: let me never be put to confusion.

chapters.zh: 求你凭你的公义搭救我，救拔我；侧耳听我，拯救我！
chapters.en: Deliver me in thy righteousness, and cause me to escape: incline thine ear unto me, and save me.

chapters.zh: 求你作我常住的磐石；你已经命定要救我，因为你是我的岩石，我的山寨。
chapters.en: Be thou my strong habitation, whereunto I may continually resort: thou hast given commandment to save me; for thou my rock and my fortress.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，求你救我脱离恶人的手，脱离不义和残暴之人的手。
chapters.en: Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.

chapters.zh: 主 ─ 耶和华啊，你是我所盼望的；从我年幼，你是我所倚靠的。
chapters.en: For thou my hope, O Lord GOD: my trust from my youth.

chapters.zh: 我从出母胎被你扶持；使我出母腹的是你。我必常常赞美你！
chapters.en: By thee have I been holden up from the womb: thou art he that took me out of my mother's bowels: my praise continually of thee.

chapters.zh: 许多人以我为怪，但你是我坚固的避难所。
chapters.en: I am as a wonder unto many; but thou my strong refuge.

chapters.zh: 你的赞美，你的荣耀终日必满了我的口。
chapters.en: Let my mouth be filled thy praise thy honour all the day.

chapters.zh: 我年老的时候，求你不要丢弃我！我力气衰弱的时候，求你不要离弃我！
chapters.en: Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.

chapters.zh: 我的仇敌议论我；那些窥探要害我命的彼此商议，
chapters.en: For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,

chapters.zh: 说：神已经离弃他；我们追赶他，捉拿他罢！因为没有人搭救。
chapters.en: Saying, God hath forsaken him: persecute and take him; for none to deliver .

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你不要远离我！我的神啊，求你速速帮助我！
chapters.en: O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.

chapters.zh: 愿那与我性命为敌的，羞愧被灭；愿那谋害我的，受辱蒙羞。
chapters.en: Let them be confounded consumed that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
