chapters.zh: 我却要常常盼望，并要越发赞美你。
chapters.en: But I will hope continually, and will yet praise thee more and more.

chapters.zh: 我的口终日要述说你的公义和你的救恩，因我不计其数。
chapters.en: My mouth shall shew forth thy righteousness thy salvation all the day; for I know not the numbers .

chapters.zh: 我要来说主 ─ 耶和华大能的事；我单要提说你的公义。
chapters.en: I will go in the strength of the Lord GOD: I will make mention of thy righteousness, of thine only.

chapters.zh: 神啊，自我年幼时，你就教训我；直到如今，我传扬你奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: O God, thou hast taught me from my youth: and hitherto have I declared thy wondrous works.

chapters.zh: 神啊，我到年老发白的时候，求你不要离弃我！等我将你的能力指示下代，将你的大能指示后世的人。
chapters.en: Now also when I am old and grayheaded, O God, forsake me not; until I have shewed thy strength unto generation, thy power to every one is to come.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你的公义甚高；行过大事的神啊，谁能像你！
chapters.en: Thy righteousness also, O God, very high, who hast done great things: O God, who like unto thee!

chapters.zh: 你是叫我们多经历重大急难的，必使我们复活，从地的深处救上来。
chapters.en: , which hast shewed me great and sore troubles, shalt quicken me again, and shalt bring me up again from the depths of the earth.

chapters.zh: 求你使我越发昌大，又转来安慰我。
chapters.en: Thou shalt increase my greatness, and comfort me on every side.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，我要鼓瑟称赞你，称赞你的诚实！以色列的圣者啊，我要弹琴歌颂你！
chapters.en: I will also praise thee with the psaltery, thy truth, O my God: unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我歌颂你的时候，我的嘴唇和你所赎我的灵魂都必欢呼；
chapters.en: My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.

chapters.zh: 并且我的舌头必终日讲论你的公义，因为那些谋害我的人已经蒙羞受辱了。
chapters.en: My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.

chapters.zh: （所罗门的诗。）神啊，求你将判断的权柄赐给王，将公义赐给王的儿子。
chapters.en: [ for Solomon.] Give the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness unto the king's son.

chapters.zh: 他要按公义审判你的民，按公平审判你的困苦人。
chapters.en: He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.

chapters.zh: 大山小山都要因公义使民得享平安。
chapters.en: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the little hills, by righteousness.

chapters.zh: 他必为民中的困苦人伸冤，拯救穷乏之辈，压碎那欺压人的。
chapters.en: He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.

chapters.zh: 太阳还存，月亮还在，人要敬畏你，直到万代！
chapters.en: They shall fear thee as long as the sun and moon endure, throughout all generations.

chapters.zh: 他必降临，像雨降在已割的草地上，如甘霖滋润田地。
chapters.en: He shall come down like rain upon the mown grass: as showers water the earth.

chapters.zh: 在他的日子，义人要发旺，大有平安，好像月亮长存。
chapters.en: In his days shall the righteous flourish; and abundance of peace so long as the moon endureth.

chapters.zh: 他要执掌权柄，从这海直到那海，从大河直到地极。
chapters.en: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.

chapters.zh: 住在旷野的，必在他面前下拜；他的仇敌必要舔土。
chapters.en: They that dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him; and his enemies shall lick the dust.

chapters.zh: 他施和海岛的王要进贡；示巴和西巴的王要献礼物。
chapters.en: The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall bring presents: the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.

chapters.zh: 诸王都要叩拜他；万国都要事奉他。
chapters.en: Yea, all kings shall fall down before him: all nations shall serve him.

chapters.zh: 因为，穷乏人呼求的时候，他要搭救；没有人帮助的困苦人，他也要搭救。
chapters.en: For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and that hath no helper.

chapters.zh: 他要怜恤贫寒和穷乏的人，拯救穷苦人的性命。
chapters.en: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.

chapters.zh: 他要救赎他们脱离欺压和强暴；他们的血在他眼中看为宝贵。
chapters.en: He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.

chapters.zh: 他们要存活。示巴的金子要奉给他；人要常常为他祷告，终日称颂他。
chapters.en: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba: prayer also shall be made for him continually; daily shall he be praised.

chapters.zh: 在地的山顶上，五谷必然茂盛（或译：有一把五谷）；所结的谷实要响动，如利巴嫩的树林；城里的人要发旺，如地上的草。
chapters.en: There shall be an handful of corn in the earth upon the top of the mountains; the fruit thereof shall shake like Lebanon: and of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.

chapters.zh: 他的名要存到永远，要留传如日之久。人要因他蒙福；万国要称他有福。
chapters.en: His name shall endure for ever: his name shall be continued as long as the sun: and shall be blessed in him: all nations shall call him blessed.

chapters.zh: 独行奇事的耶和华 ─ 以色列的神是应当称颂的！
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD God, the God of Israel, who only doeth wondrous things.

chapters.zh: 他荣耀的名也当称颂，直到永远。愿他的荣耀充满全地！阿们！阿们！
chapters.en: And blessed his glorious name for ever: and let the whole earth be filled his glory; Amen, and Amen.

chapters.zh: 耶西的儿子 ─ 大卫的祈祷完毕。
chapters.en: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗。）神实在恩待以色列那些清心的人！
chapters.en: [A Psalm of Asaph.] Truly God good to Israel, to such as are of a clean heart.

chapters.zh: 至于我，我的脚几乎失闪；我的脚险些滑跌。
chapters.en: But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.

chapters.zh: 我见恶人和狂傲人享平安就心怀不平。
chapters.en: For I was envious at the foolish, I saw the prosperity of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 他们死的时候没有疼痛；他们的力气却也壮实。
chapters.en: For no bands in their death: but their strength firm.

chapters.zh: 他们不像别人受苦，也不像别人遭灾。
chapters.en: They not in trouble men; neither are they plagued like men.

chapters.zh: 所以，骄傲如链子戴在他们的项上；强暴像衣裳遮住他们的身体。
chapters.en: Therefore pride compasseth them about as a chain; violence covereth them a garment.

chapters.zh: 他们的眼睛因体胖而凸出；他们所得的，过于心里所想的。
chapters.en: Their eyes stand out with fatness: they have more than heart could wish.

chapters.zh: 他们讥笑人，凭恶意说欺压人的话；他们说话自高。
chapters.en: They are corrupt, and speak wickedly oppression: they speak loftily.

chapters.zh: 他们的口亵渎上天；他们的舌毁谤全地。
chapters.en: They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.

chapters.zh: 所以神的民归到这里，喝尽了满杯的苦水。
chapters.en: Therefore his people return hither: and waters of a full are wrung out to them.

chapters.zh: 他们说：神怎能晓得？至高者岂有知识呢？
chapters.en: And they say, How doth God know? and is there knowledge in the most High?

chapters.zh: 看哪，这就是恶人；他们既是常享安逸，财宝便加增。
chapters.en: Behold, these the ungodly, who prosper in the world; they increase riches.

chapters.zh: 我实在徒然洁净了我的心，徒然洗手表明无辜。
chapters.en: Verily I have cleansed my heart vain, and washed my hands in innocency.

chapters.zh: 因为，我终日遭灾难；每早晨受惩治。
chapters.en: For all the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.

chapters.zh: 我若说，我要这样讲，这就是以奸诈待你的众子。
chapters.en: If I say, I will speak thus; behold, I should offend the generation of thy children.

chapters.zh: 我思索怎能明白这事，眼看实系为难，
chapters.en: When I thought to know this, it too painful for me;

chapters.zh: 等我进了神的圣所，思想他们的结局。
chapters.en: Until I went into the sanctuary of God; understood I their end.

chapters.zh: 你实在把他们安在滑地，使他们掉在沉沦之中。
chapters.en: Surely thou didst set them in slippery places: thou castedst them down into destruction.

chapters.zh: 他们转眼之间成了何等的荒凉！他们被惊恐灭尽了。
chapters.en: How are they into desolation, as in a moment! they are utterly consumed with terrors.

chapters.zh: 人睡醒了，怎样看梦；主啊，你醒了也必照样轻看他们的影像。
chapters.en: As a dream when awaketh; , O Lord, when thou awakest, thou shalt despise their image.

chapters.zh: 因而，我心里发酸，肺腑被刺。
chapters.en: Thus my heart was grieved, and I was pricked in my reins.

chapters.zh: 我这样愚昧无知，在你面前如畜类一般。
chapters.en: So foolish I, and ignorant: I was a beast before thee.

chapters.zh: 然而，我常与你同在；你搀著我的右手。
chapters.en: Nevertheless I continually with thee: thou hast holden by my right hand.

chapters.zh: 你要以你的训言引导我，以后必接我到荣耀里。
chapters.en: Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me glory.

chapters.zh: 除你以外，在天上我有谁呢？除你以外，在地上我也没有所爱慕的。
chapters.en: Whom have I in heaven ? and none upon earth I desire beside thee.

chapters.zh: 我的肉体和我的心肠衰残；但神是我心里的力量，又是我的福分，直到永远。
chapters.en: My flesh and my heart faileth: God the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.

chapters.zh: 远离你的，必要死亡；凡离弃你行邪淫的，你都灭绝了。
chapters.en: For, lo, they that are far from thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that go a whoring from thee.

chapters.zh: 但我亲近神是与我有益；我以主耶和华为我的避难所，好叫我述说你一切的作为。
chapters.en: But good for me to draw near to God: I have put my trust in the Lord GOD, that I may declare all thy works.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的训诲诗。）神啊，你为何永远丢弃我们呢？你为何向你草场的羊发怒，如烟冒出呢？
chapters.en: [Maschil of Asaph.] O God, why hast thou cast off for ever? doth thine anger smoke against the sheep of thy pasture?

chapters.zh: 求你记念你古时所得来的会众，就是你所赎 ﹔ 作你产业支派的，并记念你向来所居住的锡安山。
chapters.en: Remember thy congregation, thou hast purchased of old; the rod of thine inheritance, thou hast redeemed; this mount Zion, wherein thou hast dwelt.

chapters.zh: 求你举步去看那日久荒凉之地，仇敌在圣所中所行的一切恶事。
chapters.en: Lift up thy feet unto the perpetual desolations; all the enemy hath done wickedly in the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 你的敌人在你会中吼叫；他们竖了自己的旗为记号。
chapters.en: Thine enemies roar in the midst of thy congregations; they set up their ensigns signs.

chapters.zh: 他们好像人扬起斧子，砍伐林中的树。
chapters.en: was famous according as he had lifted up axes upon the thick trees.

chapters.zh: 圣所中一切雕刻的，他们现在用斧子锤子打坏了。
chapters.en: But now they break down the carved work thereof at once with axes and hammers.

chapters.zh: 他们用火焚烧你的圣所，亵渎你名的居所，拆毁到地。
chapters.en: They have cast fire into thy sanctuary, they have defiled the dwelling place of thy name to the ground.

chapters.zh: 他们心里说：我们要尽行毁灭；他们就在遍地把神的会所都烧毁了。
chapters.en: They said in their hearts, Let us destroy them together: they have burned up all the synagogues of God in the land.

chapters.zh: 我们不见我们的标帜，不再有先知；我们内中也没有人知道这灾祸要到几时呢！
chapters.en: We see not our signs: no more any prophet: neither among us any that knoweth how long.

chapters.zh: 神啊，敌人辱骂要到几时呢？仇敌亵渎你的名要到永远么？
chapters.en: O God, how long shall the adversary reproach? shall the enemy blaspheme thy name for ever?

chapters.zh: 你为甚么缩回你的右手？求你从怀中伸出来，毁灭他们。
chapters.en: Why withdrawest thou thy hand, even thy right hand? pluck out of thy bosom.

chapters.zh: 神自古以来为我的王，在地上施行拯救。
chapters.en: For God my King of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.

chapters.zh: 你曾用能力将海分开，将水中大鱼的头打破。
chapters.en: Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou brakest the heads of the dragons in the waters.

chapters.zh: 你曾砸碎鳄鱼的头，把他给旷野的禽兽（禽兽：原文是民）为食物。
chapters.en: Thou brakest the heads of leviathan in pieces, gavest him meat to the people inhabiting the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 你曾分裂磐石，水便成了溪河；你使长流的江河干了。
chapters.en: Thou didst cleave the fountain and the flood: thou driedst up mighty rivers.

chapters.zh: 白昼属你，黑夜也属你；亮光和日头是你所预备的。
chapters.en: The day thine, the night also thine: thou hast prepared the light and the sun.

chapters.zh: 地的一切疆界是你所立的；夏天和冬天是你所定的。
chapters.en: Thou hast set all the borders of the earth: thou hast made summer and winter.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，仇敌辱骂，愚顽民亵渎了你的名，求你记念这事。
chapters.en: Remember this, the enemy hath reproached, O LORD, and the foolish people have blasphemed thy name.

chapters.zh: 不要将你斑鸠的性命交给野兽；不要永远忘记你困苦人的性命。
chapters.en: O deliver not the soul of thy turtledove unto the multitude : forget not the congregation of thy poor for ever.

chapters.zh: 求你顾念所立的约，因为地上黑暗之处都满了强暴的居所。
chapters.en: Have respect unto the covenant: for the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of cruelty.

chapters.zh: 不要叫受欺压的人蒙羞回去；要叫困苦穷乏的人赞美你的名。
chapters.en: O let not the oppressed return ashamed: let the poor and needy praise thy name.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你起来为自己伸诉！要记念愚顽人怎样终日辱骂你。
chapters.en: Arise, O God, plead thine own cause: remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee daily.

chapters.zh: 不要忘记你敌人的声音；那起来敌你之人的喧哗时常上升。
chapters.en: Forget not the voice of thine enemies: the tumult of those that rise up against thee increaseth continually.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗歌，交与伶长。调用休要毁坏。）神啊，我们称谢你，我们称谢你！因为你的名相近，人都述说你奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, Altaschith, A Psalm Song of Asaph.] Unto thee, O God, do we give thanks, do we give thanks: for thy name is near thy wondrous works declare.

chapters.zh: 我到了所定的日期，必按正直施行审判。
chapters.en: When I shall receive the congregation I will judge uprightly.

chapters.zh: 地和其上的居民都消化了；我曾立了地的柱子。（细拉）
chapters.en: The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I bear up the pillars of it. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我对狂傲人说：不要行事狂傲！对凶恶人说：不要举角！
chapters.en: I said unto the fools, Deal not foolishly: and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn:

chapters.zh: 不要把你们的角高举；不要挺著颈项说话。
chapters.en: Lift not up your horn on high: speak a stiff neck.

chapters.zh: 因为高举非从东，非从西，也非从南而来。
chapters.en: For promotion neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the south.

chapters.zh: 惟有神断定；他使这人降卑，使那人升高。
chapters.en: But God the judge: he putteth down one, and setteth up another.

chapters.zh: 耶和华手里有杯，其中的酒起沫，杯内满了搀杂的酒；他倒出来，地上的恶人必都喝这酒的渣滓，而且喝尽。
chapters.en: For in the hand of the LORD a cup, and the wine is red; it is full of mixture; and he poureth out of the same: but the dregs thereof, all the wicked of the earth shall wring out, drink .

chapters.zh: 但我要宣扬，直到永远！我要歌颂雅各的神！
chapters.en: But I will declare for ever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 恶人一切的角，我要砍断；惟有义人的角必被高举。
chapters.en: All the horns of the wicked also will I cut off; the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗歌，交与伶长。用丝弦的乐器。）在犹大，神为人所认识；在以色列，他的名为大。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm Song of Asaph.] In Judah God known: his name great in Israel.

chapters.zh: 在撒冷有他的帐幕；在锡安有他的居所。
chapters.en: In Salem also is his tabernacle, and his dwelling place in Zion.

chapters.zh: 他在那里折断弓上的火箭，并盾牌、刀剑，和争战的兵器。（细拉）
chapters.en: There brake he the arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the battle. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你从有野食之山而来，有光华和荣美。
chapters.en: Thou more glorious excellent than the mountains of prey.

chapters.zh: 心中勇敢的人都被抢夺；他们睡了长觉，没有一个英雄能措手。
chapters.en: The stouthearted are spoiled, they have slept their sleep: and none of the men of might have found their hands.

chapters.zh: 雅各的神啊，你的斥责一发，坐车的 ﹔ 骑马的都沉睡了。
chapters.en: At thy rebuke, O God of Jacob, both the chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

chapters.zh: 惟独你是可畏的！你怒气一发，谁能在你面前站得住呢？
chapters.en: Thou, thou, to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?

chapters.zh: 你从天上使人听判断。神起来施行审判，要救地上一切谦卑的人；那时地就惧怕而静默。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou didst cause judgment to be heard from heaven; the earth feared, and was still,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: When God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.

chapters.zh: 人的忿怒要成全你的荣美；人的余怒，你要禁止。
chapters.en: Surely the wrath of man shall praise thee: the remainder of wrath shalt thou restrain.

chapters.zh: 你们许愿，当向耶和华 ─ 你们的神还愿；在他四面的人都当拿贡物献给那可畏的主。
chapters.en: Vow, and pay unto the LORD your God: let all that be round about him bring presents unto him that ought to be feared.

chapters.zh: 他要挫折王子的骄气；他向地上的君王显威可畏。
chapters.en: He shall cut off the spirit of princes: terrible to the kings of the earth.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗，照耶杜顿的作法，交与伶长。）我要向神发声呼求；我向神发声，他必留心听我。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, to Jeduthun, A Psalm of Asaph.] I cried unto God with my voice, unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.

chapters.zh: 我在患难之日寻求主；我在夜间不住地举手祷告；我的心不肯受安慰。
chapters.en: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran in the night, and ceased not: my soul refused to be comforted.

chapters.zh: 我想念神，就烦燥不安；我沉吟悲伤，心便发昏。（细拉）
chapters.en: I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你叫我不能闭眼；我烦乱不安，甚至不能说话。
chapters.en: Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.

chapters.zh: 我追想古时之日，上古之年。
chapters.en: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.

chapters.zh: 我想起我夜间的歌曲，扪心自问；我心里也仔细省察。
chapters.en: I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.

chapters.zh: 难道主要永远丢弃我，不再施恩么？
chapters.en: Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?

chapters.zh: 难道他的慈爱永远穷尽，他的应许世世废弃么？
chapters.en: Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth promise fail for evermore?

chapters.zh: 难道神忘记开恩，因发怒就止住他的慈悲么？（细拉）
chapters.en: Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.

chapters.zh: 我便说：这是我的懦弱，但我要追念至高者显出右手之年代。
chapters.en: And I said, This my infirmity: the years of the right hand of the most High.

chapters.zh: 我要提说耶和华所行的；我要记念你古时的奇事。
chapters.en: I will remember the works of the LORD: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.

chapters.zh: 我也要思想你的经营，默念你的作为。
chapters.en: I will meditate also of all thy work, and talk of thy doings.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你的作为是洁净的；有何神大如神呢？
chapters.en: Thy way, O God, in the sanctuary: who great a God as God?

chapters.zh: 你是行奇事的神；你曾在列邦中彰显你的能力。
chapters.en: Thou the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.

chapters.zh: 你曾用你的膀臂赎了你的民，就是雅各和约瑟的子孙。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou hast with arm redeemed thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，诸水见你，一见就都惊惶；深渊也都战抖。
chapters.en: The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid: the depths also were troubled.

chapters.zh: 云中倒出水来；天空发出响声；你的箭也飞行四方。
chapters.en: The clouds poured out water: the skies sent out a sound: thine arrows also went abroad.

chapters.zh: 你的雷声在旋风中；电光照亮世界；大地战抖震动。
chapters.en: The voice of thy thunder in the heaven: the lightnings lightened the world: the earth trembled and shook.

chapters.zh: 你的道在海中；你的路在大水中；你的脚踪无人知道。
chapters.en: Thy way in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.

chapters.zh: 你曾藉摩西和亚伦的手引导你的百姓，好像羊群一般。
chapters.en: Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的训诲诗。）我的民哪，你们要留心听我的训诲，侧耳听我口中的话。
chapters.en: [Maschil of Asaph.] Give ear, O my people, my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

chapters.zh: 我要开口说比喻；我要说出古时的谜语，
chapters.en: I will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:

chapters.zh: 是我们所听见、所知道的，也是我们的祖宗告诉我们的。
chapters.en: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

chapters.zh: 我们不将这些事向他们的子孙隐瞒，要将耶和华的美德和他的能力，并他奇妙的作为，述说给后代听。
chapters.en: We will not hide from their children, shewing to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.

chapters.zh: 因为，他在雅各中立法度，在以色列中设律法；是他吩咐我们祖宗要传给子孙的，
chapters.en: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children:

chapters.zh: 使将要生的后代子孙可以晓得；他们也要起来告诉他们的子孙，
chapters.en: That the generation to come might know the children should be born; should arise and declare to their children:

chapters.zh: 好叫他们仰望神，不忘记神的作为，惟要守他的命令。
chapters.en: That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:

chapters.zh: 不要像他们的祖宗，是顽梗悖逆、居心不正之辈，向著神，心不诚实。
chapters.en: And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation; a generation set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not stedfast with God.

chapters.zh: 以法莲的子孙带著兵器，拿著弓，临阵之日转身退后。
chapters.en: The children of Ephraim, armed, carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

chapters.zh: 他们不遵守神的约，不肯照他的律法行；
chapters.en: They kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;

chapters.zh: 又忘记他所行的和他显给他们奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: And forgat his works, and his wonders that he had shewed them.

chapters.zh: 他在埃及地，在琐安田，在他们祖宗的眼前施行奇事。
chapters.en: Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, the field of Zoan.

chapters.zh: 他将海分裂，使他们过去，又叫水立起如垒。
chapters.en: He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.

chapters.zh: 他白日用云彩，终夜用火光引导他们。
chapters.en: In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.

chapters.zh: 他在旷野分裂磐石，多多地给他们水喝，如从深渊而出。
chapters.en: He clave the rocks in the wilderness, and gave drink as the great depths.

chapters.zh: 他使水从磐石涌出，叫水如江河下流。
chapters.en: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

chapters.zh: 他们却仍旧得罪他，在干燥之地悖逆至高者。
chapters.en: And they sinned yet more against him by provoking the most High in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 他们心中试探神，随自己所欲的求食物，
chapters.en: And they tempted God in their heart by asking meat for their lust.

chapters.zh: 并且妄论神说：神在旷野岂能摆设筵席么？
chapters.en: Yea, they spake against God; they said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?

chapters.zh: 他曾击打磐石，使水涌出，成了江河；他还能赐粮食么？还能为他的百姓预备肉么？
chapters.en: Behold, he smote the rock, that the waters gushed out, and the streams overflowed; can he give bread also? can he provide flesh for his people?

chapters.zh: 所以，耶和华听见就发怒；有烈火向雅各烧起；有怒气向以色列上腾；
chapters.en: Therefore the LORD heard , and was wroth: so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;

chapters.zh: 因为他们不信服神，不倚赖他的救恩。
chapters.en: Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:

chapters.zh: 他却吩咐天空，又敞开天上的门，
chapters.en: Though he had commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven,

chapters.zh: 降吗哪，像雨给他们吃，将天上的粮食赐给他们。
chapters.en: And had rained down manna upon them to eat, and had given them of the corn of heaven.

chapters.zh: 各人（或译：人）吃大能者的食物；他赐下粮食，使他们饱足。
chapters.en: Man did eat angels' food: he sent them meat to the full.

chapters.zh: 他领东风起在天空，又用能力引了南风来。
chapters.en: He caused an east wind to blow in the heaven: and by his power he brought in the south wind.

chapters.zh: 他降肉，像雨在他们当中，多如尘土，又降飞鸟，多如海沙，
chapters.en: He rained flesh also upon them as dust, and feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea:

chapters.zh: 落在他们的营中，在他们住处的四面。
chapters.en: And he let fall in the midst of their camp, round about their habitations.

chapters.zh: 他们吃了，而且饱足；这样就随了他们所欲的。
chapters.en: So they did eat, and were well filled: for he gave them their own desire;

chapters.zh: 他们贪而无厌，食物还在他们口中的时候，
chapters.en: They were not estranged from their lust. But while their meat yet in their mouths,

chapters.zh: 神的怒气就向他们上腾，杀了他们内中的肥壮人，打倒以色列的少年人。
chapters.en: The wrath of God came upon them, and slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen of Israel.

chapters.zh: 虽是这样，他们仍旧犯罪，不信他奇妙的作为。
chapters.en: For all this they sinned still, and believed not for his wondrous works.

chapters.zh: 因此，他叫他们的日子全归虚空，叫他们的年岁尽属惊恐。
chapters.en: Therefore their days did he consume in vanity, and their years in trouble.

chapters.zh: 他杀他们的时候，他们才求问他，回心转意，切切地寻求神。
chapters.en: When he slew them, then they sought him: and they returned and enquired early after God.

chapters.zh: 他们也追念神是他们的磐石，至高的神是他们的救赎主。
chapters.en: And they remembered that God their rock, and the high God their redeemer.

chapters.zh: 他们却用口谄媚他，用舌向他说谎。
chapters.en: Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth, and they lied unto him with their tongues.

chapters.zh: 因他们的心向他不正，在他的约上也不忠心。
chapters.en: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they stedfast in his covenant.

chapters.zh: 但他有怜悯，赦免他们的罪孽，不灭绝他们，而且屡次消他的怒气，不发尽他的忿怒。
chapters.en: But he, full of compassion, forgave iniquity, and destroyed not: yea, many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his wrath.

chapters.zh: 他想到他们不过是血气，是一阵去而不返的风。
chapters.en: For he remembered that they flesh; a wind that passeth away, and cometh not again.

chapters.zh: 他们在旷野悖逆他，在荒地叫他担忧，何其多呢！
chapters.en: How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, grieve him in the desert!

chapters.zh: 他们再三试探神，惹动以色列的圣者。
chapters.en: Yea, they turned back and tempted God, and limited the Holy One of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他们不追念他的能力（原文是手）和赎他们脱离敌人的日子；
chapters.en: They remembered not his hand, the day when he delivered them from the enemy.

chapters.zh: 他怎样在埃及地显神迹，在琐安田显奇事，
chapters.en: How he had wrought his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the field of Zoan:

chapters.zh: 把他们的江河并河的水都变为血，使他们不能喝。
chapters.en: And had turned their rivers into blood; and their floods, that they could not drink.

chapters.zh: 他叫苍蝇成群落在他们当中，嘬尽他们，又叫青蛙灭了他们，
chapters.en: He sent divers sorts of flies among them, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

chapters.zh: 把他们的土产交给蚂蚱，把他们辛苦得来的交给蝗虫。
chapters.en: He gave also their increase unto the caterpiller, and their labour unto the locust.

chapters.zh: 他降冰雹打坏他们的葡萄树，下严霜打坏他们的桑树，
chapters.en: He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycomore trees with frost.

chapters.zh: 又把他们的牲畜交给冰雹，把他们的群畜交给闪电。
chapters.en: He gave up their cattle also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

chapters.zh: 他使猛烈的怒气和忿怒、恼恨、苦难成了一群降灾的使者，临到他们。
chapters.en: He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and trouble, by sending evil angels .

chapters.zh: 他为自己的怒气修平了路，将他们交给瘟疫，使他们死亡，
chapters.en: He made a way to his anger; he spared not their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence;

chapters.zh: 在埃及击杀一切长子，在含的帐棚中击杀他们强壮时头生的。
chapters.en: And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of strength in the tabernacles of Ham:

chapters.zh: 他却领出自己的民如羊，在旷野引他们如羊群。
chapters.en: But made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

chapters.zh: 他领他们稳稳妥妥地，使他们不致害怕；海却淹没他们的仇敌。
chapters.en: And he led them on safely, so that they feared not: but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

chapters.zh: 他带他们到自己圣地的边界，到他右手所得的这山地。
chapters.en: And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary, this mountain, his right hand had purchased.

chapters.zh: 他在他们面前赶出外邦人，用绳子将外邦的地量给他们为业，叫以色列支派的人住在他们的帐棚里。
chapters.en: He cast out the heathen also before them, and divided them an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

chapters.zh: 他们仍旧试探、悖逆至高的神，不守他的法度，
chapters.en: Yet they tempted and provoked the most high God, and kept not his testimonies:

chapters.zh: 反倒退后，行诡诈，像他们的祖宗一样；他们改变，如同翻背的弓。
chapters.en: But turned back, and dealt unfaithfully like their fathers: they were turned aside like a deceitful bow.

chapters.zh: 因他们的邱坛惹了他的怒气；因他们雕刻的偶像触动他的愤恨。
chapters.en: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their graven images.

chapters.zh: 神听见就发怒，极其憎恶以色列人。
chapters.en: When God heard , he was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel:

chapters.zh: 甚至他离弃示罗的帐幕，就是他在人间所搭的帐棚；
chapters.en: So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent he placed among men;

chapters.zh: 又将他的约柜（原文是能力）交与人掳去，将他的荣耀交在敌人手中；
chapters.en: And delivered his strength into captivity, and his glory into the enemy's hand.

chapters.zh: 并将他的百姓交与刀剑，向他的产业发怒。
chapters.en: He gave his people over also unto the sword; and was wroth with his inheritance.

chapters.zh: 少年人被火烧灭；处女也无喜歌。
chapters.en: The fire consumed their young men; and their maidens were not given to marriage.

chapters.zh: 祭司倒在刀下，寡妇却不哀哭。
chapters.en: Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation.

chapters.zh: 那时，主像世人睡醒，像勇士饮酒呼喊。
chapters.en: Then the Lord awaked as one out of sleep, like a mighty man that shouteth by reason of wine.

chapters.zh: 他就打退了他的敌人，叫他们永蒙羞辱；
chapters.en: And he smote his enemies in the hinder parts: he put them to a perpetual reproach.

chapters.zh: 并且他弃掉约瑟的帐棚，不拣选以法莲支派，
chapters.en: Moreover he refused the tabernacle of Joseph, and chose not the tribe of Ephraim:

chapters.zh: 却拣选犹大支派 ─ 他所喜爱的锡安山；
chapters.en: But chose the tribe of Judah, the mount Zion which he loved.

chapters.zh: 盖造他的圣所，好像高峰，又像他建立永存之地；
chapters.en: And he built his sanctuary like high , like the earth which he hath established for ever.

chapters.zh: 又拣选他的仆人大卫，从羊圈中将他召来，
chapters.en: He chose David also his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds:

chapters.zh: 叫他不再跟从那些带奶的母羊，为要牧养自己的百姓雅各和自己的产业以色列。
chapters.en: From following the ewes great with young he brought him to feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.

chapters.zh: 于是，他按心中的纯正牧养他们，用手中的巧妙引导他们。
chapters.en: So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗。）神啊，外邦人进入你的产业，污秽你的圣殿，使耶路撒冷变成荒堆，
chapters.en: [A Psalm of Asaph.] O God, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem on heaps.

chapters.zh: 把你仆人的尸首交与天空的飞鸟为食，把你圣民的肉交与地上的野兽，
chapters.en: The dead bodies of thy servants have they given meat unto the fowls of the heaven, the flesh of thy saints unto the beasts of the earth.

chapters.zh: 在耶路撒冷周围流他们的血如水，无人葬埋。
chapters.en: Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and none to bury .

chapters.zh: 我们成为邻国的羞辱，成为我们四围人的嗤笑讥刺。
chapters.en: We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，这到几时呢？你要动怒到永远么？你的愤恨要如火焚烧么？
chapters.en: How long, LORD? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy jealousy burn like fire?

chapters.zh: 愿你将你的忿怒倒在那不认识你的外邦和那不求告你名的国度。
chapters.en: Pour out thy wrath upon the heathen that have not known thee, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy name.

chapters.zh: 因为他们吞了雅各，把他的住处变为荒场。
chapters.en: For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his dwelling place.

chapters.zh: 求你不要记念我们先祖的罪孽，向我们追讨；愿你的慈悲快迎著我们，因为我们落到极卑微的地步。
chapters.en: O remember not against us former iniquities: let thy tender mercies speedily prevent us: for we are brought very low.

chapters.zh: 拯救我们的神啊，求你因你名的荣耀帮助我们！为你名的缘故搭救我们，赦免我们的罪。
chapters.en: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy name: and deliver us, and purge away our sins, for thy name's sake.

chapters.zh: 为何容外邦人说他们的神在哪里呢？愿你使外邦人知道你在我们眼前伸你仆人流血的冤。
chapters.en: Wherefore should the heathen say, Where their God? let him be known among the heathen in our sight the revenging of the blood of thy servants shed.

chapters.zh: 愿被囚之人的叹息达到你面前；愿你按你的大能力存留那些将要死的人。
chapters.en: Let the sighing of the prisoner come before thee; according to the greatness of thy power preserve thou those that are appointed to die;

chapters.zh: 主啊，愿你将我们邻邦所羞辱你的羞辱加七倍归到他们身上。
chapters.en: And render unto our neighbours sevenfold into their bosom their reproach, wherewith they have reproached thee, O Lord.

chapters.zh: 这样，你的民，你草场的羊，要称谢你，直到永远；要述说赞美你的话，直到万代。
chapters.en: So we thy people and sheep of thy pasture will give thee thanks for ever: we will shew forth thy praise to all generations.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗，交与伶长。调用为证的百合花。）领约瑟如领羊群之以色列的牧者啊，求你留心听！坐在二基路伯上的啊，求你发出光来！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Shoshannimeduth, A Psalm of Asaph.] Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that dwellest the cherubims, shine forth.

chapters.zh: 在以法莲、便雅悯、玛拿西前面施展你的大能，来救我们。
chapters.en: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh stir up thy strength, and come save us.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你使我们回转（或译：复兴），使你的脸发光，我们便要得救！
chapters.en: Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 万军之神啊，你向你百姓的祷告发怒，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: O LORD God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?

chapters.zh: 你以眼泪当食物给他们吃，又多量出眼泪给他们喝。
chapters.en: Thou feedest them with the bread of tears; and givest them tears to drink in great measure.

chapters.zh: 你使邻邦因我们纷争；我们的仇敌彼此戏笑。
chapters.en: Thou makest us a strife unto our neighbours: and our enemies laugh among themselves.

chapters.zh: 万军之神啊，求你使我们回转，使你的脸发光，我们便要得救！
chapters.en: Turn us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

chapters.zh: 你从埃及挪出一棵葡萄树，赶出外邦人，把这树栽上。
chapters.en: Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt: thou hast cast out the heathen, and planted it.

chapters.zh: 你在这树根前预备了地方，他就深深扎根，爬满了地。
chapters.en: Thou preparedst before it, and didst cause it to take deep root, and it filled the land.

chapters.zh: 他的影子遮满了山，枝子好像佳美的香柏树。
chapters.en: The hills were covered with the shadow of it, and the boughs thereof the goodly cedars.

chapters.zh: 他发出枝子，长到大海，发出蔓子，延到大河。
chapters.en: She sent out her boughs unto the sea, and her branches unto the river.

chapters.zh: 你为何拆毁这树的篱笆，任凭一切过路的人摘取？
chapters.en: Why hast thou broken down her hedges, so that all they which pass by the way do pluck her?

chapters.zh: 林中出来的野猪把他糟踏；野地的走兽拿他当食物。
chapters.en: The boar out of the wood doth waste it, and the wild beast of the field doth devour it.

chapters.zh: 万军之神啊，求你回转！从天上垂看，眷顾这葡萄树，
chapters.en: Return, we beseech thee, O God of hosts: look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine;

chapters.zh: 保护你右手所栽的和你为自已所坚固的枝子。
chapters.en: And the vineyard which thy right hand hath planted, and the branch thou madest strong for thyself.

chapters.zh: 这树已经被火焚烧，被刀砍伐；他们因你脸上的怒容就灭亡了。
chapters.en: burned with fire, cut down: they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.

chapters.zh: 愿你的手扶持你右边的人，就是你为自已所坚固的人子。
chapters.en: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand, upon the son of man thou madest strong for thyself.

chapters.zh: 这样，我们便不退后离开你；求你救活我们，我们就要求告你的名。
chapters.en: So will not we go back from thee: quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 万军之神啊，求你使我们回转，使你的脸发光，我们便要得救！
chapters.en: Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗，交与伶长。用迦特乐器。）你们当向神 ─ 我们的力量大声欢呼，向雅各的神发声欢乐！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Gittith, of Asaph.] Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 唱起诗歌，打手鼓，弹美琴与瑟。
chapters.en: Take a psalm, and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.

chapters.zh: 当在月朔并月望 ─ 我们过节的日期吹角，
chapters.en: Blow up the trumpet in the new moon, in the time appointed, on our solemn feast day.

chapters.zh: 因这是为以色列定的律例，是雅各神的典章。
chapters.en: For this a statute for Israel, a law of the God of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 他去攻击埃及地的时候，在约瑟中间立此为证。我在那里听见我所不明白的言语：
chapters.en: This he ordained in Joseph a testimony, when he went out through the land of Egypt: I heard a language I understood not.

chapters.zh: 神说：我使你的肩得脱重担，你的手放下筐子。
chapters.en: I removed his shoulder from the burden: his hands were delivered from the pots.

chapters.zh: 你在急难中呼求，我就搭救你；我在雷的隐密处应允你，在米利巴水那里试验你。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou calledst in trouble, and I delivered thee; I answered thee in the secret place of thunder: I proved thee at the waters of Meribah. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我的民哪，你当听，我要劝戒你；以色列啊，甚愿你肯听从我。
chapters.en: Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;

chapters.zh: 在你当中，不可有别的神；外邦的神，你也不可下拜。
chapters.en: There shall no strange god be in thee; neither shalt thou worship any strange god.

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你的神，曾把你从埃及地领上来；你要大大张口，我就给你充满。
chapters.en: I the LORD thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt: open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.

chapters.zh: 无奈，我的民不听我的声音；以色列全不理我。
chapters.en: But my people would not hearken to my voice; and Israel would none of me.

chapters.zh: 我便任凭他们心里刚硬，随自己的计谋而行。
chapters.en: So I gave them up unto their own hearts' lust: they walked in their own counsels.

chapters.zh: 甚愿我的民肯听从我，以色列肯行我的道，
chapters.en: Oh that my people had hearkened unto me, Israel had walked in my ways!

chapters.zh: 我便速速治服他们的仇敌，反手攻击他们的敌人。
chapters.en: I should soon have subdued their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.

chapters.zh: 恨耶和华的人必来投降，但他的百姓必永久长存。
chapters.en: The haters of the LORD should have submitted themselves unto him: but their time should have endured for ever.

chapters.zh: 他也必拿上好的麦子给他们吃，又拿从磐石出的蜂蜜叫他们饱足。
chapters.en: He should have fed them also with the finest of the wheat: and with honey out of the rock should I have satisfied thee.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗。）神站在有权力者的会中，在诸神中行审判，
chapters.en: [A Psalm of Asaph.] God standeth in the congregation of the mighty; he judgeth among the gods.

chapters.zh: 说：你们审判不秉公义，徇恶人的情面，要到几时呢？（细拉）
chapters.en: How long will ye judge unjustly, and accept the persons of the wicked? Selah.

chapters.zh: 你们当为贫寒的人和孤儿伸冤；当为困苦和穷乏的人施行公义。
chapters.en: Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.

chapters.zh: 当保护贫寒和穷乏的人，救他们脱离恶人的手。
chapters.en: Deliver the poor and needy: rid out of the hand of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 你们仍不知道，也不明白，在黑暗中走来走去；地的根基都摇动了。
chapters.en: They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness: all the foundations of the earth are out of course.

chapters.zh: 我曾说：你们是神，都是至高者的儿子。
chapters.en: I have said, Ye gods; and all of you children of the most High.

chapters.zh: 然而，你们要死，与世人一样，要仆倒，像王子中的一位。
chapters.en: But ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.

chapters.zh: 神啊，求你起来审判世界，因为你要得万邦为业。
chapters.en: Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.

chapters.zh: （亚萨的诗歌。）神啊，求你不要静默！神啊，求你不要闭口，也不要不作声！
chapters.en: [A Song Psalm of Asaph.] Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.

chapters.zh: 因为你的仇敌喧嚷，恨你的抬起头来。
chapters.en: For, lo, thine enemies make a tumult: and they that hate thee have lifted up the head.

chapters.zh: 他们同谋奸诈要害你的百姓，彼此商议要害你所隐藏的人。
chapters.en: They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.

chapters.zh: 他们说：来罢，我们将他们剪灭，使他们不再成国！使以色列的名不再被人记念！
chapters.en: They have said, Come, and let us cut them off from a nation; that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.

chapters.zh: 他们同心商议，彼此结盟，要抵挡你，
chapters.en: For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:

chapters.zh: 就是住帐棚的以东人和以实玛利人，摩押和夏甲人，
chapters.en: The tabernacles of Edom, and the Ishmaelites; of Moab, and the Hagarenes;

chapters.zh: 迦巴勒、亚扪，和亚玛力、非利士并推罗的居民。
chapters.en: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;

chapters.zh: 亚述也与他们连合；他们作罗得子孙的帮手。（细拉）
chapters.en: Assur also is joined with them: they have holpen the children of Lot. Selah.

chapters.zh: 求你待他们，如待米甸，如在基顺河待西西拉和耶宾一样。
chapters.en: Do unto them as the Midianites; as Sisera, as Jabin, at the brook of Kison:

chapters.zh: 他们在隐多珥灭亡，成了地上的粪土。
chapters.en: perished at Endor: they became dung for the earth.

chapters.zh: 求你叫他们的首领像俄立和西伊伯，叫他们的王子都像西巴和撒慕拿。
chapters.en: Make their nobles like Oreb, and like Zeeb: yea, all their princes as Zebah, and as Zalmunna:

chapters.zh: 他们说：我们要得神的住处，作为自己的产业。
chapters.en: Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.

chapters.zh: 我的神啊，求你叫他们像旋风的尘土，像风前的碎秸。
chapters.en: O my God, make them like a wheel; as the stubble before the wind.

chapters.zh: 火怎样焚烧树林，火燄怎样烧著山岭，
chapters.en: As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;

chapters.zh: 求你也照样用狂风追赶他们，用暴雨恐吓他们。
chapters.en: So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.

chapters.zh: 愿你使他们满面羞耻，好叫他们寻求你 ─ 耶和华的名！
chapters.en: Fill their faces with shame; that they may seek thy name, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 愿他们永远羞愧惊惶！愿他们惭愧灭亡！
chapters.en: Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish:

chapters.zh: 使他们知道：惟独你 ─ 名为耶和华的 ─ 是全地以上的至高者！
chapters.en: That may know that thou, whose name alone JEHOVAH, the most high over all the earth.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗，交与伶长。用迦特乐器。）万军之耶和华啊，你的居所何等可爱！
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician upon Gittith, A Psalm for the sons of Korah.] How amiable thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!

chapters.zh: 我羡慕渴想耶和华的院宇；我的心肠，我的肉体向永生神呼吁（或译：欢呼）。
chapters.en: My soul longeth, yea, even fainteth for the courts of the LORD: my heart and my flesh crieth out for the living God.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华 ─ 我的王，我的神啊，在你祭坛那里，麻雀为自己找著房屋，燕子为自己找著菢雏之窝。
chapters.en: Yea, the sparrow hath found an house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young, thine altars, O LORD of hosts, my King, and my God.

chapters.zh: 如此住在你殿中的便为有福！他们仍要赞美你。（细拉）
chapters.en: Blessed they that dwell in thy house: they will be still praising thee. Selah.

chapters.zh: 靠你有力量、心中想往锡安大道的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: Blessed the man whose strength in thee; in whose heart the ways .

chapters.zh: 他们经过流泪谷，叫这谷变为泉源之地；并有秋雨之福盖满了全谷。
chapters.en: passing through the valley of Baca make it a well; the rain also filleth the pools.

chapters.zh: 他们行走，力上加力，各人到锡安朝见神。
chapters.en: They go from strength to strength, in Zion appeareth before God.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 万军之神啊，求你听我的祷告！雅各的神啊，求你留心听！（细拉）
chapters.en: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer: give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.

chapters.zh: 神啊，你是我们的盾牌；求你垂顾观看你受膏者的面！
chapters.en: Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.

chapters.zh: 在你的院宇住一日，胜似在别处住千日；宁可在我神殿中看门，不愿住在恶人的帐棚里。
chapters.en: For a day in thy courts better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华 ─ 神是日头，是盾牌，要赐下恩惠和荣耀。他未尝留下一样好处不给那些行动正直的人。
chapters.en: For the LORD God a sun and shield: the LORD will give grace and glory: no good will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华啊，倚靠你的人便为有福！
chapters.en: O LORD of hosts, blessed the man that trusteth in thee.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗，交与伶长。）耶和华啊，你已经向你的地施恩，救回被掳的雅各。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah.] LORD, thou hast been favourable unto thy land: thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 你赦免了你百姓的罪孽，遮盖了他们一切的过犯。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你收转了所发的忿怒和你猛烈的怒气。
chapters.en: Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned from the fierceness of thine anger.

chapters.zh: 拯救我们的神啊，求你使我们回转，叫你的恼恨向我们止息。
chapters.en: Turn us, O God of our salvation, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.

chapters.zh: 你要向我们发怒到永远么？你要将你的怒气延留到万代么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?

chapters.zh: 你不再将我们救活，使你的百姓靠你欢喜么？
chapters.en: Wilt thou not revive us again: that thy people may rejoice in thee?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你使我们得见你的慈爱，又将你的救恩赐给我们。
chapters.en: Shew us thy mercy, O LORD, and grant us thy salvation.

chapters.zh: 我要听神 ─ 耶和华所说的话；因为他必应许将平安赐给他的百姓 ─ 他的圣民；他们却不可再转去妄行。
chapters.en: I will hear what God the LORD will speak: for he will speak peace unto his people, and to his saints: but let them not turn again to folly.

chapters.zh: 他的救恩诚然与敬畏他的人相近，叫荣耀住在我们的地上。
chapters.en: Surely his salvation nigh them that fear him; that glory may dwell in our land.

chapters.zh: 慈爱和诚实彼此相遇；公义和平安彼此相亲。
chapters.en: Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed .

chapters.zh: 诚实从地而生；公义从天而现。
chapters.en: Truth shall spring out of the earth; and righteousness shall look down from heaven.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必将好处赐给我们；我们的地也要多出土产。
chapters.en: Yea, the LORD shall give good; and our land shall yield her increase.

chapters.zh: 公义要行在他面前，叫他的脚踪成为可走的路。
chapters.en: Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set in the way of his steps.

chapters.zh: （大卫的祈祷。）耶和华啊，求你侧耳应允我，因我是困苦穷乏的。
chapters.en: [A Prayer of David.] Bow down thine ear, O LORD, hear me: for I poor and needy.

chapters.zh: 求你保存我的性命，因我是虔诚人。我的神啊，求你拯救这倚靠你的仆人！
chapters.en: Preserve my soul; for I holy: O thou my God, save thy servant that trusteth in thee.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你怜悯我，因我终日求告你。
chapters.en: Be merciful unto me, O Lord: for I cry unto thee daily.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你使仆人心里欢喜，因为我的心仰望你。
chapters.en: Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你本为良善，乐意饶恕人，有丰盛的慈爱赐给凡求告你的人。
chapters.en: For thou, Lord, good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你留心听我的祷告，垂听我恳求的声音。
chapters.en: Give ear, O LORD, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.

chapters.zh: 我在患难之日要求告你，因为你必应允我。
chapters.en: In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.

chapters.zh: 主啊，诸神之中没有可比你的；你的作为也无可比。
chapters.en: Among the gods none like unto thee, O Lord; neither like unto thy works.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你所造的万民都要来敬拜你；他们也要荣耀你的名。
chapters.en: All nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O Lord; and shall glorify thy name.

chapters.zh: 因你为大，且行奇妙的事；惟独你是神。
chapters.en: For thou great, and doest wondrous things: thou God alone.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你将你的道指教我；我要照你的真理行；求你使我专心敬畏你的名！
chapters.en: Teach me thy way, O LORD; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.

chapters.zh: 主 ─ 我的神啊，我要一心称赞你；我要荣耀你的名，直到永远。
chapters.en: I will praise thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart: and I will glorify thy name for evermore.

chapters.zh: 因为，你向我发的慈爱是大的；你救了我的灵魂免入极深的阴间。
chapters.en: For great thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest hell.

chapters.zh: 神啊，骄傲的人起来攻击我，又有一党强横的人寻索我的命；他们没有将你放在眼中。
chapters.en: O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你是有怜悯有恩典的神，不轻易发怒，并有丰盛的慈爱和诚实。
chapters.en: But thou, O Lord, a God full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering, and plenteous in mercy and truth.

chapters.zh: 求你向我转脸，怜恤我，将你的力量赐给仆人，救你婢女的儿子。
chapters.en: O turn unto me, and have mercy upon me; give thy strength unto thy servant, and save the son of thine handmaid.

chapters.zh: 求你向我显出恩待我的凭据，叫恨我的人看见便羞愧，因为你 ─ 耶和华帮助我，安慰我。
chapters.en: Shew me a token for good; that they which hate me may see , and be ashamed: because thou, LORD, hast holpen me, and comforted me.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗歌。）耶和华所立的根基在圣山上。
chapters.en: [A Psalm Song for the sons of Korah.] His foundation in the holy mountains.

chapters.zh: 他爱锡安的门，胜于爱雅各一切的住处。
chapters.en: The LORD loveth the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 神的城啊，有荣耀的事乃指著你说的。（细拉）
chapters.en: Glorious things are spoken of thee, O city of God. Selah.

chapters.zh: 我要提起拉哈伯和巴比伦人，是在认识我之中的；看哪，非利士和推罗并古实人，个个生在那里。
chapters.en: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to them that know me: behold Philistia, and Tyre, with Ethiopia; this was born there.

chapters.zh: 论到锡安，必说：这一个、那一个都生在其中，而且至高者必亲自坚立这城。
chapters.en: And of Zion it shall be said, This and that man was born in her: and the highest himself shall establish her.

chapters.zh: 当耶和华记录万民的时候，他要点出这一个生在那里。（细拉）
chapters.en: The LORD shall count, when he writeth up the people, this was born there. Selah.

chapters.zh: 歌唱的，跳舞的，都要说：我的泉源都在你里面。
chapters.en: As well the singers as the players on instruments : all my springs in thee.

chapters.zh: （可拉后裔的诗歌，就是以斯拉人希幔的训诲诗，交与伶长。调用麻哈拉利暗俄。）耶和华 ─ 拯救我的神啊，我昼夜在你面前呼吁。
chapters.en: [A Song Psalm for the sons of Korah, to the chief Musician upon Mahalath Leannoth, Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite.] O LORD God of my salvation, I have cried day night before thee:

chapters.zh: 愿我的祷告达到你面前；求你侧耳听我的呼求！
chapters.en: Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;

chapters.zh: 因为我心里满了患难；我的性命临近阴间。
chapters.en: For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.

chapters.zh: 我算和下坑的人同列，如同无力（或译：没有帮助）的人一样。
chapters.en: I am counted with them that go down into the pit: I am as a man no strength:

chapters.zh: 我被丢在死人中，好像被杀的人躺在坟墓里。他们是你不再记念的，与你隔绝了。
chapters.en: Free among the dead, like the slain that lie in the grave, whom thou rememberest no more: and they are cut off from thy hand.

chapters.zh: 你把我放在极深的坑里，在黑暗地方，在深处。
chapters.en: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.

chapters.zh: 你的忿怒重压我身；你用一切的波浪困住我。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thy wrath lieth hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted with all thy waves. Selah.

chapters.zh: 你把我所认识的隔在远处，使我为他们所憎恶；我被拘困，不得出来。
chapters.en: Thou hast put away mine acquaintance far from me; thou hast made me an abomination unto them: shut up, and I cannot come forth.

chapters.zh: 我的眼睛因困苦而干瘪。耶和华啊，我天天求告你，向你举手。
chapters.en: Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: LORD, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.

chapters.zh: 你岂要行奇事给死人看么？难道阴魂还能起来称赞你么？（细拉）
chapters.en: Wilt thou shew wonders to the dead? shall the dead arise praise thee? Selah.

chapters.zh: 岂能在坟墓里述说你的慈爱么？岂能在灭亡中述说你的信实么？
chapters.en: Shall thy lovingkindness be declared in the grave? thy faithfulness in destruction?

chapters.zh: 你的奇事岂能在幽暗里被知道么？你的公义岂能在忘记之地被知道么？
chapters.en: Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我呼求你；我早晨的祷告要达到你面前。
chapters.en: But unto thee have I cried, O LORD; and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你为何丢弃我？为何掩面不顾我？
chapters.en: LORD, why castest thou off my soul? hidest thou thy face from me?

chapters.zh: 我自幼受苦，几乎死亡；我受你的惊恐，甚至慌张。
chapters.en: I afflicted and ready to die from youth up: I suffer thy terrors I am distracted.

chapters.zh: 你的烈怒漫过我身；你的惊吓把我剪除。
chapters.en: Thy fierce wrath goeth over me; thy terrors have cut me off.

chapters.zh: 这些终日如水环绕我，一齐都来围困我。
chapters.en: They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.

chapters.zh: 你把我的良朋密友隔在远处，使我所认识的人进入黑暗里。
chapters.en: Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, mine acquaintance into darkness.

chapters.zh: （以斯拉人以探的训诲诗。）我要歌唱耶和华的慈爱，直到永远；我要用口将你的信实传与万代。
chapters.en: [Maschil of Ethan the Ezrahite.] I will sing of the mercies of the LORD for ever: with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.

chapters.zh: 因我曾说：你的慈悲必建立到永远；你的信实必坚立在天上。
chapters.en: For I have said, Mercy shall be built up for ever: thy faithfulness shalt thou establish in the very heavens.

chapters.zh: 我与我所拣选的人立了约，向我的仆人大卫起了誓：
chapters.en: I have made a covenant with my chosen, I have sworn unto David my servant,

chapters.zh: 我要建立你的后裔，直到永远；要建立你的宝座，直到万代。（细拉）
chapters.en: Thy seed will I establish for ever, and build up thy throne to all generations. Selah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，诸天要称赞你的奇事；在圣者的会中，要称赞你的信实。
chapters.en: And the heavens shall praise thy wonders, O LORD: thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.

chapters.zh: 在天空谁能比耶和华呢？神的众子中，谁能像耶和华呢？
chapters.en: For who in the heaven can be compared unto the LORD? among the sons of the mighty can be likened unto the LORD?

chapters.zh: 他在圣者的会中，是大有威严的神，比一切在他四围的更可畏惧。
chapters.en: God is greatly to be feared in the assembly of the saints, and to be had in reverence of all about him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 万军之神啊，哪一个大能者像你耶和华？你的信实是在你的四围。
chapters.en: O LORD God of hosts, who a strong LORD like unto thee? or to thy faithfulness round about thee?

chapters.zh: 你管辖海的狂傲；波浪翻腾，你就使他平静了。
chapters.en: Thou rulest the raging of the sea: when the waves thereof arise, thou stillest them.

chapters.zh: 你打碎了拉哈伯，似乎是已杀的人；你用有能的膀臂打散了你的仇敌。
chapters.en: Thou hast broken Rahab in pieces, as one that is slain; thou hast scattered thine enemies with thy strong arm.

chapters.zh: 天属你，地也属你；世界和其中所充满的都为你所建立。
chapters.en: The heavens thine, the earth also thine: the world and the fulness thereof, thou hast founded them.

chapters.zh: 南北为你所创造；他泊和黑门都因你的名欢呼。
chapters.en: The north and the south thou hast created them: Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy name.

chapters.zh: 你有大能的膀臂；你的手有力，你的右手也高举。
chapters.en: Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, high is thy right hand.

chapters.zh: 公义和公平是你宝座的根基；慈爱和诚实行在你前面。
chapters.en: Justice and judgment the habitation of thy throne: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.

chapters.zh: 知道向你欢呼的，那民是有福的！耶和华啊，他们在你脸上的末毼行走。
chapters.en: Blessed the people that know the joyful sound: they shall walk, O LORD, in the light of thy countenance.

chapters.zh: 他们因你的名终日欢乐，因你的公义得以高举。
chapters.en: In thy name shall they rejoice all the day: and in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.

chapters.zh: 你是他们力量的荣耀；因为你喜悦我们，我们的角必被高举。
chapters.en: For thou the glory of their strength: and in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.

chapters.zh: 我们的盾牌属耶和华；我们的王属以色列的圣者。
chapters.en: For the LORD our defence; and the Holy One of Israel our king.

chapters.zh: 当时，你在异象中晓谕你的圣民，说：我已把救助之力加在那有能者的身上；我高举那从民中所拣选的。
chapters.en: Then thou spakest in vision to thy holy one, and saidst, I have laid help upon mighty; I have exalted chosen out of the people.

chapters.zh: 我寻得我的仆人大卫，用我的圣膏膏他。
chapters.en: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:

chapters.zh: 我的手必使他坚立；我的膀臂也必坚固他。
chapters.en: With whom my hand shall be established: mine arm also shall strengthen him.

chapters.zh: 仇敌必不勒索他；凶恶之子也不苦害他。
chapters.en: The enemy shall not exact upon him; nor the son of wickedness afflict him.

chapters.zh: 我要在他面前打碎他的敌人，击杀那恨他的人。
chapters.en: And I will beat down his foes before his face, and plague them that hate him.

chapters.zh: 只是我的信实和我的慈爱要与他同在；因我的名，他的角必被高举。
chapters.en: But my faithfulness and my mercy with him: and in my name shall his horn be exalted.

chapters.zh: 我要使他的左手伸到海上，右手伸到河上。
chapters.en: I will set his hand also in the sea, and his right hand in the rivers.

chapters.zh: 他要称呼我说：你是我的父，是我的神，是拯救我的磐石。
chapters.en: He shall cry unto me, Thou my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.

chapters.zh: 我也要立他为长子，为世上最高的君王。
chapters.en: Also I will make him firstborn, higher than the kings of the earth.

chapters.zh: 我要为他存留我的慈爱，直到永远；我与他立的约必要坚定。
chapters.en: My mercy will I keep for him for evermore, and my covenant shall stand fast with him.

chapters.zh: 我也要使他的后裔存到永远，使他的宝座如天之久。
chapters.en: His seed also will I make for ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

chapters.zh: 倘若他的子孙离弃我的律法，不照我的典章行，
chapters.en: If his children forsake my law, and walk not in my judgments;

chapters.zh: 背弃我的律例，不遵守我的诫命，
chapters.en: If they break my statutes, and keep not my commandments;

chapters.zh: 我就要用杖责罚他们的过犯，用鞭责罚他们的罪孽。
chapters.en: Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

chapters.zh: 只是我必不将我的慈爱全然收回，也必不叫我的信实废弃。
chapters.en: Nevertheless my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him, nor suffer my faithfulness to fail.

chapters.zh: 我必不背弃我的约，也不改变我口中所出的。
chapters.en: My covenant will I not break, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips.

chapters.zh: 我一次指著自己的圣洁起誓：我决不向大卫说谎！
chapters.en: Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.

chapters.zh: 他的后裔要存到永远；他的宝座在我面前如日之恒一般，
chapters.en: His seed shall endure for ever, and his throne as the sun before me.

chapters.zh: 又如月亮永远坚立，如天上确实的见证。（细拉）
chapters.en: It shall be established for ever as the moon, and a faithful witness in heaven. Selah.

chapters.zh: 但你恼怒你的受膏者，就丢掉弃绝他。
chapters.en: But thou hast cast off and abhorred, thou hast been wroth with thine anointed.

chapters.zh: 你厌恶了与仆人所立的约，将他的冠冕践踏于地。
chapters.en: Thou hast made void the covenant of thy servant: thou hast profaned his crown to the ground.

chapters.zh: 你拆毁了他一切的篱笆，使他的保障变为荒场。
chapters.en: Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strong holds to ruin.

chapters.zh: 凡过路的人都抢夺他；他成为邻邦的羞辱。
chapters.en: All that pass by the way spoil him: he is a reproach to his neighbours.

chapters.zh: 你高举了他敌人的右手；你叫他一切的仇敌欢喜。
chapters.en: Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.

chapters.zh: 你叫他的刀剑卷刃，叫他在争战之中站立不住。
chapters.en: Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword, and hast not made him to stand in the battle.

chapters.zh: 你使他的光辉止息，将他的宝座推倒于地。
chapters.en: Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.

chapters.zh: 你减少他青年的日子，又使他蒙羞。（细拉）
chapters.en: The days of his youth hast thou shortened: thou hast covered him with shame. Selah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，这要到几时呢？你要将自己隐藏到永远么？你的忿怒如火焚烧要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long, LORD? wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?

chapters.zh: 求你想念我的时候是何等的短少；你创造世人，要使他们归何等的虚空呢？
chapters.en: Remember how short my time is: wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?

chapters.zh: 谁能常活免死、救他的灵魂脱离阴间的权柄呢？（细拉）
chapters.en: What man liveth, and shall not see death? shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你从前凭你的信实向大卫立誓要施行的慈爱在哪里呢？
chapters.en: Lord, where thy former lovingkindnesses, thou swarest unto David in thy truth?

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你记念仆人们所受的羞辱，记念我怎样将一切强盛民的羞辱存在我怀里。
chapters.en: Remember, Lord, the reproach of thy servants; I do bear in my bosom all the mighty people;

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的仇敌用这羞辱羞辱了你的仆人，羞辱了你受膏者的脚踪。
chapters.en: Wherewith thine enemies have reproached, O LORD; wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是应当称颂的，直到永远。阿们！阿们！
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD for evermore. Amen, and Amen.

chapters.zh: （神人摩西的祈祷）主啊，你世世代代作我们的居所。
chapters.en: [A Prayer of Moses the man of God.] Lord, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.

chapters.zh: 诸山未曾生出，地与世界你未曾造成，从亘古到永远，你是神。
chapters.en: Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, thou God.

chapters.zh: 你使人归于尘土，说：你们世人要归回。
chapters.en: Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.

chapters.zh: 在你看来，千年如已过的昨日，又如夜间的一更。
chapters.en: For a thousand years in thy sight as yesterday when it is past, and a watch in the night.

chapters.zh: 你叫他们如水冲去；他们如睡一觉。早晨，他们如生长的草，
chapters.en: Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are a sleep: in the morning like grass groweth up.

chapters.zh: 早晨发芽生长，晚上割下枯干。
chapters.en: In the morning it flourisheth, and groweth up; in the evening it is cut down, and withereth.

chapters.zh: 我们因你的怒气而消灭，因你的忿怒而惊惶。
chapters.en: For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.

chapters.zh: 你将我们的罪孽摆在你面前，将我们的隐恶摆在你面光之中。
chapters.en: Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret in the light of thy countenance.

chapters.zh: 我们经过的日子都在你震怒之下；我们度尽的年岁好像一声叹息。
chapters.en: For all our days are passed away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a tale .

chapters.zh: 我们一生的年日是七十岁，若是强壮可到八十岁；但其中所矜夸的不过是劳苦愁烦，转眼成空，我们便如飞而去。
chapters.en: The days of our years threescore years and ten; and if by reason of strength fourscore years, yet their strength labour and sorrow; for it is soon cut off, and we fly away.

chapters.zh: 谁晓得你怒气的权势？谁按著你该受的敬畏晓得你的忿怒呢？
chapters.en: Who knoweth the power of thine anger? even according to thy fear, thy wrath.

chapters.zh: 求你指教我们怎样数算自己的日子，好叫我们得著智慧的心。
chapters.en: So teach to number our days, that we may apply hearts unto wisdom.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我们要等到几时呢？求你转回，为你的仆人后悔。
chapters.en: Return, O LORD, how long? and let it repent thee concerning thy servants.

chapters.zh: 求你使我们早早饱得你的慈爱，好叫我们一生一世欢呼喜乐。
chapters.en: O satisfy us early with thy mercy; that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.

chapters.zh: 求你照著你使我们受苦的日子，和我们遭难的年岁，叫我们喜乐。
chapters.en: Make us glad according to the days thou hast afflicted us, the years we have seen evil.

chapters.zh: 愿你的作为向你仆人显现；愿你的荣耀向他们子孙显明。
chapters.en: Let thy work appear unto thy servants, and thy glory unto their children.

chapters.zh: 愿主 ─ 我们神的荣美归于我们身上。愿你坚立我们手所做的工；我们手所做的工，愿你坚立。
chapters.en: And let the beauty of the LORD our God be upon us: and establish thou the work of our hands upon us; yea, the work of our hands establish thou it.

chapters.zh: 住在至高者隐密处的，必住在全能者的荫下。
chapters.en: He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

chapters.zh: 我要论到耶和华说：他是我的避难所，是我的山寨，是我的神，是我所倚靠的。
chapters.en: I will say of the LORD, my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.

chapters.zh: 他必救你脱离捕鸟人的网罗和毒害的瘟疫。
chapters.en: Surely he shall deliver thee from the snare of the fowler, from the noisome pestilence.

chapters.zh: 他必用自己的翎毛遮蔽你；你要投靠在他的翅膀底下；他的诚实是大小的盾牌。
chapters.en: He shall cover thee with his feathers, and under his wings shalt thou trust: his truth shield and buckler.

chapters.zh: 你必不怕黑夜的惊骇，或是白日飞的箭，
chapters.en: Thou shalt not be afraid for the terror by night; for the arrow flieth by day;

chapters.zh: 也不怕黑夜行的瘟疫，或是午间灭人的毒病。
chapters.en: for the pestilence walketh in darkness; for the destruction wasteth at noonday.

chapters.zh: 虽有千人仆倒在你旁边，万人仆倒在你右边，这灾却不得临近你。
chapters.en: A thousand shall fall at thy side, and ten thousand at thy right hand; it shall not come nigh thee.

chapters.zh: 你惟亲眼观看，见恶人遭报。
chapters.en: Only with thine eyes shalt thou behold and see the reward of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的避难所；你已将至高者当你的居所，
chapters.en: Because thou hast made the LORD, my refuge, the most High, thy habitation;

chapters.zh: 祸患必不临到你，灾害也不挨近你的帐棚。
chapters.en: There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling.

chapters.zh: 因他要为你吩咐他的使者，在你行的一切道路上保护你。
chapters.en: For he shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee in all thy ways.

chapters.zh: 他们要用手托著你，免得你的脚碰在石头上。
chapters.en: They shall bear thee up in hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone.

chapters.zh: 你要踹在狮子和虺蛇的身上，践踏少壮狮子和大蛇。
chapters.en: Thou shalt tread upon the lion and adder: the young lion and the dragon shalt thou trample under feet.

chapters.zh: 神说：因为他专心爱我，我就要搭救他；因为他知道我的名，我要把他安置在高处。
chapters.en: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him on high, because he hath known my name.

chapters.zh: 他若求告我，我就应允他；他在急难中，我要与他同在；我要搭救他，使他尊贵。
chapters.en: He shall call upon me, and I will answer him: I with him in trouble; I will deliver him, and honour him.

chapters.zh: 我要使他足享长寿，将我的救恩显明给他。
chapters.en: With long life will I satisfy him, and shew him my salvation.

chapters.zh: （安息日的诗歌。）称谢耶和华！歌颂你至高者的名！
chapters.en: [A Psalm Song for the sabbath day.] good to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High:

chapters.zh: 用十弦的乐器和瑟，用琴弹幽雅的声音，早晨传扬你的慈爱；每夜传扬你的信实。这本为美事。
chapters.en: To shew forth thy lovingkindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness every night,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: Upon an instrument of ten strings, and upon the psaltery; upon the harp with a solemn sound.

chapters.zh: 因你 ─ 耶和华藉著你的作为叫我高兴，我要因你手的工作欢呼。
chapters.en: For thou, LORD, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的工作何其大！你的心思极其深！
chapters.en: O LORD, how great are thy works! thy thoughts are very deep.

chapters.zh: 畜类人不晓得；愚顽人也不明白。
chapters.en: A brutish man knoweth not; neither doth a fool understand this.

chapters.zh: 恶人茂盛如草，一切作孽之人发旺的时候，正是他们要灭亡，直到永远。
chapters.en: When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity do flourish; that they shall be destroyed for ever:

chapters.zh: 惟你 ─ 耶和华是至高，直到永远。
chapters.en: But thou, LORD, high for evermore.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的仇敌都要灭亡；一切作孽的也要离散。
chapters.en: For, lo, thine enemies, O LORD, for, lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.

chapters.zh: 你却高举了我的角，如野牛的角；我是被新油膏了的。
chapters.en: But my horn shalt thou exalt like an unicorn: I shall be anointed with fresh oil.

chapters.zh: 我眼睛看见仇敌遭报；我耳朵听见那些起来攻击我的恶人受罚。
chapters.en: Mine eye also shall see on mine enemies, mine ears shall hear of the wicked that rise up against me.

chapters.zh: 义人要发旺如棕树，生长如利巴嫩的香柏树。
chapters.en: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

chapters.zh: 他们栽于耶和华的殿中，发旺在我们神的院里。
chapters.en: Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.

chapters.zh: 他们年老的时候仍要结果子，要满了汁浆而常发青，
chapters.en: They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing;

chapters.zh: 好显明耶和华是正直的。他是我的磐石，在他毫无不义。
chapters.en: To shew that the LORD upright: my rock, and no unrighteousness in him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华作王！他以威严为衣穿上；耶和华以能力为衣，以能力束腰，世界就坚定，不得动摇。
chapters.en: The LORD reigneth, he is clothed with majesty; the LORD is clothed with strength, he hath girded himself: the world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved.

chapters.zh: 你的宝座从太初立定；你从亘古就有。
chapters.en: Thy throne established of old: thou from everlasting.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，大水扬起，大水发声，波浪澎湃。
chapters.en: The floods have lifted up, O LORD, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在高处大有能力，胜过诸水的响声，洋海的大浪。
chapters.en: The LORD on high mightier than the noise of many waters, the mighty waves of the sea.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的法度最的确；你的殿永称为圣，是合宜的。
chapters.en: Thy testimonies are very sure: holiness becometh thine house, O LORD, for ever.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你是伸冤的神；伸冤的神啊，求你发出光来！
chapters.en: O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongeth; O God, to whom vengeance belongeth, shew thyself.

chapters.zh: 审判世界的主啊，求你挺身而立，使骄傲人受应得的报应！
chapters.en: Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth: render a reward to the proud.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，恶人夸胜要到几时呢？要到几时呢？
chapters.en: LORD, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?

chapters.zh: 他们絮絮叨叨说傲慢的话；一切作孽的人都自己夸张。
chapters.en: shall they utter speak hard things? all the workers of iniquity boast themselves?

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，他们强压你的百姓，苦害你的产业。
chapters.en: They break in pieces thy people, O LORD, and afflict thine heritage.

chapters.zh: 他们杀死寡妇和寄居的，又杀害孤儿。
chapters.en: They slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless.

chapters.zh: 他们说：耶和华必不看见；雅各的神必不思念。
chapters.en: Yet they say, The LORD shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob regard .

chapters.zh: 你们民间的畜类人当思想；你们愚顽人到几时才有智慧呢？
chapters.en: Understand, ye brutish among the people: and fools, when will ye be wise?

chapters.zh: 造耳朵的，难道自己不听见么？造眼睛的，难道自己不看见么？
chapters.en: He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? he that formed the eye, shall he not see?

chapters.zh: 管教列邦的，就是叫人得知识的，难道自己不惩治人么？
chapters.en: He that chastiseth the heathen, shall not he correct? he that teacheth man knowledge, ?

chapters.zh: 耶和华知道人的意念是虚妄的。
chapters.en: The LORD knoweth the thoughts of man, that they vanity.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你所管教、用律法所教训的人是有福的！
chapters.en: Blessed the man whom thou chastenest, O LORD, and teachest him out of thy law;

chapters.zh: 你使他在遭难的日子得享平安；惟有恶人陷在所挖的坑中。
chapters.en: That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华必不丢弃他的百姓，也不离弃他的产业。
chapters.en: For the LORD will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.

chapters.zh: 审判要转向公义；心里正直的，必都随从。
chapters.en: But judgment shall return unto righteousness: and all the upright in heart shall follow it.

chapters.zh: 谁肯为我起来攻击作恶的？谁肯为我站起抵挡作孽的？
chapters.en: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?

chapters.zh: 若不是耶和华帮助我，我就住在寂静之中了。
chapters.en: Unless the LORD my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

chapters.zh: 我正说我失了脚，耶和华啊，那时你的慈爱扶助我。
chapters.en: When I said, My foot slippeth; thy mercy, O LORD, held me up.

chapters.zh: 我心里多忧多疑，你安慰我，就使我欢乐。
chapters.en: In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.

chapters.zh: 那藉著律例架弄残害、在位上行奸恶的，岂能与你相交么？
chapters.en: Shall the throne of iniquity have fellowship with thee, which frameth mischief by a law?

chapters.zh: 他们大家聚集攻击义人，将无辜的人定为死罪。
chapters.en: They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.

chapters.zh: 但耶和华向来作了我的高台；我的神作了我投靠的磐石。
chapters.en: But the LORD is my defence; and my God the rock of my refuge.

chapters.zh: 他叫他们的罪孽归到他们身上。他们正在行恶之中，他要剪除他们；耶和华 ─ 我们的神要把他们剪除。
chapters.en: And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; , the LORD our God shall cut them off.

chapters.zh: 来啊，我们要向耶和华歌唱，向拯救我们的磐石欢呼！
chapters.en: O come, let us sing unto the LORD: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.

chapters.zh: 我们要来感谢他，用诗歌向他欢呼！
chapters.en: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise unto him with psalms.

chapters.zh: 因耶和华为大神，为大王，超乎万神之上。
chapters.en: For the LORD a great God, and a great King above all gods.

chapters.zh: 地的深处在他手中；山的高峰也属他。
chapters.en: In his hand the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills his also.

chapters.zh: 海洋属他，是他造的；旱地也是他手造成的。
chapters.en: The sea his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry .

chapters.zh: 来啊，我们要屈身敬拜，在造我们的耶和华面前跪下。
chapters.en: O come, let us worship and bow down: let us kneel before the LORD our maker.

chapters.zh: 因为他是我们的神；我们是他草场的羊，是他手下的民。惟愿你们今天听他的话：
chapters.en: For he our God; and we the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. To day if ye will hear his voice,

chapters.zh: 你们不可硬著心，像当日在米利巴，就是在旷野的玛撒。
chapters.en: Harden not your heart, as in the provocation, as the day of temptation in the wilderness:

chapters.zh: 那时，你们的祖宗试我探我，并且观看我的作为。
chapters.en: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.

chapters.zh: 四十年之久，我厌烦那世代，说：这是心里迷糊的百姓，竟不晓得我的作为！
chapters.en: Forty years long was I grieved with generation, and said, It a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways:

chapters.zh: 所以，我在怒中起誓，说：他们断不可进入我的安息！
chapters.en: Unto whom I sware in my wrath that they should not enter into my rest.

chapters.zh: 你们要向耶和华唱新歌！全地都要向耶和华歌唱！
chapters.en: O sing unto the LORD a new song: sing unto the LORD, all the earth.

chapters.zh: 要向耶和华歌唱，称颂他的名！天天传扬他的救恩！
chapters.en: Sing unto the LORD, bless his name; shew forth his salvation from day to day.

chapters.zh: 在列邦中述说他的荣耀！在万民中述说他的奇事！
chapters.en: Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.

chapters.zh: 因耶和华为大，当受极大的赞美；他在万神之上，当受敬畏。
chapters.en: For the LORD great, and greatly to be praised: he to be feared above all gods.

chapters.zh: 外邦的神都属虚无；惟独耶和华创造诸天。
chapters.en: For all the gods of the nations idols: but the LORD made the heavens.

chapters.zh: 有尊荣和威严在他面前；有能力与华美在他圣所。
chapters.en: Honour and majesty before him: strength and beauty in his sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 民中的万族啊，你们要将荣耀、能力归给耶和华，都归给耶和华！
chapters.en: Give unto the LORD, O ye kindreds of the people, give unto the LORD glory and strength.

chapters.zh: 要将耶和华的名所当得的荣耀归给他，拿供物来进入他的院宇。
chapters.en: Give unto the LORD the glory his name: bring an offering, and come into his courts.

chapters.zh: 当以圣洁的（的：或译为）妆饰敬拜耶和华；全地要在他面前战抖！
chapters.en: O worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness: fear before him, all the earth.

chapters.zh: 人在列邦中要说：耶和华作王！世界就坚定，不得动摇；他要按公正审判众民。
chapters.en: Say among the heathen the LORD reigneth: the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved: he shall judge the people righteously.

chapters.zh: 愿天欢喜，愿地快乐！愿海和其中所充满的澎湃！
chapters.en: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof.

chapters.zh: 愿田和其中所有的都欢乐！那时，林中的树木都要在耶和华面前欢呼。
chapters.en: Let the field be joyful, and all that therein: then shall all the trees of the wood rejoice

chapters.zh: 因为他来了，他来要审判全地。他要按公义审判世界，按他的信实审判万民。
chapters.en: Before the LORD: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华作王！愿地快乐！愿众海岛欢喜！
chapters.en: The LORD reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad .

chapters.zh: 密云和幽暗在他的四围；公义和公平是他宝座的根基。
chapters.en: Clouds and darkness round about him: righteousness and judgment the habitation of his throne.

chapters.zh: 有烈火在他前头行，烧灭他四围的敌人。
chapters.en: A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.

chapters.zh: 他的闪电光照世界，大地看见便震动。
chapters.en: His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.

chapters.zh: 诸山见耶和华的面，就是全地之主的面，便消化如蜡。
chapters.en: The hills melted like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

chapters.zh: 诸天表明他的公义；万民看见他的荣耀。
chapters.en: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.

chapters.zh: 愿一切事奉雕刻的偶像、靠虚无之神自夸的，都蒙羞愧。万神哪，你们都当拜他。
chapters.en: Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols: worship him, all gods.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，锡安听见你的判断就欢喜；犹大的城邑（原文是女子）也都快乐。
chapters.en: Zion heard, and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为你 ─ 耶和华至高，超乎全地；你被尊崇，远超万神之上。
chapters.en: For thou, LORD, high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.

chapters.zh: 你们爱耶和华的，都当恨恶罪恶；他保护圣民的性命，搭救他们脱离恶人的手。
chapters.en: Ye that love the LORD, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints; he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 散布亮光是为义人；预备喜乐是为正直人。
chapters.en: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

chapters.zh: 你们义人当靠耶和华欢喜，称谢他可记念的圣名。
chapters.en: Rejoice in the LORD, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

chapters.zh: （一篇诗。）你们要向耶和华唱新歌！因为他行过奇妙的事；他的右手和圣臂施行救恩。
chapters.en: [A Psalm.] O sing unto the LORD a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.

chapters.zh: 耶和华发明了他的救恩，在列邦人眼前显出公义；
chapters.en: The LORD hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen.

chapters.zh: 记念他向以色列家所发的慈爱，所凭的信实。地的四极都看见我们神的救恩。
chapters.en: He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

chapters.zh: 全地都要向耶和华欢乐；要发起大声，欢呼歌颂！
chapters.en: Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all the earth: make a loud noise, and rejoice, and sing praise.

chapters.zh: 要用琴歌颂耶和华，用琴和诗歌的声音歌颂他！
chapters.en: Sing unto the LORD with the harp; with the harp, and the voice of a psalm.

chapters.zh: 用号和角声，在大君王耶和华面前欢呼！
chapters.en: With trumpets and sound of cornet make a joyful noise before the LORD, the King.

chapters.zh: 愿海和其中所充满的澎湃；世界和住在其间的也要发声。
chapters.en: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 愿大水拍手；愿诸山在耶和华面前一同欢呼；
chapters.en: Let the floods clap hands: let the hills be joyful together

chapters.zh: 因为他来要审判遍地。他要按公义审判世界，按公正审判万民。
chapters.en: Before the LORD; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.

chapters.zh: 耶和华作王；万民当战抖！他坐在二基路伯上，地当动摇。
chapters.en: The LORD reigneth; let the people tremble: he sitteth the cherubims; let the earth be moved.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在锡安为大；他超乎万民之上。
chapters.en: The LORD great in Zion; and he high above all the people.

chapters.zh: 他们当称赞他大而可畏的名；他本为圣！
chapters.en: Let them praise thy great and terrible name; it holy.

chapters.zh: 王有能力，喜爱公平，坚立公正，在雅各中施行公平和公义。
chapters.en: The king's strength also loveth judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.

chapters.zh: 你们当尊崇耶和华 ─ 我们的神，在他脚凳前下拜。他本为圣！
chapters.en: Exalt ye the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; he holy.

chapters.zh: 在他的祭司中有摩西和亚伦；在求告他名的人中有撒母耳。他们求告耶和华，他就应允他们。
chapters.en: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

chapters.zh: 他在云柱中对他们说话；他们遵守他的法度和他所赐给他们的律例。
chapters.en: He spake unto them in the cloudy pillar: they kept his testimonies, and the ordinance he gave them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我们的神啊，你应允他们；你是赦免他们的神，却按他们所行的报应他们。
chapters.en: Thou answeredst them, O LORD our God: thou wast a God that forgavest them, though thou tookest vengeance of their inventions.

chapters.zh: 你们要尊崇耶和华 ─ 我们的神，在他的圣山下拜，因为耶和华 ─ 我们的神本为圣！
chapters.en: Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy hill; for the LORD our God holy.

chapters.zh: （称谢诗。）普天下当向耶和华欢呼！
chapters.en: [A Psalm of praise.] Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all ye lands.

chapters.zh: 你们当乐意事奉耶和华，当来向他歌唱！
chapters.en: Serve the LORD with gladness: come before his presence with singing.

chapters.zh: 你们当晓得耶和华是神！我们是他造的，也是属他的；我们是他的民，也是他草场的羊。
chapters.en: Know ye that the LORD he God: he hath made us, and not we ourselves; his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

chapters.zh: 当称谢进入他的门；当赞美进入他的院。当感谢他，称颂他的名！
chapters.en: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, into his courts with praise: be thankful unto him, bless his name.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华本为善。他的慈爱存到永远；他的信实直到万代。
chapters.en: For the LORD good; his mercy everlasting; and his truth to all generations.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗）我要歌唱慈爱和公平；耶和华啊，我要向你歌颂！
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O LORD, will I sing.

chapters.zh: 我要用智慧行完全的道。你几时到我这里来呢？我要存完全的心行在我家中。
chapters.en: I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. O when wilt thou come unto me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.

chapters.zh: 邪僻的事，我都不摆在我眼前；悖逆人所做的事，我甚恨恶，不容沾在我身上。
chapters.en: I will set no wicked thing before mine eyes: I hate the work of them that turn aside; shall not cleave to me.

chapters.zh: 弯曲的心思，我必远离；一切的恶人（或译：恶事），我不认识。
chapters.en: A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know a wicked .

chapters.zh: 在暗中谗谤他邻居的，我必将他灭绝；眼目高傲、心里骄纵的，我必不容他。
chapters.en: Whoso privily slandereth his neighbour, him will I cut off: him that hath an high look and a proud heart will not I suffer.

chapters.zh: 我眼要看国中的诚实人，叫他们与我同住；行为完全的，他要伺候我。
chapters.en: Mine eyes upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me: he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.

chapters.zh: 行诡诈的，必不得住在我家里；说谎话的，必不得立在我眼前。
chapters.en: He that worketh deceit shall not dwell within my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.

chapters.zh: 我每日早晨要灭绝国中所有的恶人，好把一切作孽的从耶和华的城里剪除。
chapters.en: I will early destroy all the wicked of the land; that I may cut off all wicked doers from the city of the LORD.

chapters.zh: （困苦人发昏的时候，在耶和华面前吐露苦情的祷告。）耶和华啊，求你听我的祷告，容我的呼求达到你面前！
chapters.en: [A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed, and poureth out his complaint before the LORD.] Hear my prayer, O LORD, and let my cry come unto thee.

chapters.zh: 我在急难的日子，求你向我侧耳；不要向我掩面！我呼求的日子，求你快快应允我！
chapters.en: Hide not thy face from me in the day I am in trouble; incline thine ear unto me: in the day I call answer me speedily.

chapters.zh: 因为，我的年日如烟云消灭；我的骨头如火把烧著。
chapters.en: For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are burned as an hearth.

chapters.zh: 我的心被伤，如草枯干，甚至我忘记吃饭。
chapters.en: My heart is smitten, and withered like grass; so that I forget to eat my bread.

chapters.zh: 因我唉哼的声音，我的肉紧贴骨头。
chapters.en: By reason of the voice of my groaning my bones cleave to my skin.

chapters.zh: 我如同旷野的鹈鹕；我好像荒场的鸮鸟。
chapters.en: I am like a pelican of the wilderness: I am like an owl of the desert.

chapters.zh: 我警醒不睡；我像房顶上孤单的麻雀。
chapters.en: I watch, and am as a sparrow alone upon the house top.

chapters.zh: 我的仇敌终日辱骂我；向我猖狂的人指著我赌咒。
chapters.en: Mine enemies reproach me all the day; they that are mad against me are sworn against me.

chapters.zh: 我吃过炉灰，如同吃饭；我所喝的与眼泪搀杂。
chapters.en: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,

chapters.zh: 这都因你的恼恨和忿怒；你把我拾起来，又把我摔下去。
chapters.en: Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.

chapters.zh: 我的年日如日影偏斜；我也如草枯干。
chapters.en: My days like a shadow that declineth; and I am withered like grass.

chapters.zh: 惟你 ─ 耶和华必存到永远；你可记念的名也存到万代。
chapters.en: But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.

chapters.zh: 你必起来怜恤锡安，因现在是可怜他的时候，日期已经到了。
chapters.en: Thou shalt arise, have mercy upon Zion: for the time to favour her, yea, the set time, is come.

chapters.zh: 你的仆人原来喜悦他的石头，可怜他的尘土。
chapters.en: For thy servants take pleasure in her stones, and favour the dust thereof.

chapters.zh: 列国要敬畏耶和华的名；世上诸王都敬畏你的荣耀。
chapters.en: So the heathen shall fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华建造了锡安，在他荣耀里显现。
chapters.en: When the LORD shall build up Zion, he shall appear in his glory.

chapters.zh: 他垂听穷人的祷告，并不藐视他们的祈求。
chapters.en: He will regard the prayer of the destitute, and not despise their prayer.

chapters.zh: 这必为后代的人记下，将来受造的民要赞美耶和华。
chapters.en: This shall be written for the generation to come: and the people which shall be created shall praise the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为，他从至高的圣所垂看；耶和华从天向地观察，
chapters.en: For he hath looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from heaven did the LORD behold the earth;

chapters.zh: 要垂听被囚之人的叹息，要释放将要死的人，
chapters.en: To hear the groaning of the prisoner; to loose those that are appointed to death;

chapters.zh: 使人在锡安传扬耶和华的名，在耶路撒冷传扬赞美他的话，
chapters.en: To declare the name of the LORD in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem;

chapters.zh: 就是在万民和列国聚会事奉耶和华的时候。
chapters.en: When the people are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他使我的力量中道衰弱，使我的年日短少。
chapters.en: He weakened my strength in the way; he shortened my days.

chapters.zh: 我说：我的神啊，不要使我中年去世。你的年数世世无穷！
chapters.en: I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years throughout all generations.

chapters.zh: 你起初立了地的根基；天也是你手所造的。
chapters.en: Of old hast thou laid the foundation of the earth: and the heavens the work of thy hands.

chapters.zh: 天地都要灭没，你却要长存；天地都要如外衣渐渐旧了。你要将天地如里衣更换，天地就改变了。
chapters.en: They shall perish, but thou shalt endure: yea, all of them shall wax old like a garment; as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed:

chapters.zh: 惟有你永不改变；你的年数没有穷尽。
chapters.en: But thou the same, and thy years shall have no end.

chapters.zh: 你仆人的子孙要长存；他们的后裔要坚立在你面前。
chapters.en: The children of thy servants shall continue, and their seed shall be established before thee.
