chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）我的心哪，你要称颂耶和华！凡在我里面的，也要称颂他的圣名！
chapters.en: [ of David.] Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, his holy name.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你要称颂耶和华！不可忘记他的一切恩惠！
chapters.en: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits:

chapters.zh: 他赦免你的一切罪孽，医治你的一切疾病。
chapters.en: Who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases;

chapters.zh: 他救赎你的命脱离死亡，以仁爱和慈悲为你的冠冕。
chapters.en: Who redeemeth thy life from destruction; who crowneth thee with lovingkindness and tender mercies;

chapters.zh: 他用美物使你所愿的得以知足，以致你如鹰返老还童。
chapters.en: Who satisfieth thy mouth with good thy youth is renewed like the eagle's.

chapters.zh: 耶和华施行公义，为一切受屈的人伸冤。
chapters.en: The LORD executeth righteousness and judgment for all that are oppressed.

chapters.zh: 他使摩西知道他的法则，叫以色列人晓得他的作为。
chapters.en: He made known his ways unto Moses, his acts unto the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 耶和华有怜悯，有恩典，不轻易发怒，且有丰盛的慈爱。
chapters.en: The LORD merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.

chapters.zh: 他不长久责备，也不永远怀怒。
chapters.en: He will not always chide: neither will he keep for ever.

chapters.zh: 他没有按我们的罪过待我们，也没有照我们的罪孽报应我们。
chapters.en: He hath not dealt with us after our sins; nor rewarded us according to our iniquities.

chapters.zh: 天离地何等的高，他的慈爱向敬畏他的人也是何等的大！
chapters.en: For as the heaven is high above the earth, great is his mercy toward them that fear him.

chapters.zh: 东离西有多远，他叫我们的过犯离我们也有多远！
chapters.en: As far as the east is from the west, far hath he removed our transgressions from us.

chapters.zh: 父亲怎样怜恤他的儿女，耶和华也怎样怜恤敬畏他的人！
chapters.en: Like as a father pitieth children, the LORD pitieth them that fear him.

chapters.zh: 因为他知道我们的本体，思念我们不过是尘土。
chapters.en: For he knoweth our frame; he remembereth that we dust.

chapters.zh: 至于世人，他的年日如草一样。他发旺如野地的花，
chapters.en: man, his days as grass: as a flower of the field, so he flourisheth.

chapters.zh: 经风一吹，便归无有；他的原处也不再认识他。
chapters.en: For the wind passeth over it, and it is gone; and the place thereof shall know it no more.

chapters.zh: 但耶和华的慈爱归于敬畏他的人，从亘古到永远；他的公义也归于子子孙孙
chapters.en: But the mercy of the LORD from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children's children;

chapters.zh: 就是那些遵守他的约、记念他的训词而遵行的人。
chapters.en: To such as keep his covenant, and to those that remember his commandments to do them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在天上立定宝座；他的权柄（原文是国）统管万有。
chapters.en: The LORD hath prepared his throne in the heavens; and his kingdom ruleth over all.

chapters.zh: 听从他命令、成全他旨意、有大能的天使，都要称颂耶和华！
chapters.en: Bless the LORD, ye his angels, that excel in strength, that do his commandments, hearkening unto the voice of his word.

chapters.zh: 你们作他的诸军，作他的仆役，行他所喜悦的，都要称颂耶和华！
chapters.en: Bless ye the LORD, all his hosts; ministers of his, that do his pleasure.

chapters.zh: 你们一切被他造的，在他所治理的各处，都要称颂耶和华！我的心哪，你要称颂耶和华！
chapters.en: Bless the LORD, all his works in all places of his dominion: bless the LORD, O my soul.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪，你要称颂耶和华！耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，你为至大！你以尊荣威严为衣服，
chapters.en: Bless the LORD, O my soul. O LORD my God, thou art very great; thou art clothed with honour and majesty.

chapters.zh: 披上亮光，如披外袍，铺张穹苍，如铺幔子，
chapters.en: Who coverest with light as a garment: who stretchest out the heavens like a curtain:

chapters.zh: 在水中立楼阁的栋梁，用云彩为车辇，藉著风的翅膀而行，
chapters.en: Who layeth the beams of his chambers in the waters: who maketh the clouds his chariot: who walketh upon the wings of the wind:

chapters.zh: 以风为使者，以火燄为仆役，
chapters.en: Who maketh his angels spirits; his ministers a flaming fire:

chapters.zh: 将地立在根基上，使地永不动摇。
chapters.en: laid the foundations of the earth, it should not be removed for ever.

chapters.zh: 你用深水遮盖地面，犹如衣裳；诸水高过山岭。
chapters.en: Thou coveredst it with the deep as a garment: the waters stood above the mountains.

chapters.zh: 你的斥责一发，水便奔逃；你的雷声一发，水便奔流。
chapters.en: At thy rebuke they fled; at the voice of thy thunder they hasted away.

chapters.zh: 诸山升上，诸谷沉下（或译：随山上翻，随谷下流），归你为他所安定之地。
chapters.en: They go up by the mountains; they go down by the valleys unto the place which thou hast founded for them.

chapters.zh: 你定了界限，使水不能过去，不再转回遮盖地面。
chapters.en: Thou hast set a bound that they may not pass over; that they turn not again to cover the earth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华使泉源涌在山谷，流在山间，
chapters.en: He sendeth the springs into the valleys, run among the hills.

chapters.zh: 使野地的走兽有水喝，野驴得解其渴。
chapters.en: They give drink to every beast of the field: the wild asses quench their thirst.

chapters.zh: 天上的飞鸟在水旁住宿，在树枝上啼叫。
chapters.en: By them shall the fowls of the heaven have their habitation, sing among the branches.

chapters.zh: 他从楼阁中浇灌山岭；因他作为的功效，地就丰足。
chapters.en: He watereth the hills from his chambers: the earth is satisfied with the fruit of thy works.

chapters.zh: 他使草生长，给六畜吃，使菜蔬发长，供给人用，使人从地里能得食物，
chapters.en: He causeth the grass to grow for the cattle, and herb for the service of man: that he may bring forth food out of the earth;

chapters.zh: 又得酒能悦人心，得油能润人面，得粮能养人心。
chapters.en: And wine maketh glad the heart of man, oil to make face to shine, and bread strengtheneth man's heart.

chapters.zh: 佳美的树木，就是利巴嫩的香柏树，是耶和华所栽种的，都满了汁浆。
chapters.en: The trees of the LORD are full ; the cedars of Lebanon, which he hath planted;

chapters.zh: 雀鸟在其上搭窝；至于鹤，松树是他的房屋。
chapters.en: Where the birds make their nests: the stork, the fir trees her house.

chapters.zh: 高山为野山羊的住所；岩石为沙番的藏处。
chapters.en: The high hills a refuge for the wild goats; the rocks for the conies.

chapters.zh: 你安置月亮为定节令；日头自知沉落。
chapters.en: He appointed the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down.

chapters.zh: 你造黑暗为夜，林中的百兽就都爬出来。
chapters.en: Thou makest darkness, and it is night: wherein all the beasts of the forest do creep .

chapters.zh: 少壮狮子吼叫，要抓食，向神寻求食物。
chapters.en: The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their meat from God.

chapters.zh: 日头一出，兽便躲避，卧在洞里。
chapters.en: The sun ariseth, they gather themselves together, and lay them down in their dens.

chapters.zh: 人出去做工，劳碌直到晚上。
chapters.en: Man goeth forth unto his work and to his labour until the evening.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你所造的何其多！都是你用智慧造成的；遍地满了你的丰富。
chapters.en: O LORD, how manifold are thy works! in wisdom hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches.

chapters.zh: 那里有海，又大又广；其中有无数的动物，大小活物都有。
chapters.en: this great and wide sea, wherein things creeping innumerable, both small and great beasts.

chapters.zh: 那里有船行走，有你所造的鳄鱼游泳在其中。
chapters.en: There go the ships: that leviathan, thou hast made to play therein.

chapters.zh: 这都仰望你按时给他食物。
chapters.en: These wait all upon thee; that thou mayest give their meat in due season.

chapters.zh: 你给他们，他们便拾起来；你张手，他们饱得美食。
chapters.en: thou givest them they gather: thou openest thine hand, they are filled with good.

chapters.zh: 你掩面，他们便惊惶；你收回他们的气，他们就死亡，归于尘土。
chapters.en: Thou hidest thy face, they are troubled: thou takest away their breath, they die, and return to their dust.

chapters.zh: 你发出你的灵，他们便受造；你使地面更换为新。
chapters.en: Thou sendest forth thy spirit, they are created: and thou renewest the face of the earth.

chapters.zh: 愿耶和华的荣耀存到永远！愿耶和华喜悦自己所造的！
chapters.en: The glory of the LORD shall endure for ever: the LORD shall rejoice in his works.

chapters.zh: 他看地，地便震动；他摸山，山就冒烟。
chapters.en: He looketh on the earth, and it trembleth: he toucheth the hills, and they smoke.

chapters.zh: 我要一生向耶和华唱诗！我还活的时候，要向我神歌颂！
chapters.en: I will sing unto the LORD as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.

chapters.zh: 愿他以我的默念为甘甜！我要因耶和华欢喜！
chapters.en: My meditation of him shall be sweet: I will be glad in the LORD.

chapters.zh: 愿罪人从世上消灭！愿恶人归于无有！我的心哪，要称颂耶和华！你们要赞美耶和华（原文是哈利路亚；下同）！
chapters.en: Let the sinners be consumed out of the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless thou the LORD, O my soul. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要称谢耶和华，求告他的名，在万民中传扬他的作为！
chapters.en: O give thanks unto the LORD; call upon his name: make known his deeds among the people.

chapters.zh: 要向他唱诗歌颂，谈论他一切奇妙的作为！
chapters.en: Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him: talk ye of all his wondrous works.

chapters.zh: 要以他的圣名夸耀！寻求耶和华的人，心中应当欢喜！
chapters.en: Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the LORD.

chapters.zh: 要寻求耶和华与他的能力，时常寻求他的面。
chapters.en: Seek the LORD, and his strength: seek his face evermore.

chapters.zh: 他仆人亚伯拉罕的后裔，他所拣选雅各的子孙哪，你们要记念他奇妙的作为和他的奇事，并他口中的判语。
chapters.en: Remember his marvellous works that he hath done; his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: O ye seed of Abraham his servant, ye children of Jacob his chosen.

chapters.zh: 他是耶和华 ─ 我们的神；全地都有他的判断。
chapters.en: He the LORD our God: his judgments in all the earth.

chapters.zh: 他记念他的约，直到永远；他所吩咐的话，直到千代 ─
chapters.en: He hath remembered his covenant for ever, the word he commanded to a thousand generations.

chapters.zh: 就是与亚伯拉罕所立的约，向以撒所起的誓。
chapters.en: Which he made with Abraham, and his oath unto Isaac;

chapters.zh: 他又将这约向雅各定为律例，向以色列定为永远的约，
chapters.en: And confirmed the same unto Jacob for a law, to Israel an everlasting covenant:

chapters.zh: 说：我必将迦南地赐给你，作你产业的分。
chapters.en: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance:

chapters.zh: 当时，他们人丁有限，数目稀少，并且在那地为寄居的。
chapters.en: When they were a few men in number; yea, very few, and strangers in it.

chapters.zh: 他们从这邦游到那邦，从这国行到那国。
chapters.en: When they went from one nation to another, from kingdom to another people;

chapters.zh: 他不容甚么人欺负他们，为他们的缘故责备君王，
chapters.en: He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes;

chapters.zh: 说：不可难为我受膏的人，也不可恶待我的先知。
chapters.en: , Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.

chapters.zh: 他命饥荒降在那地上，将所倚靠的粮食全行断绝，
chapters.en: Moreover he called for a famine upon the land: he brake the whole staff of bread.

chapters.zh: 在他们以先打发一个人去 ─ 约瑟被卖为奴仆。
chapters.en: He sent a man before them, Joseph, was sold for a servant:

chapters.zh: 人用脚镣伤他的脚；他被铁炼捆拘。
chapters.en: Whose feet they hurt with fetters: he was laid in iron:

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话试炼他，直等到他所说的应验了。
chapters.en: Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him.

chapters.zh: 王打发人把他解开，就是治理众民的，把他释放，
chapters.en: The king sent and loosed him; the ruler of the people, and let him go free.

chapters.zh: 立他作王家之主，掌管他一切所有的，
chapters.en: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his substance:

chapters.zh: 使他随意捆绑他的臣宰，将智慧教导他的长老。
chapters.en: To bind his princes at his pleasure; and teach his senators wisdom.

chapters.zh: 以色列也到了埃及；雅各在含地寄居。
chapters.en: Israel also came into Egypt; and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.

chapters.zh: 耶和华使他的百姓生养众多，使他们比敌人强盛，
chapters.en: And he increased his people greatly; and made them stronger than their enemies.

chapters.zh: 使敌人的心转去恨他的百姓，并用诡计待他的仆人。
chapters.en: He turned their heart to hate his people, to deal subtilly with his servants.

chapters.zh: 他打发他的仆人摩西和他所拣选的亚伦，
chapters.en: He sent Moses his servant; Aaron whom he had chosen.

chapters.zh: 在敌人中间显他的神迹，在含地显他的奇事。
chapters.en: They shewed his signs among them, and wonders in the land of Ham.

chapters.zh: 他命黑暗，就有黑暗；没有违背他话的。
chapters.en: He sent darkness, and made it dark; and they rebelled not against his word.

chapters.zh: 他叫埃及的水变为血，叫他们的鱼死了。
chapters.en: He turned their waters into blood, and slew their fish.

chapters.zh: 在他们的地上以及王宫的内室，青蛙多多滋生。
chapters.en: Their land brought forth frogs in abundance, in the chambers of their kings.

chapters.zh: 他说一声，苍蝇就成群而来，并有虱子进入他们四境。
chapters.en: He spake, and there came divers sorts of flies, lice in all their coasts.

chapters.zh: 他给他们降下冰雹为雨，在他们的地上降下火燄。
chapters.en: He gave them hail for rain, flaming fire in their land.

chapters.zh: 他也击打他们的葡萄树和无花果树，毁坏他们境内的树木。
chapters.en: He smote their vines also and their fig trees; and brake the trees of their coasts.

chapters.zh: 他说一声，就有蝗虫蚂蚱上来，不计其数，
chapters.en: He spake, and the locusts came, and caterpillers, and that without number,

chapters.zh: 吃尽了他们地上各样的菜蔬和田地的出产。
chapters.en: And did eat up all the herbs in their land, and devoured the fruit of their ground.

chapters.zh: 他又击杀他们国内一切的长子，就是他们强壮时头生的。
chapters.en: He smote also all the firstborn in their land, the chief of all their strength.

chapters.zh: 他领自己的百姓带银子金子出来；他支派中没有一个软弱的。
chapters.en: He brought them forth also with silver and gold: and not one feeble among their tribes.

chapters.zh: 他们出来的时候，埃及人便欢喜；原来埃及人惧怕他们。
chapters.en: Egypt was glad when they departed: for the fear of them fell upon them.

chapters.zh: 他铺张云彩当遮盖，夜间使火光照。
chapters.en: He spread a cloud for a covering; and fire to give light in the night.

chapters.zh: 他们一求，他就使鹌鹑飞来，并用天上的粮食叫他们饱足。
chapters.en: asked, and he brought quails, and satisfied them with the bread of heaven.

chapters.zh: 他打开磐石，水就涌出；在干旱之处，水流成河。
chapters.en: He opened the rock, and the waters gushed out; they ran in the dry places a river.

chapters.zh: 这都因他记念他的圣言和他的仆人亚伯拉罕。
chapters.en: For he remembered his holy promise, Abraham his servant.

chapters.zh: 他带领百姓欢乐而出，带领选民欢呼前往。
chapters.en: And he brought forth his people with joy, his chosen with gladness:

chapters.zh: 他将列国的地赐给他们，他们便承巡蛦民劳碌得来的，
chapters.en: And gave them the lands of the heathen: and they inherited the labour of the people;

chapters.zh: 好使他们遵他的律例，守他的律法。你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: That they might observe his statutes, and keep his laws. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要赞美耶和华！要称谢耶和华，因他本为善；他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for good: for his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 谁能传说耶和华的大能？谁能表明他一切的美德？
chapters.en: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? can shew forth all his praise?

chapters.zh: 凡遵守公平 ﹔ 常行公义的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: Blessed they that keep judgment, he that doeth righteousness at all times.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你用恩惠待你的百姓；求你也用这恩惠记念我，开你的救恩眷顾我，
chapters.en: Remember me, O LORD, with the favour thy people: O visit me with thy salvation;

chapters.zh: 使我见你选民的福，乐你国民的乐，与你的产业一同夸耀。
chapters.en: That I may see the good of thy chosen, that I may rejoice in the gladness of thy nation, that I may glory with thine inheritance.

chapters.zh: 我们与我们的祖宗一同犯罪；我们作了孽，行了恶。
chapters.en: We have sinned with our fathers, we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.

chapters.zh: 我们的祖宗在埃及不明白你的奇事，不记念你丰盛的慈爱，反倒在红海行了悖逆。
chapters.en: Our fathers understood not thy wonders in Egypt; they remembered not the multitude of thy mercies; but provoked at the sea, at the Red sea.

chapters.zh: 然而，他因自己的名拯救他们，为要彰显他的大能，
chapters.en: Nevertheless he saved them for his name's sake, that he might make his mighty power to be known.

chapters.zh: 并且斥责红海，海便干了；他带领他们经过深处，如同经过旷野。
chapters.en: He rebuked the Red sea also, and it was dried up: so he led them through the depths, as through the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 他拯救他们脱离恨他们人的手，从仇敌手中救赎他们。
chapters.en: And he saved them from the hand of him that hated , and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.

chapters.zh: 水淹没他们的敌人，没有一个存留。
chapters.en: And the waters covered their enemies: there was not one of them left.

chapters.zh: 那时，他们才信了他的话，歌唱赞美他。
chapters.en: Then believed they his words; they sang his praise.

chapters.zh: 等不多时，他们就忘了他的作为，不仰望他的指教，
chapters.en: They soon forgat his works; they waited not for his counsel:

chapters.zh: 反倒在旷野大起欲心，在荒地试探神。
chapters.en: But lusted exceedingly in the wilderness, and tempted God in the desert.

chapters.zh: 他将他们所求的赐给他们，却使他们的心灵软弱。
chapters.en: And he gave them their request; but sent leanness into their soul.

chapters.zh: 他们又在营中嫉妒摩西和耶和华的圣者亚伦。
chapters.en: They envied Moses also in the camp, Aaron the saint of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 地裂开，吞下大坍，掩盖亚比兰一党的人。
chapters.en: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, and covered the company of Abiram.

chapters.zh: 有火在他们的党中发起；有火燄烧毁了恶人。
chapters.en: And a fire was kindled in their company; the flame burned up the wicked.

chapters.zh: 他们在何烈山造了牛犊，叩拜铸成的像。
chapters.en: They made a calf in Horeb, and worshipped the molten image.

chapters.zh: 如此将他们荣耀的主换为吃草之牛的像，
chapters.en: Thus they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.

chapters.zh: 忘了神 ─ 他们的救主；他曾在埃及行大事，
chapters.en: They forgat God their saviour, which had done great things in Egypt;

chapters.zh: 在含地行奇事，在红海行可畏的事。
chapters.en: Wondrous works in the land of Ham, terrible things by the Red sea.

chapters.zh: 所以，他说要灭绝他们；若非有他所拣选的摩西站在当中（原文是破口），使他的忿怒转消，恐怕他就灭绝他们。
chapters.en: Therefore he said that he would destroy them, had not Moses his chosen stood before him in the breach, to turn away his wrath, lest he should destroy .

chapters.zh: 他们又藐视那美地，不信他的话，
chapters.en: Yea, they despised the pleasant land, they believed not his word:

chapters.zh: 在自己帐棚内发怨言，不听耶和华的声音。
chapters.en: But murmured in their tents, hearkened not unto the voice of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 所以，他对他们起誓：必叫他们倒在旷野，
chapters.en: Therefore he lifted up his hand against them, to overthrow them in the wilderness:

chapters.zh: 叫他们的后裔倒在列国之中，分散在各地。
chapters.en: To overthrow their seed also among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands.

chapters.zh: 他们又与巴力 • 毗珥连合，且吃了祭死神（或译：人）的物。
chapters.en: They joined themselves also unto Baalpeor, and ate the sacrifices of the dead.

chapters.zh: 他们这样行，惹耶和华发怒，便有瘟疫流行在他们中间。
chapters.en: Thus they provoked to anger with their inventions: and the plague brake in upon them.

chapters.zh: 那时，非尼哈站起，刑罚恶人，瘟疫这才止息。
chapters.en: Then stood up Phinehas, and executed judgment: and the plague was stayed.

chapters.zh: 那就算为他的义，世世代代，直到永远。
chapters.en: And that was counted unto him for righteousness unto all generations for evermore.

chapters.zh: 他们在米利巴水又叫耶和华发怒，甚至摩西也受了亏损，
chapters.en: They angered also at the waters of strife, so that it went ill with Moses for their sakes:

chapters.zh: 是因他们惹动他的灵，摩西（原文是他）用嘴说了急躁的话。
chapters.en: Because they provoked his spirit, so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.

chapters.zh: 他们不照耶和华所吩咐的灭绝外邦人，
chapters.en: They did not destroy the nations, concerning whom the LORD commanded them:

chapters.zh: 反与他们混杂相合，学习他们的行为，
chapters.en: But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their works.

chapters.zh: 事奉他们的偶像，这就成了自己的网罗，
chapters.en: And they served their idols: which were a snare unto them.

chapters.zh: 把自己的儿女祭祀鬼魔，
chapters.en: Yea, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters unto devils,

chapters.zh: 流无辜人的血，就是自己儿女的血，把他们祭祀迦南的偶像，那地就被血污秽了。
chapters.en: And shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they sacrificed unto the idols of Canaan: and the land was polluted with blood.

chapters.zh: 这样，他们被自己所做的污秽了，在行为上犯了邪淫。
chapters.en: Thus were they defiled with their own works, and went a whoring with their own inventions.

chapters.zh: 所以，耶和华的怒气向他的百姓发作，憎恶他的产业，
chapters.en: Therefore was the wrath of the LORD kindled against his people, insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.

chapters.zh: 将他们交在外邦人的手里；恨他们的人就辖制他们。
chapters.en: And he gave them into the hand of the heathen; and they that hated them ruled over them.

chapters.zh: 他们的仇敌也欺压他们，他们就伏在敌人手下。
chapters.en: Their enemies also oppressed them, and they were brought into subjection under their hand.

chapters.zh: 他屡次搭救他们，他们却设谋背逆，因自己的罪孽降为卑下。
chapters.en: Many times did he deliver them; but they provoked with their counsel, and were brought low for their iniquity.

chapters.zh: 然而，他听见他们哀告的时候，就眷顾他们的急难，
chapters.en: Nevertheless he regarded their affliction, when he heard their cry:

chapters.zh: 为他们记念他的约，照他丰盛的慈爱后悔。
chapters.en: And he remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his mercies.

chapters.zh: 他也使他们在凡掳掠他们的人面前蒙怜恤。
chapters.en: He made them also to be pitied of all those that carried them captives.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我们的神啊，求你拯救我们，从外邦中招聚我们，我们好称赞你的圣名，以赞美你为夸胜。
chapters.en: Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the heathen, to give thanks unto thy holy name, to triumph in thy praise.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 以色列的神是应当称颂的，从亘古直到永远。愿众民都说：阿们！你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD God of Israel from everlasting to everlasting: and let all the people say, Amen. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要称谢耶和华，因他本为善；他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: O give thanks unto the LORD, for good: for his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 愿耶和华的赎民说这话，就是他从敌人手中所救赎的，
chapters.en: Let the redeemed of the LORD say , whom he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemy;

chapters.zh: 从各地，从东从西，从南从北，所招聚来的。
chapters.en: And gathered them out of the lands, from the east, and from the west, from the north, and from the south.

chapters.zh: 他们在旷野荒地漂流，寻不见可住的城邑，
chapters.en: They wandered in the wilderness in a solitary way; they found no city to dwell in.

chapters.zh: 又饥又渴，心里发昏。
chapters.en: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.

chapters.zh: 于是，他们在苦难中哀求耶和华；他从他们的祸患中搭救他们，
chapters.en: Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, he delivered them out of their distresses.

chapters.zh: 又领他们行走直路，使他们往可居住的城邑。
chapters.en: And he led them forth by the right way, that they might go to a city of habitation.

chapters.zh: 但愿人因耶和华的慈爱和他向人所行的奇事都称赞他；
chapters.en: Oh that would praise the LORD his goodness, and his wonderful works to the children of men!

chapters.zh: 因他使心里渴慕的人得以知足，使心里饥饿的人得饱美物。
chapters.en: For he satisfieth the longing soul, and filleth the hungry soul with goodness.

chapters.zh: 那些坐在黑暗中、死荫里的人被困苦和铁炼捆锁，
chapters.en: Such as sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, bound in affliction and iron;

chapters.zh: 是因他们违背神的话语，藐视至高者的旨意。
chapters.en: Because they rebelled against the words of God, and contemned the counsel of the most High:

chapters.zh: 所以，他用劳苦治服他们的心；他们仆倒，无人扶助。
chapters.en: Therefore he brought down their heart with labour; they fell down, and none to help.

chapters.zh: 于是，他们在苦难中哀求耶和华；他从他们的祸患中拯救他们。
chapters.en: Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, he saved them out of their distresses.

chapters.zh: 他从黑暗中和死荫里领他们出来，折断他们的绑索。
chapters.en: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and brake their bands in sunder.

chapters.zh: 但愿人因耶和华的慈爱和他向人所行的奇事都称赞他；
chapters.en: Oh that would praise the LORD his goodness, and his wonderful works to the children of men!

chapters.zh: 因为他打破了铜门，砍断了铁闩。
chapters.en: For he hath broken the gates of brass, and cut the bars of iron in sunder.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人因自己的过犯和自己的罪孽便受苦楚。
chapters.en: Fools because of their transgression, and because of their iniquities, are afflicted.

chapters.zh: 他们心里厌恶各样的食物，就临近死门。
chapters.en: Their soul abhorreth all manner of meat; and they draw near unto the gates of death.

chapters.zh: 于是，他们在苦难中哀求耶和华；他从他们的祸患中拯救他们。
chapters.en: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, he saveth them out of their distresses.

chapters.zh: 他发命医治他们，救他们脱离死亡。
chapters.en: He sent his word, and healed them, and delivered from their destructions.

chapters.zh: 但愿人因耶和华的慈爱和他向人所行的奇事都称赞他。
chapters.en: Oh that would praise the LORD his goodness, and his wonderful works to the children of men!

chapters.zh: 愿他们以感谢为祭献给他，欢呼述说他的作为！
chapters.en: And let them sacrifice the sacrifices of thanksgiving, and declare his works with rejoicing.

chapters.zh: 在海上坐船，在大水中经理事务的，
chapters.en: They that go down to the sea in ships, that do business in great waters;

chapters.zh: 他们看见耶和华的作为，并他在深水中的奇事。
chapters.en: These see the works of the LORD, and his wonders in the deep.

chapters.zh: 因他一吩咐，狂风就起来，海中的波浪也扬起。
chapters.en: For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind, which lifteth up the waves thereof.

chapters.zh: 他们上到天空，下到海底；他们的心因患难便消化。
chapters.en: They mount up to the heaven, they go down again to the depths: their soul is melted because of trouble.

chapters.zh: 他们摇摇幌幌，东倒西歪，好像醉酒的人；他们的智慧无法可施。
chapters.en: They reel to and fro, and stagger like a drunken man, and are at their wits' end.

chapters.zh: 于是，他们在苦难中哀求耶和华，他从他们的祸患中领出他们来。
chapters.en: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he bringeth them out of their distresses.

chapters.zh: 他使狂风止息，波浪就平静。
chapters.en: He maketh the storm a calm, so that the waves thereof are still.

chapters.zh: 风息浪静，他们便欢喜；他就引他们到所愿去的海口。
chapters.en: Then are they glad because they be quiet; so he bringeth them unto their desired haven.

chapters.zh: 但愿人因耶和华的慈爱和他向人所行的奇事都称赞他。
chapters.en: Oh that would praise the LORD his goodness, and his wonderful works to the children of men!

chapters.zh: 愿他们在民的会中尊崇他，在长老的位上赞美他！
chapters.en: Let them exalt him also in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.

chapters.zh: 他使江河变为旷野，叫水泉变为干渴之地，
chapters.en: He turneth rivers into a wilderness, and the watersprings into dry ground;

chapters.zh: 使肥地变为碱地；这都因其间居民的罪恶。
chapters.en: A fruitful land into barrenness, for the wickedness of them that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 他使旷野变为水潭，叫旱地变为水泉。
chapters.en: He turneth the wilderness into a standing water, and dry ground into watersprings.

chapters.zh: 他使饥饿的人住在那里，好建造可住的城邑，
chapters.en: And there he maketh the hungry to dwell, that they may prepare a city for habitation;

chapters.zh: 又种田地，栽葡萄园，得享所出的土产。
chapters.en: And sow the fields, and plant vineyards, which may yield fruits of increase.

chapters.zh: 他又赐福给他们，叫他们生养众多，也不叫他们的牲畜减少。
chapters.en: He blesseth them also, so that they are multiplied greatly; and suffereth not their cattle to decrease.

chapters.zh: 他们又因暴虐、患难、愁苦，就减少且卑下。
chapters.en: Again, they are minished and brought low through oppression, affliction, and sorrow.

chapters.zh: 他使君王蒙羞被辱，使他们在荒废无路之地漂流。
chapters.en: He poureth contempt upon princes, and causeth them to wander in the wilderness, no way.

chapters.zh: 他却将穷乏人安置在高处，脱离苦难，使他的家属多如羊群。
chapters.en: Yet setteth he the poor on high from affliction, and maketh families like a flock.

chapters.zh: 正直人看见就欢喜；罪孽之辈必塞口无言。
chapters.en: The righteous shall see , and rejoice: and all iniquity shall stop her mouth.

chapters.zh: 凡有智慧的，必在这些事上留心，也必思想耶和华的慈爱。
chapters.en: Whoso wise, and will observe these , even they shall understand the lovingkindness of the LORD.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗歌。）神啊，我心坚定；我口（原文是荣耀）要唱诗歌颂！
chapters.en: [A Song Psalm of David.] O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and give praise, even with my glory.

chapters.zh: 琴瑟啊，你们当醒起！我自己要极早醒起！
chapters.en: Awake, psaltery and harp: I will awake early.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我要在万民中称谢你，在列邦中歌颂你！
chapters.en: I will praise thee, O LORD, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.

chapters.zh: 因为，你的慈爱大过诸天；你的诚实达到穹苍。
chapters.en: For thy mercy great above the heavens: and thy truth unto the clouds.

chapters.zh: 神啊，愿你崇高过于诸天！愿你的荣耀高过全地！
chapters.en: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: and thy glory above all the earth;

chapters.zh: 求你应允我们，用右手拯救我们，好叫你所亲爱的人得救。
chapters.en: That thy beloved may be delivered: save thy right hand, and answer me.

chapters.zh: 神已经指著他的圣洁说（说：或译应许我）：我要欢乐；我要分开示剑，丈量疏割谷。
chapters.en: God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.

chapters.zh: 基列是我的；玛拿西是我的；以法莲是护卫我头的；犹大是我的杖；
chapters.en: Gilead mine; Manasseh mine; Ephraim also the strength of mine head; Judah my lawgiver;

chapters.zh: 摩押是我的沐浴盆；我要向以东抛鞋；我必因胜非利士呼喊。
chapters.en: Moab my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe; over Philistia will I triumph.

chapters.zh: 谁能领我进坚固城？谁能引我到以东地？
chapters.en: Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?

chapters.zh: 神啊，你不是丢弃了我们么？神啊，你不和我们的军兵同去么？
chapters.en: not , O God, hast cast us off? and wilt not thou, O God, go forth with our hosts?

chapters.zh: 求你帮助我们攻击敌人，因为人的帮助是枉然的。
chapters.en: Give us help from trouble: for vain the help of man.

chapters.zh: 我们倚靠神才得施展大能，因为践踏我们敌人的就是他。
chapters.en: Through God we shall do valiantly: for he shall tread down our enemies.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗，交与伶长。）我所赞美的神啊，求你不要闭口不言。
chapters.en: [To the chief Musician, A Psalm of David.] Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;

chapters.zh: 因为恶人的嘴和诡诈人的口已经张开攻击我；他们用撒谎的舌头对我说话。
chapters.en: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me: they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.

chapters.zh: 他们围绕我，说怨恨的话，又无故地攻打我。
chapters.en: They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.

chapters.zh: 他们与我为敌以报我爱，但我专心祈祷。
chapters.en: For my love they are my adversaries: but I prayer.

chapters.zh: 他们向我以恶报善，以恨报爱。
chapters.en: And they have rewarded me evil for good, and hatred for my love.

chapters.zh: 愿你派一个恶人辖制他，派一个对头站在他右边！
chapters.en: Set thou a wicked man over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.

chapters.zh: 他受审判的时候，愿他出来担当罪名！愿他的祈祷反成为罪！
chapters.en: When he shall be judged, let him be condemned: and let his prayer become sin.

chapters.zh: 愿他的年日短少！愿别人得他的职分！
chapters.en: Let his days be few; let another take his office.

chapters.zh: 愿他的儿女为孤儿，他的妻子为寡妇！
chapters.en: Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.

chapters.zh: 愿他的儿女漂流讨饭，从他们荒凉之处出来求食！
chapters.en: Let his children be continually vagabonds, and beg: let them seek also out of their desolate places.

chapters.zh: 愿强暴的债主牢笼他一切所有的！愿外人抢他劳碌得来的！
chapters.en: Let the extortioner catch all that he hath; and let the strangers spoil his labour.

chapters.zh: 愿无人向他延绵施恩！愿无人可怜他的孤儿！
chapters.en: Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.

chapters.zh: 愿他的后人断绝，名字被涂抹，不传于下代！
chapters.en: Let his posterity be cut off; in the generation following let their name be blotted out.

chapters.zh: 愿他祖宗的罪孽被耶和华记念！愿他母亲的罪过不被涂抹！
chapters.en: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with the LORD; and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.

chapters.zh: 愿这些罪常在耶和华面前，使他的名号断绝于世！
chapters.en: Let them be before the LORD continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.

chapters.zh: 因为他不想施恩，却逼迫困苦穷乏的和伤心的人，要把他们治死。
chapters.en: Because that he remembered not to shew mercy, but persecuted the poor and needy man, that he might even slay the broken in heart.

chapters.zh: 他爱咒骂，咒骂就临到他；他不喜爱福乐，福乐就与他远离！
chapters.en: As he loved cursing, so let it come unto him: as he delighted not in blessing, so let it be far from him.

chapters.zh: 他拿咒骂当衣服穿上；这咒骂就如水进他里面，像油入他的骨头。
chapters.en: As he clothed himself with cursing like as with his garment, so let it come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.

chapters.zh: 愿这咒骂当他遮身的衣服，当他常束的腰带！
chapters.en: Let it be unto him as the garment covereth him, and for a girdle wherewith he is girded continually.

chapters.zh: 这就是我对头和用恶言议论我的人从耶和华那里所受的报应。
chapters.en: this the reward of mine adversaries from the LORD, and of them that speak evil against my soul.

chapters.zh: 主 ─ 耶和华啊，求你为你的名恩待我；因你的慈爱美好，求你搭救我！
chapters.en: But do thou for me, O GOD the Lord, for thy name's sake: because thy mercy good, deliver thou me.

chapters.zh: 因为我困苦穷乏，内心受伤。
chapters.en: For I poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.

chapters.zh: 我如日影渐渐偏斜而去；我如蝗虫被抖出来。
chapters.en: I am gone like the shadow when it declineth: I am tossed up and down as the locust.

chapters.zh: 我因禁食，膝骨软弱；我身上的肉也渐渐瘦了。
chapters.en: My knees are weak through fasting; and my flesh faileth of fatness.

chapters.zh: 我受他们的羞辱，他们看见我便摇头。
chapters.en: I became also a reproach unto them: they looked upon me they shaked their heads.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 我的神啊，求你帮助我，照你的慈爱拯救我，
chapters.en: Help me, O LORD my God: O save me according to thy mercy:

chapters.zh: 使他们知道这是你的手，是你 ─ 耶和华所行的事。
chapters.en: That they may know that this thy hand; thou, LORD, hast done it.

chapters.zh: 任凭他们咒骂，惟愿你赐福；他们几时起来就必蒙羞，你的仆人却要欢喜。
chapters.en: Let them curse, but bless thou: when they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.

chapters.zh: 愿我的对头披戴羞辱！愿他们以自己的羞愧为外袍遮身！
chapters.en: Let mine adversaries be clothed with shame, and let them cover themselves with their own confusion, as with a mantle.

chapters.zh: 我要用口极力称谢耶和华；我要在众人中间赞美他；
chapters.en: I will greatly praise the LORD with my mouth; yea, I will praise him among the multitude.

chapters.zh: 因为他必站在穷乏人的右边，要救他脱离审判他灵魂的人。
chapters.en: For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor, to save from those that condemn his soul.

chapters.zh: （大卫的诗。）耶和华对我主说：你坐在我的右边，等我使你仇敌作你的脚凳。
chapters.en: [A Psalm of David.] The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必使你从锡安伸出能力的杖来；你要在你仇敌中掌权。
chapters.en: The LORD shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.

chapters.zh: 当你掌权的日子（或译：行军的日子），你的民要以圣洁的妆饰为衣（或译：以圣洁为妆饰），甘心牺牲自己；你的民多如清晨的甘露（或译：你少年时光耀如清晨的甘露）。
chapters.en: Thy people willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holiness from the womb of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华起了誓，决不后悔，说：你是照著麦基洗德的等次永远为祭司。
chapters.en: The LORD hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek.

chapters.zh: 在你右边的主，当他发怒的日子，必打伤列王。
chapters.en: The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath.

chapters.zh: 他要在列邦中刑罚恶人，尸首就遍满各处；他要在许多国中打破仇敌的头。
chapters.en: He shall judge among the heathen, he shall fill with the dead bodies; he shall wound the heads over many countries.

chapters.zh: 他要喝路旁的河水，因此必抬起头来。
chapters.en: He shall drink of the brook in the way: therefore shall he lift up the head.

chapters.zh: 你们要赞美耶和华！我要在正直人的大会中，并公会中，一心称谢耶和华。
chapters.en: Praise ye the LORD. I will praise the LORD with whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and the congregation.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的作为本为大；凡喜爱的都必考察。
chapters.en: The works of the LORD great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.

chapters.zh: 他所行的是尊荣和威严；他的公义存到永远。
chapters.en: His work honourable and glorious: and his righteousness endureth for ever.

chapters.zh: 他行了奇事，使人记念；耶和华有恩惠，有怜悯。
chapters.en: He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the LORD gracious and full of compassion.

chapters.zh: 他赐粮食给敬畏他的人；他必永远记念他的约。
chapters.en: He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.

chapters.zh: 他向百姓显出大能的作为，把外邦的地赐给他们为业。
chapters.en: He hath shewed his people the power of his works, that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.

chapters.zh: 他手所行的是诚实公平；他的训词都是确实的，
chapters.en: The works of his hands verity and judgment; all his commandments sure.

chapters.zh: 是永永远远坚定的，是按诚实正直设立的。
chapters.en: They stand fast for ever and ever, done in truth and uprightness.

chapters.zh: 他向百姓施行救赎，命定他的约，直到永远；他的名圣而可畏。
chapters.en: He sent redemption unto his people: he hath commanded his covenant for ever: holy and reverend his name.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华是智慧的开端；凡遵行他命令的是聪明人。耶和华是永远当赞美的！
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do : his praise endureth for ever.

chapters.zh: 你们要赞美耶和华！敬畏耶和华，甚喜爱他命令的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: Praise ye the LORD. Blessed the man feareth the LORD, delighteth greatly in his commandments.

chapters.zh: 他的后裔在世必强盛；正直人的后代必要蒙福。
chapters.en: His seed shall be mighty upon earth: the generation of the upright shall be blessed.

chapters.zh: 他家中有货物，有钱财；他的公义存到永远。
chapters.en: Wealth and riches in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.

chapters.zh: 正直人在黑暗中，有光向他发现；他有恩惠，有怜悯，有公义。
chapters.en: Unto the upright there ariseth light in the darkness: gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.

chapters.zh: 施恩与人、借贷与人的，这人事情顺利；他被审判的时候要诉明自己的冤。
chapters.en: A good man sheweth favour, and lendeth: he will guide his affairs with discretion.

chapters.zh: 他永不动摇；义人被记念，直到永远。
chapters.en: Surely he shall not be moved for ever: the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.

chapters.zh: 他必不怕凶恶的信息；他心坚定，倚靠耶和华。
chapters.en: He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他心确定，总不惧怕，直到他看见敌人遭报。
chapters.en: His heart established, he shall not be afraid, until he see upon his enemies.

chapters.zh: 他施舍钱财，赒济贫穷；他的仁义存到永远。他的角必被高举，大有荣耀。
chapters.en: He hath dispersed, he hath given to the poor; his righteousness endureth for ever; his horn shall be exalted with honour.

chapters.zh: 恶人看见便恼恨，必咬牙而消化；恶人的心愿要归灭绝。
chapters.en: The wicked shall see , and be grieved; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.

chapters.zh: 你们要赞美耶和华！耶和华的仆人哪，你们要赞美，赞美耶和华的名！
chapters.en: Praise ye the LORD. Praise, O ye servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的名是应当称颂的，从今时直到永远！
chapters.en: Blessed be the name of the LORD from this time forth and for evermore.

chapters.zh: 从日出之地到日落之处，耶和华的名是应当赞美的！
chapters.en: From the rising of the sun unto the going down of the same the LORD'S name to be praised.

chapters.zh: 耶和华超乎万民之上；他的荣耀高过诸天。
chapters.en: The LORD high above all nations, his glory above the heavens.

chapters.zh: 谁像耶和华 ─ 我们的神呢？他坐在至高之处，
chapters.en: Who like unto the LORD our God, who dwelleth on high,

chapters.zh: 自己谦卑，观看天上地下的事。
chapters.en: Who humbleth to behold in heaven, and in the earth!

chapters.zh: 他从灰尘里抬举贫寒人，从粪堆中提拔穷乏人，
chapters.en: He raiseth up the poor out of the dust, lifteth the needy out of the dunghill;

chapters.zh: 使他们与王子同坐，就是与本国的王子同坐。
chapters.en: That he may set with princes, with the princes of his people.

chapters.zh: 他使不能生育的妇人安居家中，为多子的乐母。你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: He maketh the barren woman to keep house, a joyful mother of children. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 以色列出了埃及，雅各家离开说异言之民；
chapters.en: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language;

chapters.zh: 那时，犹大为主的圣所，以色列为他所治理的国度。
chapters.en: Judah was his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.

chapters.zh: 沧海看见就奔逃；约但河也倒流。
chapters.en: The sea saw , and fled: Jordan was driven back.

chapters.zh: 大山踊跃，如公羊；小山跳舞，如羊羔。
chapters.en: The mountains skipped like rams, the little hills like lambs.

chapters.zh: 沧海啊，你为何奔逃？约但哪，你为何倒流？
chapters.en: What thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, thou wast driven back?

chapters.zh: 大山哪，你为何踊跃，如公羊？小山哪，你为何跳舞，如羊羔？
chapters.en: Ye mountains, ye skipped like rams; ye little hills, like lambs?

chapters.zh: 大地啊，你因见主的面，就是雅各神的面，便要震动。
chapters.en: Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob;

chapters.zh: 他叫磐石变为水池，叫坚石变为泉源。
chapters.en: Which turned the rock a standing water, the flint into a fountain of waters.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，荣耀不要归与我们，不要归与我们；要因你的慈爱和诚实归在你的名下！
chapters.en: Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, for thy truth's sake.

chapters.zh: 为何容外邦人说：他们的神在哪里呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore should the heathen say, Where now their God?

chapters.zh: 然而，我们的神在天上，都随自己的意旨行事。
chapters.en: But our God in the heavens: he hath done whatsoever he hath pleased.

chapters.zh: 他们的偶像是金的，银的，是人手所造的，
chapters.en: Their idols silver and gold, the work of men's hands.

chapters.zh: 有口却不能言，有眼却不能看，
chapters.en: They have mouths, but they speak not: eyes have they, but they see not:

chapters.zh: 有耳却不能听，有鼻却不能闻，
chapters.en: They have ears, but they hear not: noses have they, but they smell not:

chapters.zh: 有手却不能摸，有脚却不能走，有喉咙也不能出声。
chapters.en: They have hands, but they handle not: feet have they, but they walk not: neither speak they through their throat.

chapters.zh: 造他的要和他一样；凡靠他的也要如此。
chapters.en: They that make them are like unto them; every one that trusteth in them.

chapters.zh: 以色列啊，你要倚靠耶和华！他是你的帮助和你的盾牌。
chapters.en: O Israel, trust thou in the LORD: he their help and their shield.

chapters.zh: 亚伦家啊，你们要倚靠耶和华！他是你们的帮助和你们的盾牌。
chapters.en: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he their help and their shield.

chapters.zh: 你们敬畏耶和华的，要倚靠耶和华！他是你们的帮助和你们的盾牌。
chapters.en: Ye that fear the LORD, trust in the LORD: he their help and their shield.

chapters.zh: 耶和华向来眷念我们；他还要赐福给我们：要赐福给以色列的家，赐福给亚伦的家。
chapters.en: The LORD hath been mindful of us: he will bless ; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron.

chapters.zh: 凡敬畏耶和华的，无论大小，主必赐福给他。
chapters.en: He will bless them that fear the LORD, small and great.

chapters.zh: 愿耶和华叫你们和你们的子孙日见加增。
chapters.en: The LORD shall increase you more and more, you and your children.

chapters.zh: 你们蒙了造天地之耶和华的福！
chapters.en: Ye blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.

chapters.zh: 天，是耶和华的天；地，他却给了世人。
chapters.en: The heaven, the heavens, the LORD'S: but the earth hath he given to the children of men.

chapters.zh: 死人不能赞美耶和华；下到寂静中的也都不能。
chapters.en: The dead praise not the LORD, neither any that go down into silence.

chapters.zh: 但我们要称颂耶和华，从今时直到永远。你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and for evermore. Praise the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我爱耶和华，因为他听了我的声音和我的恳求。
chapters.en: I love the LORD, because he hath heard my voice my supplications.

chapters.zh: 他既向我侧耳，我一生要求告他。
chapters.en: Because he hath inclined his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon as long as I live.

chapters.zh: 死亡的绳索缠绕我；阴间的痛苦抓住我；我遭遇患难愁苦。
chapters.en: The sorrows of death compassed me, and the pains of hell gat hold upon me: I found trouble and sorrow.

chapters.zh: 那时，我便求告耶和华的名，说：耶和华啊，求你救我的灵魂！
chapters.en: Then called I upon the name of the LORD; O LORD, I beseech thee, deliver my soul.

chapters.zh: 耶和华有恩惠，有公义；我们的神以怜悯为怀。
chapters.en: Gracious the LORD, and righteous; yea, our God merciful.

chapters.zh: 耶和华保护愚人；我落到卑微的地步，他救了我。
chapters.en: The LORD preserveth the simple: I was brought low, and he helped me.

chapters.zh: 我的心哪！你要仍归安乐，因为耶和华用厚恩待你。
chapters.en: Return unto thy rest, O my soul; for the LORD hath dealt bountifully with thee.

chapters.zh: 主啊，你救我的命免了死亡，救我的眼免了流泪，救我的脚免了跌倒。
chapters.en: For thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, my feet from falling.

chapters.zh: 我要在耶和华面前行活人之路。
chapters.en: I will walk before the LORD in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 我因信，所以如此说话；我受了极大的困苦。
chapters.en: I believed, therefore have I spoken: I was greatly afflicted:

chapters.zh: 我曾急促地说：人都是说谎的！
chapters.en: I said in my haste, All men liars.

chapters.zh: 我拿甚么报答耶和华向我所赐的一切厚恩？
chapters.en: What shall I render unto the LORD all his benefits toward me?

chapters.zh: 我要举起救恩的杯，称扬耶和华的名。
chapters.en: I will take the cup of salvation, and call upon the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我要在他众民面前向耶和华还我的愿。
chapters.en: I will pay my vows unto the LORD now in the presence of all his people.

chapters.zh: 在耶和华眼中，看圣民之死极为宝贵。
chapters.en: Precious in the sight of the LORD the death of his saints.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我真是你的仆人；我是你的仆人，是你婢女的儿子。你已经解开我的绑索。
chapters.en: O LORD, truly I thy servant; I thy servant, the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.

chapters.zh: 我要以感谢为祭献给你，又要求告耶和华的名。
chapters.en: I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call upon the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我要在他众民面前，在耶和华殿的院内，在耶路撒冷当中，向耶和华还我的愿。你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: I will pay my vows unto the LORD now in the presence of all his people,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: In the courts of the LORD'S house, in the midst of thee, O Jerusalem. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 万国啊，你们都当赞美耶和华！万民哪，你们都当颂赞他！
chapters.en: O praise the LORD, all ye nations: praise him, all ye people.

chapters.zh: 因为他向我们大施慈爱；耶和华的诚实存到永远。你们要赞美耶和华！
chapters.en: For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the LORD for ever. Praise ye the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要称谢耶和华，因他本为善；他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: O give thanks unto the LORD; for good: because his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 愿以色列说：他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: Let Israel now say, that his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 愿亚伦的家说：他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: Let the house of Aaron now say, that his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 愿敬畏耶和华的说：他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: Let them now that fear the LORD say, that his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 我在急难中求告耶和华，他就应允我，把我安置在宽阔之地。
chapters.en: I called upon the LORD in distress: the LORD answered me, in a large place.

chapters.zh: 有耶和华帮助我，我必不惧怕，人能把我怎么样呢？
chapters.en: The LORD on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?

chapters.zh: 在那帮助我的人中，有耶和华帮助我，所以我要看见那恨我的人遭报。
chapters.en: The LORD taketh my part with them that help me: therefore shall I see upon them that hate me.

chapters.zh: 投靠耶和华，强似倚赖人；
chapters.en: better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in man.

chapters.zh: 投靠耶和华，强似倚赖王子。
chapters.en: better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in princes.

chapters.zh: 万民围绕我，我靠耶和华的名必剿灭他们。
chapters.en: All nations compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD will I destroy them.

chapters.zh: 他们环绕我，围困我，我靠耶和华的名必剿灭他们。
chapters.en: They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.

chapters.zh: 他们如同蜂子围绕我，好像烧荆棘的火，必被熄灭；我靠耶和华的名，必剿灭他们。
chapters.en: They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.

chapters.zh: 你推我，要叫我跌倒，但耶和华帮助了我。
chapters.en: Thou hast thrust sore at me that I might fall: but the LORD helped me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的力量，是我的诗歌；他也成了我的拯救。
chapters.en: The LORD my strength and song, and is become my salvation.

chapters.zh: 在义人的帐棚里，有欢呼拯救的声音；耶和华的右手施展大能。
chapters.en: The voice of rejoicing and salvation in the tabernacles of the righteous: the right hand of the LORD doeth valiantly.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的右手高举；耶和华的右手施展大能。
chapters.en: The right hand of the LORD is exalted: the right hand of the LORD doeth valiantly.

chapters.zh: 我必不致死，仍要存活，并要传扬耶和华的作为。
chapters.en: I shall not die, but live, and declare the works of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华虽严严地惩治我，却未曾将我交于死亡。
chapters.en: The LORD hath chastened me sore: but he hath not given me over unto death.

chapters.zh: 给我敞开义门；我要进去称谢耶和华！
chapters.en: Open to me the gates of righteousness: I will go into them, I will praise the LORD:

chapters.zh: 这是耶和华的门；义人要进去！
chapters.en: This gate of the LORD, into which the righteous shall enter.

chapters.zh: 我要称谢你，因为你已经应允我，又成了我的拯救！
chapters.en: I will praise thee: for thou hast heard me, and art become my salvation.

chapters.zh: 匠人所弃的石头已成了房角的头块石头。
chapters.en: The stone the builders refused is become the head of the corner.

chapters.zh: 这是耶和华所做的，在我们眼中看为希奇。
chapters.en: This is the LORD'S doing; it marvellous in our eyes.

chapters.zh: 这是耶和华所定的日子，我们在其中要高兴欢喜！
chapters.en: This the day the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你拯救！耶和华啊，求你使我们亨通！
chapters.en: Save now, I beseech thee, O LORD: O LORD, I beseech thee, send now prosperity.

chapters.zh: 奉耶和华名来的是应当称颂的！我们从耶和华的殿中为你们祝福！
chapters.en: Blessed he that cometh in the name of the LORD: we have blessed you out of the house of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是神；他光照了我们。理当用绳索把祭牲拴住，牵到坛角那里。
chapters.en: God the LORD, which hath shewed us light: bind the sacrifice with cords, unto the horns of the altar.

chapters.zh: 你是我的神，我要称谢你！你是我的神，我要尊崇你！
chapters.en: Thou my God, and I will praise thee: my God, I will exalt thee.

chapters.zh: 你们要称谢耶和华，因他本为善；他的慈爱永远长存！
chapters.en: O give thanks unto the LORD; for good: for his mercy for ever.

chapters.zh: 行为完全、遵行耶和华律法的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: ALEPH. Blessed the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 遵守他的法度、一心寻求他的，这人便为有福！
chapters.en: Blessed they that keep his testimonies, seek him with the whole heart.

chapters.zh: 这人不做非义的事，但遵行他的道。
chapters.en: They also do no iniquity: they walk in his ways.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你曾将你的训词吩咐我们，为要我们殷勤遵守。
chapters.en: Thou hast commanded to keep thy precepts diligently.

chapters.zh: 但愿我行事坚定，得以遵守你的律例。
chapters.en: O that my ways were directed to keep thy statutes!

chapters.zh: 我看重你的一切命令，就不至于羞愧。
chapters.en: Then shall I not be ashamed, when I have respect unto all thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 我学了你公义的判语，就要以正直的心称谢你。
chapters.en: I will praise thee with uprightness of heart, when I shall have learned thy righteous judgments.

chapters.zh: 我必守你的律例；求你总不要丢弃我！
chapters.en: I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly.

chapters.zh: 少年人用甚么洁净他的行为呢？是要遵行你的话！
chapters.en: BETH. Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 我一心寻求了你；求你不要叫我偏离你的命令。
chapters.en: With my whole heart have I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 我将你的话藏在心里，免得我得罪你。
chapters.en: Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你是应当称颂的！求你将你的律例教训我！
chapters.en: Blessed thou, O LORD: teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 我用嘴唇传扬你口中的一切典章。
chapters.en: With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 我喜悦你的法度，如同喜悦一切的财物。
chapters.en: I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies, as in all riches.

chapters.zh: 我要默想你的训词，看重你的道路。
chapters.en: I will meditate in thy precepts, and have respect unto thy ways.

chapters.zh: 我要在你的律例中自乐；我不忘记你的话。
chapters.en: I will delight myself in thy statutes: I will not forget thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你用厚恩待你的仆人，使我存活，我就遵守你的话。
chapters.en: GIMEL. Deal bountifully with thy servant, I may live, and keep thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你开我的眼睛，使我看出你律法中的奇妙。
chapters.en: Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wondrous things out of thy law.

chapters.zh: 我是在地上作寄居的；求你不要向我隐瞒你的命令！
chapters.en: I a stranger in the earth: hide not thy commandments from me.

chapters.zh: 我时常切慕你的典章，甚至心碎。
chapters.en: My soul breaketh for the longing unto thy judgments at all times.

chapters.zh: 受咒诅、偏离你命令的骄傲人，你已经责备他们。
chapters.en: Thou hast rebuked the proud cursed, which do err from thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 求你除掉我所受的羞辱和藐视，因我遵守你的法度。
chapters.en: Remove from me reproach and contempt; for I have kept thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 虽有首领坐著妄论我，你仆人却思想你的律例。
chapters.en: Princes also did sit speak against me: thy servant did meditate in thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 你的法度是我所喜乐的，是我的谋士。
chapters.en: Thy testimonies also my delight my counsellors.

chapters.zh: 我的性命几乎归于尘土；求你照你的话将我救活！
chapters.en: DALETH. My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 我述说我所行的，你应允了我；求你将你的律例教训我！
chapters.en: I have declared my ways, and thou heardest me: teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 求你使我明白你的训词，我就思想你的奇事。
chapters.en: Make me to understand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talk of thy wondrous works.

chapters.zh: 我的心因愁苦而消化；求你照你的话使我坚立！
chapters.en: My soul melteth for heaviness: strengthen thou me according unto thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你使我离开奸诈的道，开恩将你的律法赐给我！
chapters.en: Remove from me the way of lying: and grant me thy law graciously.

chapters.zh: 我拣选了忠信的道，将你的典章摆在我面前。
chapters.en: I have chosen the way of truth: thy judgments have I laid .

chapters.zh: 我持守你的法度；耶和华啊，求你不要叫我羞愧！
chapters.en: I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O LORD, put me not to shame.

chapters.zh: 你开广我心的时候，我就往你命令的道上直奔。
chapters.en: I will run the way of thy commandments, when thou shalt enlarge my heart.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你将你的律例指教我，我必遵守到底！
chapters.en: HE. Teach me, O LORD, the way of thy statutes; and I shall keep it the end.

chapters.zh: 求你赐我悟性，我便遵守你的律法，且要一心遵守。
chapters.en: Give me understanding, and I shall keep thy law; yea, I shall observe it with whole heart.

chapters.zh: 求你叫我遵行你的命令，因为这是我所喜乐的。
chapters.en: Make me to go in the path of thy commandments; for therein do I delight.

chapters.zh: 求你使我的心趋向你的法度，不趋向非义之财。
chapters.en: Incline my heart unto thy testimonies, and not to covetousness.

chapters.zh: 求你叫我转眼不看虚假，又叫我在你的道中生活。
chapters.en: Turn away mine eyes from beholding vanity; quicken thou me in thy way.

chapters.zh: 你向敬畏你的人所应许的话，求你向仆人坚定！
chapters.en: Stablish thy word unto thy servant, who to thy fear.

chapters.zh: 求你使我所怕的羞辱远离我，因你的典章本为美。
chapters.en: Turn away my reproach which I fear: for thy judgments good.

chapters.zh: 我羡慕你的训词；求你使我在你的公义上生活！
chapters.en: Behold, I have longed after thy precepts: quicken me in thy righteousness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，愿你照你的话，使你的慈爱，就是你的救恩，临到我身上，
chapters.en: VAU. Let thy mercies come also unto me, O LORD, thy salvation, according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 我就有话回答那羞辱我的，因我倚靠你的话。
chapters.en: So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你叫真理的话总不离开我口，因我仰望你的典章。
chapters.en: And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth; for I have hoped in thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 我要常守你的律法，直到永永远远。
chapters.en: So shall I keep thy law continually for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 我要自由而行（或译：我要行在宽阔之地），因我素来考究你的训词。
chapters.en: And I will walk at liberty: for I seek thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 我也要在君王面前论说你的法度，并不至于羞愧。
chapters.en: I will speak of thy testimonies also before kings, and will not be ashamed.

chapters.zh: 我要在你的命令中自乐；这命令素来是我所爱的。
chapters.en: And I will delight myself in thy commandments, which I have loved.

chapters.zh: 我又要遵行（原文是举手）你的命令，这命令素来是我所爱的；我也要思想你的律例。
chapters.en: My hands also will I lift up unto thy commandments, which I have loved; and I will meditate in thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 求你记念向你仆人所应许的话，叫我有盼望。
chapters.en: ZAIN. Remember the word unto thy servant, upon which thou hast caused me to hope.

chapters.zh: 这话将我救活了；我在患难中，因此得安慰。
chapters.en: This my comfort in my affliction: for thy word hath quickened me.

chapters.zh: 骄傲的人甚侮慢我，我却未曾偏离你的律法。
chapters.en: The proud have had me greatly in derision: have I not declined from thy law.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我记念你从古以来的典章，就得了安慰。
chapters.en: I remembered thy judgments of old, O LORD; and have comforted myself.

chapters.zh: 我见恶人离弃你的律法，就怒气发作，犹如火烧。
chapters.en: Horror hath taken hold upon me because of the wicked that forsake thy law.

chapters.zh: 我在世寄居，素来以你的律例为诗歌。
chapters.en: Thy statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我夜间记念你的名，遵守你的律法。
chapters.en: I have remembered thy name, O LORD, in the night, and have kept thy law.

chapters.zh: 我所以如此，是因我守你的训词。
chapters.en: This I had, because I kept thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的福分；我曾说，我要遵守你的言语。
chapters.en: CHETH. my portion, O LORD: I have said that I would keep thy words.

chapters.zh: 我一心求过你的恩；愿你照你的话怜悯我！
chapters.en: I intreated thy favour with whole heart: be merciful unto me according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 我思想我所行的道，就转步归向你的法度。
chapters.en: I thought on my ways, and turned my feet unto thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 我急忙遵守你的命令，并不迟延。
chapters.en: I made haste, and delayed not to keep thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 恶人的绳索缠绕我，我却没有忘记你的律法。
chapters.en: The bands of the wicked have robbed me: I have not forgotten thy law.

chapters.zh: 我因你公义的典章，半夜必起来称谢你。
chapters.en: At midnight I will rise to give thanks unto thee because of thy righteous judgments.

chapters.zh: 凡敬畏你、守你训词的人，我都与他作伴。
chapters.en: I a companion of all that fear thee, and of them that keep thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的慈爱遍满大地；求你将你的律例教训我！
chapters.en: The earth, O LORD, is full of thy mercy: teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你向来是照你的话善待仆人。
chapters.en: TETH. Thou hast dealt well with thy servant, O LORD, according unto thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你将精明和知识赐给我，因我信了你的命令。
chapters.en: Teach me good judgment and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 我未受苦以先走迷了路，现在却遵守你的话。
chapters.en: Before I was afflicted I went astray: but now have I kept thy word.

chapters.zh: 你本为善，所行的也善；求你将你的律例教训我！
chapters.en: Thou good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 骄傲人编造谎言攻击我，我却要一心守你的训词。
chapters.en: The proud have forged a lie against me: I will keep thy precepts with whole heart.

chapters.zh: 他们心蒙脂油，我却喜爱你的律法。
chapters.en: Their heart is as fat as grease; I delight in thy law.

chapters.zh: 我受苦是与我有益，为要使我学习你的律例。
chapters.en: good for me that I have been afflicted; that I might learn thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 你口中的训言（或译：律法）与我有益，胜于千万的金银。
chapters.en: The law of thy mouth better unto me than thousands of gold and silver.

chapters.zh: 你的手制造我，建立我；求你赐我悟性，可以学习你的命令！
chapters.en: JOD. Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 敬畏你的人见我就要欢喜，因我仰望你的话。
chapters.en: They that fear thee will be glad when they see me; because I have hoped in thy word.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我知道你的判语是公义的；你使我受苦是以诚实待我。
chapters.en: I know, O LORD, that thy judgments right, and thou in faithfulness hast afflicted me.

chapters.zh: 求你照著应许仆人的话，以慈爱安慰我。
chapters.en: Let, I pray thee, thy merciful kindness be for my comfort, according to thy word unto thy servant.

chapters.zh: 愿你的慈悲临到我，使我存活，因你的律法是我所喜爱的。
chapters.en: Let thy tender mercies come unto me, that I may live: for thy law my delight.

chapters.zh: 愿骄傲人蒙羞，因为他们无理地倾覆我；但我要思想你的训词。
chapters.en: Let the proud be ashamed; for they dealt perversely with me without a cause: I will meditate in thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 愿敬畏你的人归向我，他们就知道你的法度。
chapters.en: Let those that fear thee turn unto me, and those that have known thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 愿我的心在你的律例上完全，使我不致蒙羞。
chapters.en: Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed.

chapters.zh: 我心渴想你的救恩，仰望你的应许。
chapters.en: CAPH. My soul fainteth for thy salvation: I hope in thy word.

chapters.zh: 我因盼望你的应许眼睛失明，说：你何时安慰我？
chapters.en: Mine eyes fail for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?

chapters.zh: 我好像烟薰的皮袋，却不忘记你的律例。
chapters.en: For I am become like a bottle in the smoke; do I not forget thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 你仆人的年日有多少呢？你几时向逼迫我的人施行审判呢？
chapters.en: How many the days of thy servant? when wilt thou execute judgment on them that persecute me?

chapters.zh: 不从你律法的骄傲人为我掘了坑。
chapters.en: The proud have digged pits for me, which not after thy law.

chapters.zh: 你的命令尽都诚实；他们无理地逼迫我，求你帮助我！
chapters.en: All thy commandments faithful: they persecute me wrongfully; help thou me.

chapters.zh: 他们几乎把我从世上灭绝，但我没有离弃你的训词。
chapters.en: They had almost consumed me upon earth; but I forsook not thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 求你照你的慈爱将我救活，我就遵守你口中的法度。
chapters.en: Quicken me after thy lovingkindness; so shall I keep the testimony of thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的话安定在天，直到永远。
chapters.en: LAMED. For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.

chapters.zh: 你的诚实存到万代；你坚定了地，地就长存。
chapters.en: Thy faithfulness unto all generations: thou hast established the earth, and it abideth.

chapters.zh: 天地照你的安排存到今日；万物都是你的仆役。
chapters.en: They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all thy servants.

chapters.zh: 我若不是喜爱你的律法，早就在苦难中灭绝了！
chapters.en: Unless thy law my delights, I should then have perished in mine affliction.

chapters.zh: 我永不忘记你的训词，因你用这训词将我救活了。
chapters.en: I will never forget thy precepts: for with them thou hast quickened me.

chapters.zh: 我是属你的，求你救我，因我寻求了你的训词。
chapters.en: I thine, save me; for I have sought thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 恶人等待我，要灭绝我，我却要揣摩你的法度。
chapters.en: The wicked have waited for me to destroy me: I will consider thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 我看万事尽都有限，惟有你的命令极其宽广。
chapters.en: I have seen an end of all perfection: thy commandment exceeding broad.

chapters.zh: 我何等爱慕你的律法，终日不住地思想。
chapters.en: MEM. O how love I thy law! it my meditation all the day.

chapters.zh: 你的命令常存在我心里，使我比仇敌有智慧。
chapters.en: Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they ever with me.

chapters.zh: 我比我的师傅更通达，因我思想你的法度。
chapters.en: I have more understanding than all my teachers: for thy testimonies my meditation.

chapters.zh: 我比年老的更明白，因我守了你的训词。
chapters.en: I understand more than the ancients, because I keep thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 我禁止我脚走一切的邪路，为要遵守你的话。
chapters.en: I have refrained my feet from every evil way, that I might keep thy word.

chapters.zh: 我没有偏离你的典章，因为你教训了我。
chapters.en: I have not departed from thy judgments: for thou hast taught me.

chapters.zh: 你的言语在我上膛何等甘美，在我口中比蜜更甜！
chapters.en: How sweet are thy words unto my taste! than honey to my mouth!

chapters.zh: 我藉著你的训词得以明白，所以我恨一切的假道。
chapters.en: Through thy precepts I get understanding: therefore I hate every false way.

chapters.zh: 你的话是我脚前的灯，是我路上的光。
chapters.en: NUN. Thy word a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.

chapters.zh: 你公义的典章，我曾起誓遵守，我必按誓而行。
chapters.en: I have sworn, and I will perform , that I will keep thy righteous judgments.

chapters.zh: 我甚是受苦；耶和华啊，求你照你的话将我救活！
chapters.en: I am afflicted very much: quicken me, O LORD, according unto thy word.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你悦纳我口中的赞美为供物，又将你的典章教训我！
chapters.en: Accept, I beseech thee, the freewill offerings of my mouth, O LORD, and teach me thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 我的性命常在危险之中，我却不忘记你的律法。
chapters.en: My soul continually in my hand: yet do I not forget thy law.

chapters.zh: 恶人为我设下网罗，我却没有偏离你的训词。
chapters.en: The wicked have laid a snare for me: yet I erred not from thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 我以你的法度为永远的产业，因这是我心中所喜爱的。
chapters.en: Thy testimonies have I taken as an heritage for ever: for they the rejoicing of my heart.

chapters.zh: 我的心专向你的律例，永远遵行，一直到底。
chapters.en: I have inclined mine heart to perform thy statutes alway, the end.

chapters.zh: 心怀二意的人为我所恨；但你的律法为我所爱。
chapters.en: SAMECH. I hate thoughts: but thy law do I love.

chapters.zh: 你是我藏身之处，又是我的盾牌；我甚仰望你的话语。
chapters.en: Thou my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.

chapters.zh: 作恶的人哪，你们离开我罢！我好遵守我神的命令。
chapters.en: Depart from me, ye evildoers: for I will keep the commandments of my God.

chapters.zh: 求你照你的话扶持我，使我存活，也不叫我因失望而害羞。
chapters.en: Uphold me according unto thy word, that I may live: and let me not be ashamed of my hope.

chapters.zh: 求你扶持我，我便得救，时常看重你的律例。
chapters.en: Hold thou me up, and I shall be safe: and I will have respect unto thy statutes continually.

chapters.zh: 凡偏离你律例的人，你都轻弃他们，因为他们的诡诈必归虚空。
chapters.en: Thou hast trodden down all them that err from thy statutes: for their deceit falsehood.

chapters.zh: 凡地上的恶人，你除掉他，好像除掉渣滓；因此我爱你的法度。
chapters.en: Thou puttest away all the wicked of the earth dross: therefore I love thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 我因惧怕你，肉就发抖；我也怕你的判语。
chapters.en: My flesh trembleth for fear of thee; and I am afraid of thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 我行过公平和公义，求你不要撇下我给欺压我的人！
chapters.en: AIN. I have done judgment and justice: leave me not to mine oppressors.

chapters.zh: 求你为仆人作保，使我得好处，不容骄傲人欺压我！
chapters.en: Be surety for thy servant for good: let not the proud oppress me.

chapters.zh: 我因盼望你的救恩和你公义的话眼睛失明。
chapters.en: Mine eyes fail for thy salvation, and for the word of thy righteousness.

chapters.zh: 求你照你的慈爱待仆人，将你的律例教训我。
chapters.en: Deal with thy servant according unto thy mercy, and teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 我是你的仆人，求你赐我悟性，使我得知你的法度。
chapters.en: I thy servant; give me understanding, that I may know thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 这是耶和华降罚的时候，因人废了你的律法。
chapters.en: time for , LORD, to work: they have made void thy law.

chapters.zh: 所以，我爱你的命令胜于金子，更胜于精金。
chapters.en: Therefore I love thy commandments above gold; yea, above fine gold.

chapters.zh: 你一切的训词，在万事上我都以为正直；我却恨恶一切假道。
chapters.en: Therefore I esteem all precepts all right; I hate every false way.

chapters.zh: 你的法度奇妙，所以我一心谨守。
chapters.en: PE. Thy testimonies wonderful: therefore doth my soul keep them.

chapters.zh: 你的言语一解开就发出亮光，使愚人通达。
chapters.en: The entrance of thy words giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple.

chapters.zh: 我张口而气喘，因我切慕你的命令。
chapters.en: I opened my mouth, and panted: for I longed for thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 求你转向我，怜悯我，好像你素常待那些爱你名的人。
chapters.en: Look thou upon me, and be merciful unto me, as thou usest to do unto those that love thy name.

chapters.zh: 求你用你的话使我脚步稳当，不许甚么罪孽辖制我。
chapters.en: Order my steps in thy word: and let not any iniquity have dominion over me.

chapters.zh: 求你救我脱离人的欺压，我要遵守你的训词。
chapters.en: Deliver me from the oppression of man: so will I keep thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 求你用脸光照仆人，又将你的律例教训我。
chapters.en: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; and teach me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 我的眼泪下流成河，因为他们不守你的律法。
chapters.en: Rivers of waters run down mine eyes, because they keep not thy law.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你是公义的；你的判语也是正直的！
chapters.en: TZADDI. Righteous thou, O LORD, and upright thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 你所命定的法度是凭公义和至诚。
chapters.en: Thy testimonies thou hast commanded righteous and very faithful.

chapters.zh: 我心焦急，如同火烧，因我敌人忘记你的言语。
chapters.en: My zeal hath consumed me, because mine enemies have forgotten thy words.

chapters.zh: 你的话极其精炼，所以你的仆人喜爱。
chapters.en: Thy word very pure: therefore thy servant loveth it.

chapters.zh: 我微小，被人藐视，却不忘记你的训词。
chapters.en: I small and despised: do not I forget thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 你的公义永远长存；你的律法尽都真实。
chapters.en: Thy righteousness an everlasting righteousness, and thy law the truth.

chapters.zh: 我遭遇患难愁苦，你的命令却是我所喜爱的。
chapters.en: Trouble and anguish have taken hold on me: thy commandments my delights.

chapters.zh: 你的法度永远是公义的；求你赐我悟性，我就活了。
chapters.en: The righteousness of thy testimonies everlasting: give me understanding, and I shall live.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我一心呼吁你；求你应允我，我必谨守你的律例！
chapters.en: KOPH. I cried with whole heart; hear me, O LORD: I will keep thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 我向你呼吁，求你救我！我要遵守你的法度。
chapters.en: I cried unto thee; save me, and I shall keep thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 我趁天未亮呼求；我仰望了你的言语。
chapters.en: I prevented the dawning of the morning, and cried: I hoped in thy word.

chapters.zh: 我趁夜更未换将眼睁开，为要思想你的话语。
chapters.en: Mine eyes prevent the watches, that I might meditate in thy word.

chapters.zh: 求你照你的慈爱听我的声音；耶和华啊，求你照你的典章将我救活！
chapters.en: Hear my voice according unto thy lovingkindness: O LORD, quicken me according to thy judgment.

chapters.zh: 追求奸恶的人临近了；他们远离你的律法。
chapters.en: They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你与我相近；你一切的命令尽都真实！
chapters.en: Thou near, O LORD; and all thy commandments truth.

chapters.zh: 我因学你的法度，久已知道是你永远立定的。
chapters.en: Concerning thy testimonies, I have known of old that thou hast founded them for ever.

chapters.zh: 求你看顾我的苦难，搭救我，因我不忘记你的律法。
chapters.en: RESH. Consider mine affliction, and deliver me: for I do not forget thy law.

chapters.zh: 求你为我辨屈，救赎我，照你的话将我救活。
chapters.en: Plead my cause, and deliver me: quicken me according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 救恩远离恶人，因为他们不寻求你的律例。
chapters.en: Salvation far from the wicked: for they seek not thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的慈悲本为大；求你照你的典章将我救活。
chapters.en: Great thy tender mercies, O LORD: quicken me according to thy judgments.

chapters.zh: 逼迫我的，抵挡我的，很多，我却没有偏离你的法度。
chapters.en: Many my persecutors and mine enemies; do I not decline from thy testimonies.

chapters.zh: 我看见奸恶的人就甚憎恶，因为他们不遵守你的话。
chapters.en: I beheld the transgressors, and was grieved; because they kept not thy word.

chapters.zh: 你看我怎样爱你的训词！耶和华啊，求你照你的慈爱将我救活！
chapters.en: Consider how I love thy precepts: quicken me, O LORD, according to thy lovingkindness.

chapters.zh: 你话的总纲是真实；你一切公义的典章是永远长存。
chapters.en: Thy word true the beginning: and every one of thy righteous judgments for ever.

chapters.zh: 首领无故地逼迫我，但我的心畏惧你的言语。
chapters.en: SCHIN. Princes have persecuted me without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.

chapters.zh: 我喜爱你的话，好像人得了许多掳物。
chapters.en: I rejoice at thy word, as one that findeth great spoil.

chapters.zh: 谎话是我所恨恶所憎嫌的；惟你的律法是我所爱的。
chapters.en: I hate and abhor lying: thy law do I love.

chapters.zh: 我因你公义的典章一天七次赞美你。
chapters.en: Seven times a day do I praise thee because of thy righteous judgments.

chapters.zh: 爱你律法的人有大平安，甚么都不能使他们绊脚。
chapters.en: Great peace have they which love thy law: and nothing shall offend them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我仰望了你的救恩，遵行了你的命令。
chapters.en: LORD, I have hoped for thy salvation, and done thy commandments.

chapters.zh: 我心里守了你的法度；这法度我甚喜爱。
chapters.en: My soul hath kept thy testimonies; and I love them exceedingly.

chapters.zh: 我遵守了你的训词和法度，因我一切所行的都在你面前。
chapters.en: I have kept thy precepts and thy testimonies: for all my ways before thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，愿我的呼吁达到你面前，照你的话赐我悟性。
chapters.en: TAU. Let my cry come near before thee, O LORD: give me understanding according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 愿我的恳求达到你面前，照你的话搭救我。
chapters.en: Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me according to thy word.

chapters.zh: 愿我的嘴发出赞美的话，因为你将律例教训我。
chapters.en: My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.

chapters.zh: 愿我的舌头歌唱你的话，因你一切的命令尽都公义。
chapters.en: My tongue shall speak of thy word: for all thy commandments righteousness.

chapters.zh: 愿你用手帮助我，因我拣选了你的训词。
chapters.en: Let thine hand help me; for I have chosen thy precepts.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我切慕你的救恩！你的律法也是我所喜爱的。
chapters.en: I have longed for thy salvation, O LORD; and thy law my delight.

chapters.zh: 愿我的性命存活，得以赞美你！愿你的典章帮助我！
chapters.en: Let my soul live, and it shall praise thee; and let thy judgments help me.

chapters.zh: 我如亡羊走迷了路，求你寻找仆人，因我不忘记你的命令。
chapters.en: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek thy servant; for I do not forget thy commandments.

chapters.zh: （上行（或作登阶，下同）之诗。）我在急难中求告耶和华，他就应允我。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] In my distress I cried unto the LORD, and he heard me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你救我脱离说谎的嘴唇和诡诈的舌头！
chapters.en: Deliver my soul, O LORD, from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.

chapters.zh: 诡诈的舌头啊，要给你甚么呢？要拿甚么加给你呢？
chapters.en: What shall be given unto thee? or what shall be done unto thee, thou false tongue?

chapters.zh: 就是勇士的利箭和罗腾木（小树名，松类）的炭火。
chapters.en: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.

chapters.zh: 我寄居在米设，住在基达帐棚之中，有祸了！
chapters.en: Woe is me, that I sojourn in Mesech, I dwell in the tents of Kedar!

chapters.zh: 我与那恨恶和睦的人许久同住。
chapters.en: My soul hath long dwelt with him that hateth peace.

chapters.zh: 我愿和睦，但我发言，他们就要争战。
chapters.en: I peace: but when I speak, they for war.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）我要向山举目；我的帮助从何而来？
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.

chapters.zh: 我的帮助从造天地的耶和华而来。
chapters.en: My help from the LORD, which made heaven and earth.

chapters.zh: 他必不叫你的脚摇动；保护你的必不打盹！
chapters.en: He will not suffer thy foot to be moved: he that keepeth thee will not slumber.

chapters.zh: 保护以色列的，也不打盹也不睡觉。
chapters.en: Behold, he that keepeth Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.

chapters.zh: 保护你的是耶和华；耶和华在你右边荫庇你。
chapters.en: The LORD thy keeper: the LORD thy shade upon thy right hand.

chapters.zh: 白日，太阳必不伤你；夜间，月亮必不害你。
chapters.en: The sun shall not smite thee by day, nor the moon by night.

chapters.zh: 耶和华要保护你，免受一切的灾害；他要保护你的性命。
chapters.en: The LORD shall preserve thee from all evil: he shall preserve thy soul.

chapters.zh: 你出你入，耶和华要保护你，从今时直到永远。
chapters.en: The LORD shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in from this time forth, and even for evermore.

chapters.zh: （大卫上行之诗。）人对我说：我们往耶和华的殿去，我就欢喜。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees of David.] I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go into the house of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷啊，我们的脚站在你的门内。
chapters.en: Our feet shall stand within thy gates, O Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷被建造，如同连络整齐的一座城。
chapters.en: Jerusalem is builded as a city that is compact together:

chapters.zh: 众支派，就是耶和华的支派，上那里去，按以色列的常例（或译：作以色列的证据）称赞耶和华的名。
chapters.en: Whither the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, unto the testimony of Israel, to give thanks unto the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为在那里设立审判的宝座，就是大卫家的宝座。
chapters.en: For there are set thrones of judgment, the thrones of the house of David.

chapters.zh: 你们要为耶路撒冷求平安！耶路撒冷啊，爱你的人必然兴旺！
chapters.en: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee.

chapters.zh: 愿你城中平安！愿你宫内兴旺！
chapters.en: Peace be within thy walls, prosperity within thy palaces.

chapters.zh: 因我弟兄和同伴的缘故，我要说：愿平安在你中间！
chapters.en: For my brethren and companions' sakes, I will now say, Peace within thee.

chapters.zh: 因耶和华 ─ 我们神殿的缘故，我要为你求福！
chapters.en: Because of the house of the LORD our God I will seek thy good.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）坐在天上的主啊，我向你举目。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.

chapters.zh: 看哪，仆人的眼睛怎样望主人的手，使女的眼睛怎样望主母的手，我们的眼睛也照样望耶和华 ─ 我们的神，直到他怜悯我们。
chapters.en: Behold, as the eyes of servants unto the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes upon the LORD our God, until that he have mercy upon us.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你怜悯我们，怜悯我们！因为我们被藐视，已到极处。
chapters.en: Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy upon us: for we are exceedingly filled with contempt.

chapters.zh: 我们被那些安逸人的讥诮和骄傲人的藐视，已到极处。
chapters.en: Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scorning of those that are at ease, with the contempt of the proud.

chapters.zh: （大卫上行之诗。）以色列人要说：若不是耶和华帮助我们，
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees of David.] If the LORD who was on our side, now may Israel say;

chapters.zh: 若不是耶和华帮助我们，当人起来攻击我们、
chapters.en: If the LORD who was on our side, when men rose up against us:

chapters.zh: 向我们发怒的时候，就把我们活活地吞了。
chapters.en: Then they had swallowed us up quick, when their wrath was kindled against us:

chapters.zh: 那时，波涛必漫过我们，河水必淹没我们，
chapters.en: Then the waters had overwhelmed us, the stream had gone over our soul:

chapters.zh: 狂傲的水必淹没我们。
chapters.en: Then the proud waters had gone over our soul.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是应当称颂的！他没有把我们当野食交给他们吞吃（原文是牙齿）。
chapters.en: Blessed the LORD, who hath not given us a prey to their teeth.

chapters.zh: 我们好像雀鸟，从捕鸟人的网罗里逃脱；网罗破裂，我们逃脱了。
chapters.en: Our soul is escaped as a bird out of the snare of the fowlers: the snare is broken, and we are escaped.

chapters.zh: 我们得帮助，是在乎倚靠造天地之耶和华的名。
chapters.en: Our help in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）倚靠耶和华的人好像锡安山，永不动摇。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] They that trust in the LORD as mount Zion, cannot be removed, abideth for ever.

chapters.zh: 众山怎样围绕耶路撒冷，耶和华也照样围绕他的百姓，从今时直到永远。
chapters.en: As the mountains round about Jerusalem, so the LORD round about his people from henceforth even for ever.

chapters.zh: 恶人的杖不常落在义人的分上，免得义人伸手作恶。
chapters.en: For the rod of the wicked shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous; lest the righteous put forth their hands unto iniquity.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你善待那些为善和心里正直的人。
chapters.en: Do good, O LORD, unto good, and upright in their hearts.

chapters.zh: 至于那偏行弯曲道路的人，耶和华必使他和作恶的人一同出去受刑。愿平安归于以色列！
chapters.en: As for such as turn aside unto their crooked ways, the LORD shall lead them forth with the workers of iniquity: peace upon Israel.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）当耶和华将那些被掳的带回锡安的时候，我们好像做梦的人。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] When the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream.

chapters.zh: 我们满口喜笑、满舌欢呼的时候，外邦中就有人说：耶和华为他们行了大事！
chapters.en: Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing: then said they among the heathen, The LORD hath done great things for them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华果然为我们行了大事，我们就欢喜。
chapters.en: The LORD hath done great things for us; we are glad.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你使我们被掳的人归回，好像南地的河水复流。
chapters.en: Turn again our captivity, O LORD, as the streams in the south.

chapters.zh: 流泪撒种的，必欢呼收割！
chapters.en: They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.

chapters.zh: 那带种流泪出去的，必要欢欢乐乐地带禾捆回来！
chapters.en: He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves .

chapters.zh: （所罗门上行之诗。）若不是耶和华建造房屋，建造的人就枉然劳力；若不是耶和华看守城池，看守的人就枉然警醒。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees for Solomon.] Except the LORD build the house, they labour in vain that build it: except the LORD keep the city, the watchman waketh in vain.

chapters.zh: 你们清晨早起，夜晚安歇，吃劳碌得来的饭，本是枉然；惟有耶和华所亲爱的，必叫他安然睡觉。
chapters.en: vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows: so he giveth his beloved sleep.

chapters.zh: 儿女是耶和华所赐的产业；所怀的胎是他所给的赏赐。
chapters.en: Lo, children an heritage of the LORD: the fruit of the womb reward.

chapters.zh: 少年时所生的儿女好像勇士手中的箭。
chapters.en: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man; so children of the youth.

chapters.zh: 箭袋充满的人便为有福；他们在城门口和仇敌说话的时候，必不至于羞愧。
chapters.en: Happy the man that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed, but they shall speak with the enemies in the gate.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）凡敬畏耶和华、遵行他道的人便为有福！
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] Blessed every one that feareth the LORD; that walketh in his ways.

chapters.zh: 你要吃劳碌得来的；你要享福，事情顺利。
chapters.en: For thou shalt eat the labour of thine hands: happy thou , and well with thee.

chapters.zh: 你妻子在你的内室，好像多结果子的葡萄树；你儿女围绕你的桌子，好像橄榄栽子。
chapters.en: Thy wife as a fruitful vine by the sides of thine house: thy children like olive plants round about thy table.

chapters.zh: 看哪，敬畏耶和华的人必要这样蒙福！
chapters.en: Behold, that thus shall the man be blessed that feareth the LORD.

chapters.zh: 愿耶和华从锡安赐福给你！愿你一生一世看见耶路撒冷的好处！
chapters.en: The LORD shall bless thee out of Zion: and thou shalt see the good of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.

chapters.zh: 愿你看见你儿女的儿女！愿平安归于以色列！
chapters.en: Yea, thou shalt see thy children's children, peace upon Israel.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）以色列当说：从我幼年以来，敌人屡次苦害我，
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, may Israel now say:

chapters.zh: 从我幼年以来，敌人屡次苦害我，却没有胜了我。
chapters.en: Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.

chapters.zh: 如同扶犁的在我背上扶犁而耕，耕的犁沟甚长。
chapters.en: The plowers plowed upon my back: they made long their furrows.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是公义的；他砍断了恶人的绳索。
chapters.en: The LORD righteous: he hath cut asunder the cords of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 愿恨恶锡安的都蒙羞退后！
chapters.en: Let them all be confounded and turned back that hate Zion.

chapters.zh: 愿他们像房顶上的草，未长成而枯干，
chapters.en: Let them be as the grass the housetops, which withereth afore it groweth up:

chapters.zh: 收割的不够一把，捆禾的也不满怀。
chapters.en: Wherewith the mower filleth not his hand; nor he that bindeth sheaves his bosom.

chapters.zh: 过路的也不说：愿耶和华所赐的福归与你们！我们奉耶和华的名给你们祝福！
chapters.en: Neither do they which go by say, The blessing of the LORD upon you: we bless you in the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）耶和华啊，我从深处向你求告！
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees.] Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 主啊，求你听我的声音！愿你侧耳听我恳求的声音！
chapters.en: Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.

chapters.zh: 主 ─ 耶和华啊，你若究察罪孽，谁能站得住呢？
chapters.en: If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?

chapters.zh: 但在你有赦免之恩，要叫人敬畏你。
chapters.en: But forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.

chapters.zh: 我等候耶和华，我的心等候；我也仰望他的话。
chapters.en: I wait for the LORD, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.

chapters.zh: 我的心等候主，胜于守夜的，等候天亮，胜于守夜的，等候天亮。
chapters.en: My soul for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: they that watch for the morning.

chapters.zh: 以色列啊，你当仰望耶和华！因他有慈爱，有丰盛的救恩。
chapters.en: Let Israel hope in the LORD: for with the LORD mercy, and with him plenteous redemption.

chapters.zh: 他必救赎以色列脱离一切的罪孽。
chapters.en: And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

chapters.zh: （大卫上行之诗。）耶和华啊，我的心不狂傲，我的眼不高大；重大和测不透的事，我也不敢行。
chapters.en: [A Song of degrees of David.] LORD, my heart is not haughty, nor mine eyes lofty: neither do I exercise myself in great matters, or in things too high for me.

chapters.zh: 我的心平稳安静，好像断过奶的孩子在他母亲的怀中；我的心在我里面真像断过奶的孩子。
chapters.en: Surely I have behaved and quieted myself, as a child that is weaned of his mother: my soul even as a weaned child.

chapters.zh: 以色列啊，你当仰望耶和华，从今时直到永远！
chapters.en: Let Israel hope in the LORD from henceforth and for ever.

chapters.zh: （上行之诗。）耶和华啊，求你记念大卫所受的一切苦难！
chapters.en: A Song of degrees. LORD, remember David, all his afflictions:

chapters.zh: 他怎样向耶和华起誓，向雅各的大能者许愿，
chapters.en: How he sware unto the LORD, vowed unto the mighty of Jacob;

chapters.zh: 说：我必不进我的帐幕，也不上我的床榻；
chapters.en: Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house, nor go up into my bed;

chapters.zh: 我不容我的眼睛睡觉，也不容我的眼目打盹；
chapters.en: I will not give sleep to mine eyes, slumber to mine eyelids,

chapters.zh: 直等我为耶和华寻得所在，为雅各的大能者寻得居所。
chapters.en: Until I find out a place for the LORD, an habitation for the mighty of Jacob.

chapters.zh: 我们听说约柜在以法他，我们在基列耶琳就寻见了。
chapters.en: Lo, we heard of it at Ephratah: we found it in the fields of the wood.

chapters.zh: 我们要进他的居所，在他脚凳前下拜。
chapters.en: We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstool.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你兴起，和你有能力的约柜同入安息之所！
chapters.en: Arise, O LORD, into thy rest; thou, and the ark of thy strength.

chapters.zh: 愿你的祭司披上公义！愿你的圣民欢呼！
chapters.en: Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.

chapters.zh: 求你因你仆人大卫的缘故，不要厌弃你的受膏者！
chapters.en: For thy servant David's sake turn not away the face of thine anointed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华向大卫、凭诚实起了誓，必不反覆，说：我要使你所生的坐在你的宝座上。
chapters.en: The LORD hath sworn truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.

chapters.zh: 你的众子若守我的约和我所教训他们的法度，他们的子孙必永远坐在你的宝座上。
chapters.en: If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华拣选了锡安，愿意当作自己的居所，
chapters.en: For the LORD hath chosen Zion; he hath desired for his habitation.

chapters.zh: 说：这是我永远安息之所；我要住在这里，因为是我所愿意的。
chapters.en: This my rest for ever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it.

chapters.zh: 我要使其中的粮食丰满，使其中的穷人饱足。
chapters.en: I will abundantly bless her provision: I will satisfy her poor with bread.

chapters.zh: 我要使祭司披上救恩，圣民大声欢呼！
